room_borrowed_separation.speaker: lt_mara_owen
room_borrowed_separation.line_1: Owen reports Column C-4 failing. Reiss is at its base, with three broken restraint sets on the floor.
room_borrowed_separation.line_2: Park marks the collapse tape while Brooks holds left sector. Torah has to choose an intervention before the next fracture event.

buttons.label: Brooks: Pin and Cut
buttons.preview: Brooks leads a direct contact approach: she and Owen drive Reiss back from the column, pin...

buttons.label: Park: Controlled Burn
buttons.preview: Park's plan uses the bodily cost window—the tremor and thirst that follow each burst—to approach Reiss...

operation_plans.label: Brooks: Pin and Cut
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix Reiss with combined physical pressure, breach the column zone, hold him stationary while Park cuts the wig with the thermal cutter, CASEVAC immediately after
operation_plans.intent: Brooks leads a direct contact approach: she and Owen drive Reiss back from the column, pin him against the far wall, and hold him stationary long enough for Park to cut the wig free with the thermal cutter. Fast, violent, procedurally clean if it works.
operation_plans.yield: Wig is cut. Reiss is separated from the sign. Strength discharges—but without the burn, the discharge is uncontrolled. The column takes a final stress pulse. The building holds, barely. Reiss collapses into tremor and thirst. CASEVAC proceeds.
operation_plans.risk: If Brooks cannot hold Reiss stationary—if the pin fails—Reiss redirects force into whoever is closest. The column fracture accelerates. The mezzanine becomes an active collapse risk. Brooks takes the first injury.

lines: Brooks hits Reiss low and hard, driving him off the column and into the far wall. Owen is on the right side a half-second later. Reiss does not fight them—he pushes, the way a door pushes when the pressure behind it is too great. Brooks locks her arms and does not let go. Park is already moving. The thermal cutter takes four seconds. The hair separates from the scalp with a sound that is not mechanical. Reiss goes limp between them. The column makes one loud crack and then goes quiet. Dust falls. Brooks is breathing through her teeth. Her left forearm is bent at an angle it should not be. She does not mention it until they are outside.

Brooks gets Reiss off the column. That part works. The pin does not hold—Reiss's arm comes free and his hand finds the squat rack frame, which folds. Park cuts the wig but not cleanly; a section of the base remains adhered to the scalp. The strength drops but does not end. Reiss is on his knees, shaking, bleeding from the scalp, and the column is still cracking. Owen calls the partial CASEVAC: Ross brings the carry route in from the east. They get Reiss out. The column is shored with the temporary props. The anomaly is suppressed, not closed. The remaining hair section is in an evidence bag. Park is very quiet on the drive back.

Brooks makes contact and Reiss moves. Not toward Brooks—just moves, the way a man moves when he is finishing a set and someone is in the way. Brooks goes into the wall. Owen calls break contact before the echo stops. The column fracture runs another twenty centimeters in the next thirty seconds. Brooks is on the floor, not unconscious but not standing. Owen pulls her back to the breach point. Reiss returns to the column. The ceiling above Bay 3 groans. Ross is on comms asking for a status update and getting silence. Owen looks at Torah and does not say anything. She does not need to.

operation_plans.label: Park: Controlled Burn
operation_plans.primary_skill: anomalous material destruction, controlled burn protocol
operation_plans.tactical_step: establish sector security at collapse radius, suppress Reiss's movement with verbal and positional pressure during a post-burst tremor window, breach the column zone to stage the burn frame, cut and burn the wig with deliberate release intent, CASEVAC Reiss after discharge
operation_plans.intent: Park's plan uses the bodily cost window—the tremor and thirst that follow each burst—to approach Reiss when his output is lowest. She stages the fire-suppression blanket as a burn frame, cuts the wig, and burns it in place. The burn requires Reiss to be stationary and requires Torah to verbally acknowledge the end of the false vow as the fire takes.
operation_plans.yield: The wig burns. The strength discharges into the fire. The column fracture stops. Reiss is conscious, confused, and in significant pain from the scalp adhesion removal, but ambulatory. The anomaly closes cleanly. CASEVAC is a carry-assist, not an emergency extract.
operation_plans.risk: If Reiss moves during the burn—if the tremor window closes early—the fire-suppression blanket is not enough to contain the discharge. The burn becomes uncontrolled. Park is inside the collapse radius when it happens. The column may not survive the discharge pulse.

lines: Park watches Reiss's hands. After the third contact event with the column, they start to shake. She moves before Owen can stop her—not running, walking, the way you walk when you have already decided. She sets the blanket frame in a three-meter square around Reiss and the column base. He looks at her. She says, quietly, 'You're done. Let me finish it.' He does not agree. He does not disagree. He is very thirsty and his scalp is bleeding and he has been here for a long time. Park cuts the hair. Torah speaks the release. The fire takes the hair in under ten seconds. The column crack does not widen. The discharge goes into the smoke. Reiss sits down on the gym floor and asks for water. Park gives him her bottle. She does not look at the evidence container when she seals it.

Park reaches Reiss in the tremor window. The burn frame goes up. The cut is clean. But Torah's release acknowledgment comes a half-second late—the fire takes the hair but the discharge pulse is not fully contained by the blanket. The column takes a final stress event: the crack runs to the floor. The temporary props hold it. Reiss is down, conscious, scalp raw and bleeding. Park gets him to the breach point. Ross brings the carry route in. The building stands. The anomaly is closed but the column is condemned. Park files her report that night and notes the timing error without attributing it to anyone.

The tremor window closes faster than Park's thermal data predicted. Reiss straightens up while she is still staging the blanket frame. He does not see her as a threat. He sees her as an obstacle between him and the column, and he moves her the way you move a chair. Park hits the floor hard. Owen calls CASEVAC immediately—Ross is through the east entrance in forty seconds. Park is conscious and furious and has two cracked ribs. Reiss returns to the column. The crack is now floor-to-ceiling. Owen gets everyone out. The building does not collapse, but it is close. The anomaly is still active when the squad exits. Ross sets the outer cordon and calls Saye. Saye has been quiet for six minutes. She picks up on the first ring.

Brooks hits Reiss low and hard, driving him off the column and into the far wall. Owen is on the right side a half-second later. Reiss does not fight them—he pushes, the way a door pushes when the pressure behind it is too great. Brooks locks her arms and does not let go. Park is already moving. The thermal cutter takes four seconds. The hair separates from the scalp with a sound that is not mechanical. Reiss goes limp between them. The column makes one loud crack and then goes quiet. Dust falls. Brooks is breathing through her teeth. Her left forearm is bent at an angle it should not be. She does not mention it until they are outside.

Park watches Reiss's hands. After the third contact event with the column, they start to shake. She moves before Owen can stop her—not running, walking, the way you walk when you have already decided. She sets the blanket frame in a three-meter square around Reiss and the column base. He looks at her. She says, quietly, 'You're done. Let me finish it.' He does not agree. He does not disagree. He is very thirsty and his scalp is bleeding and he has been here for a long time. Park cuts the hair. Torah speaks the release. The fire takes the hair in under ten seconds. The column crack does not widen. The discharge goes into the smoke. Reiss sits down on the gym floor and asks for water. Park gives him her bottle. She does not look at the evidence container when she seals it.

intercept:success.queued_line: Brooks has Reiss off the column, but the sign still has to be closed in the record.

intercept:partial.queued_line: Reiss is out, the column is shored, and the remaining hair section is still warm inside the evidence bag.

intercept:failure.queued_line: The first entry failed. Bay 3 is still active, and the next decision is containment, not closure.

destroy:success.queued_line: The burn took, the column held, and the evidence record is now the live question.

destroy:partial.queued_line: The burn closed the immediate danger but left a residual trace in custody.

destroy:failure.queued_line: The burn window closed too early. Bay 3 is still active, and the next decision is containment, not closure.

intercept.queued_line: Brooks has Reiss off the column, but the sign still has to be closed in the record.

destroy.queued_line: The burn took, the column held, and the evidence record is now the live question.

corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: The hair is the visible sign of separation; strength follows the sign, not the person
corpus_influences.room_application: The wig counterfeits the sign; the anomaly enforces the sign's obligations on whoever wears it
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: The wig counterfeits the sign; the anomaly enforces the sign's obligations on whoever wears it
required_visible_details: Long synthetic hair fused to Reiss's scalp, bleeding at the adhesion line
Reiss moving with the deliberate calm of someone fulfilling an obligation, not attacking
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: The squad cannot end the anomaly by stopping Reiss; they must end the sign

corpus_influences.source_moment: The hair of separation is cut and burned at the place of offering when the vow is complete
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: The hair of separation is cut and burned at the place of offering when the vow is complete

The smell of burning synthetic hair as the anomaly's close condition

corpus_influences.source_moment: New ropes fail; every failed restraint confirms the sign's authority; the body pays afterward with tremor and thirst

babel_meridian_dispatch.speaker: SITREP
babel_meridian_dispatch.line_1: The command floor terminal logs an approved-syntax order every ninety seconds. Each cycle seals another corridor, and two civilian groups are trapped.
babel_meridian_dispatch.line_2: Owen reports a scaffold pole across the stairwell. Ross holds the counter while Saye counts seventeen minutes before the roof exit seals.

buttons.label: Ross: breach the terminal
buttons.preview: Ross uses his pry tool to clear the brick form at the counter, reaches the junction...

buttons.label: Owen: hold the stairwell
buttons.preview: Owen uses her bar to break the scaffold pole before it fully sets across the stairwell...

operation_plans.label: Ross: breach the terminal
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix the masonry obstruction at the counter, then breach the junction box housing and cut the routing board
operation_plans.intent: Ross uses his pry tool to clear the brick form at the counter, reaches the junction box, and physically cuts the routing board to stop new wall generation
operation_plans.yield: Terminal goes silent mid-cycle; no new walls rise; Saye confirms the loop is broken; Ross has time to help with civilian egress
operation_plans.risk: If the counter masonry shifts while Ross is inside it, he is pinned; if he rushes and misreads the schematic, he cuts the wrong board and the loop accelerates

lines: Ross gets the pry tool under the brick form and leans into it. The form cracks along the mortar line and drops. He is at the junction box in eight seconds, tablet propped against the counter, cutting by the diagram. The terminal speaker cuts out mid-phrase. The scaffold pole across the stairwell stops moving. Ross does not look up. He says, quietly, 'That's the board.' Dust settles. The building is still.

Ross reaches the junction box but the brick form shifts before he can fully clear it. He gets one arm through and cuts two of the three routing leads. The loop slows—cycle time doubles—but it does not stop. New walls are still rising, just slower. Ross is half-pinned at the counter, one shoulder jammed, telling Torah through clenched teeth which lead is left and where the cutters are.

The brick form collapses inward as Ross pries it. He pulls back but not fast enough; the edge catches his left arm and he goes down behind the counter. The terminal keeps cycling. A new wall segment rises at the base of the stairwell, six inches thick, and Owen has to step back. Ross is on the floor, not transmitting, not speaking, staring at the ceiling with the tablet cracked beside him. Saye's voice on the hardline says: 'Fourteen minutes.'

operation_plans.label: Owen: hold the stairwell
operation_plans.tactical_step: react to contact with the scaffold, break the pole before it sets, establish security on the stairwell corridor for civilian egress
operation_plans.intent: Owen uses her bar to break the scaffold pole before it fully sets across the stairwell, keeping the egress route open while Ross works the terminal
operation_plans.yield: Stairwell stays open; civilians can move; Owen holds the corridor and signals Torah with hand gestures to begin moving the first civilian group
operation_plans.risk: If Owen swings too early the pole is still flexible and wraps around the bar; if she waits too long it sets solid and she cannot break it alone

lines: Owen does not wait for a second opinion. She steps into the stairwell entrance, plants her feet, and hits the scaffold pole at the joint where it meets the wall bracket. The pole rings like a bell and snaps at the weld. She kicks the free end clear and turns to face Torah, two fingers up, then pointing at the stairwell: move them now. Ross takes the opening she bought him, slides behind the counter form, and cuts the routing board before the next cycle completes. The terminal speaker dies mid-phrase. Owen keeps the stairwell open with one hand on the rail and no radio traffic at all.

Owen breaks the pole but the bracket holds and the stub is still blocking the lower half of the stairwell opening. Civilians can get through if they crouch. Owen is waving them down with one hand and watching the ceiling with the other, because a second pole is beginning to extend from the opposite wall. She has maybe three minutes before the gap closes entirely. She looks at Torah and mouths: faster.

The pole sets before Owen reaches it. She hits it twice with the bar and it does not move—solid concrete core, scaffold shell, fully bonded to the wall. The stairwell is closed. Owen stands in front of it for a moment, bar at her side, and does not move. Then she turns and walks back to Torah without speaking. She pulls out her notepad and writes one word: roof. She holds it up. That is the only exit left.

Ross gets the pry tool under the brick form and leans into it. The form cracks along the mortar line and drops. He is at the junction box in eight seconds, tablet propped against the counter, cutting by the diagram. The terminal speaker cuts out mid-phrase. The scaffold pole across the stairwell stops moving. Ross does not look up. He says, quietly, 'That's the board.' Dust settles. The building is still.

Owen does not wait for a second opinion. She steps into the stairwell entrance, plants her feet, and hits the scaffold pole at the joint where it meets the wall bracket. The pole rings like a bell and snaps at the weld. She kicks the free end clear and turns to face Torah, two fingers up, then pointing at the stairwell: move them now. Ross takes the opening she bought him, slides behind the counter form, and cuts the routing board before the next cycle completes. The terminal speaker dies mid-phrase. Owen keeps the stairwell open with one hand on the rail and no radio traffic at all.

artifact_options.label: Show the voided tokens
artifact_options.effect_line: Ross uses the prior token case to force command onto paper custody instead of the unified dispatch net.

lines: Ross opens the old token case on the dispatch counter. He does not make a speech. He shows the heat-warped badges to the duty officer and writes the next order by hand.
The terminal waits for a spoken syntax phrase that never comes. The next wall segment fails to rise.

intercept:ross-breach-the-terminal:succe.queued_line: Ross cuts the routing board. The loop dies, but the building still needs its authority scattered before anyone uses the standard net.

intercept:ross-breach-the-terminal:parti.queued_line: Ross slows the loop but is pinned at the counter with one routing lead still live.

intercept:ross-breach-the-terminal:failu.queued_line: Ross is down behind the counter, and the terminal is still broadcasting.

intercept:owen-hold-the-stairwell:succes.queued_line: Owen holds the stairwell long enough for Ross to cut the board. The hub is quiet but not yet safe to speak through.

intercept:owen-hold-the-stairwell:partia.queued_line: Owen leaves a crawl gap in the stairwell, but the terminal is still cycling and a second pole is forming.

intercept:owen-hold-the-stairwell:failur.queued_line: The stairwell sets solid. The terminal is live, and the roof is the last known exit.

intercept.queued_line: The squad must break routing authority before the loop completes, then scatter command before any responder uses the standard net.

the dispatch terminal cycling one approved phrase on loop

corpus_influences.source_moment: Language is confused and scattered; the builders no longer understand one another; the city stops
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: Language is confused and scattered; the builders no longer understand one another; the city stops
corpus_influences.room_application: Closure requires deliberate scattering of command authority into handwritten local cards and hand signals so no single phrase can complete a circuit
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: Closure requires deliberate scattering of command authority into handwritten local cards and hand signals so no single phrase can complete a circuit

room_aldermoor_14.speaker: SITREP
room_aldermoor_14.line_1: Brooks holds the threshold of Room 14. The door has struck him once, and the floor inside is seeping around the outline.
room_aldermoor_14.line_2: Ross bags the evidence log at the nurse station. Iyad reports a different-ink clearance signature, and the keeper has not been named inside Room 14.

buttons.label: Brooks forces the threshold
buttons.preview: Brooks times the door's four-minute cycle, takes the impact window, and forces entry—shoulder to panel, through...

buttons.label: Ross seals the evidence chain
buttons.preview: Ross bags the duty log formally, walks it to Room 14, and hands it to Torah...

operation_plans.label: Brooks forces the threshold
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix the door anomaly by forced breach, establish sector security inside Room 14, hold the room for evidence delivery
operation_plans.intent: Brooks times the door's four-minute cycle, takes the impact window, and forces entry—shoulder to panel, through the frame, sector left, sector right, announces clear so Ross can bring the log in
operation_plans.yield: Room 14 is open and held; Ross can deliver the duty log; the naming can proceed
operation_plans.risk: If Brooks mistimes the cycle, the door strikes him on entry—wrist, shoulder, or ribs—and the entry collapses; the seepage accelerates while the team regroups

lines: Brooks hits the door at the exact moment the pressure drops. It gives. He's through in two steps, moving left along the wall, and his voice comes back flat and immediate over comms: 'Clear left. Clear right. Bring the log.' The floor is wet under his boots but it holds. He does not look at the outline.

Brooks gets through but the door catches his right arm on the frame—not a break, but he pulls it back hard and stands inside the room with his teeth together, breathing through it. 'I'm in,' he says. 'Bring the log. Don't wait.' He is favoring the arm. He does not say how bad it is.

The door hits Brooks full across the shoulder and drives him back into the corridor. He goes down on one knee and stays there for a moment, one hand on the floor. When he looks up his expression is controlled but his eyes are somewhere else. 'Door's faster than I read it,' he says quietly. Torah has to make a new call.

operation_plans.label: Ross seals the evidence chain
operation_plans.primary_skill: evidence custody and document transport under pressure
operation_plans.tactical_step: secure the physical duty log, transport it to Room 14 under Torah's authorization, establish evidence chain before the naming
operation_plans.intent: Ross bags the duty log formally, walks it to Room 14, and hands it to Torah inside the room—the physical document must be present for the naming to carry weight
operation_plans.yield: The duty log is inside Room 14 in proper evidence custody; the naming can proceed with the document as witness
operation_plans.risk: If Ross is intercepted by facility management or if Voss is moved toward the corridor before Ross reaches Room 14, the evidence chain breaks and the naming cannot be completed this phase

lines: Ross walks the forty meters without hurrying. He has the log in a clear evidence bag, chain-of-custody label already filled out in his neat block print. He hands it to Torah at the threshold without a word. Then he steps inside and stands against the wall, watching the outline on the floor. He does not look away from it.

Ross gets the log to the threshold but facility management has followed him down the corridor—a night supervisor demanding to know what is in the bag. Ross holds the bag behind his back and looks at Torah. He is not going to lie. He is waiting for Torah to handle it. The delay costs two minutes and the seepage line crosses the threshold edge.

Voss has been moved. Someone—Ross doesn't know who—walked him out of the manager's office and toward the east wing. Ross is in the corridor with the log when he sees Voss twenty meters away, and the floor between them goes dark with fluid all at once. Ross stops. He does not advance. He gets on comms: 'Torah. We have a problem.'

Brooks hits the door at the exact moment the pressure drops. It gives. He's through in two steps, moving left along the wall, and his voice comes back flat and immediate over comms: 'Clear left. Clear right. Bring the log.' The floor is wet under his boots but it holds. He does not look at the outline.

Ross walks the forty meters without hurrying. He has the log in a clear evidence bag, chain-of-custody label already filled out in his neat block print. He hands it to Torah at the threshold without a word. Then he steps inside and stands against the wall, watching the outline on the floor. He does not look away from it.

artifact_options.label: Use the dispute language
artifact_options.effect_line: Iyad borrows the File 7-C wording to describe the evidence as condition and custody, not accusation.

lines: Iyad pulls the File 7-C dispute record and rewrites the Room 14 note in condition language: time, pressure, duty, custody. No interpretation.
The floor keeps seeping, but the threshold stops reacting to the report itself. Ross has a cleaner path to the duty log.

intercept:success.queued_line: Brooks has Room 14 open and held. Ross has to bring the document in before the floor reaches the corridor.

intercept:partial.queued_line: Brooks is inside and hurt, but the room is open long enough for Ross to bring the document in.

intercept:failure.queued_line: The first door entry failed. Room 14 is not held, and the naming cannot happen as planned.

seal:success.queued_line: The duty log is in custody at Room 14. Torah can name the keeper against the physical document.

seal:partial.queued_line: Ross has the log at the threshold, but facility management has challenged the custody chain and the floor is crossing the line.

seal:failure.queued_line: Voss has been moved toward the east wing. The evidence chain and the suspect are now in the same live corridor.

intercept.queued_line: Someone has to go through that door with the document before the floor reaches the corridor.

seal.queued_line: Someone has to go through that door with the document before the floor reaches the corridor.

offering_floor_01.speaker: SITREP
offering_floor_01.line_1: Owen reports the east wall pinned, two officers down, and eleven civilians on the offering line. The squad has one surge window.
offering_floor_01.line_2: The floor gauge logs metal pulling from squad gear. Park reaches the door with containment before the next pressure wave.

buttons.label: Owen: Suppress the Line
buttons.preview: Owen uses her fire team to suppress the offering line perimeter—noise, movement, directed light—fixing civilians in...

buttons.label: Saye: Call the Window
buttons.preview: Saye uses her real-time emission read to call the four-minute window between surges, giving the squad...

operation_plans.label: Owen: Suppress the Line
operation_plans.primary_skill: tactical fire command under anomalous contact
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress and fix the offering line perimeter, establish sectors, hold civilians back without crossing the line, create a bound lane to the core for Park
operation_plans.intent: Owen uses her fire team to suppress the offering line perimeter—noise, movement, directed light—fixing civilians in place without crossing the line, opening a lane for Park to reach the core
operation_plans.yield: Offering line is fixed; civilians stop moving forward; Park can bound to the core in the next window; Owen's sector hold gives Torah time to begin the disclosure
operation_plans.risk: If the fix breaks or a civilian surges, Owen has to cross the line to intercept; the idol hits her with a pressure wave; she takes a rib injury and her availability drops for the resolution phase

lines: Owen's voice cuts through the hum: 'Sectors set. Nobody moves.' The fire team's lights sweep the offering line and the civilians stop, blinking, some of them looking at their own hands like they forgot what they were carrying. The hum does not stop but it does not climb. Park is already moving.

The line holds for twenty seconds. Then a man near the back—older, contractor vest, no badge left on his chest—takes one step forward and Owen has to step into the lane to block him. The idol registers her. The pressure hits her left side. She goes down on one knee, gets back up, does not say anything on comms for three seconds. 'Line is partial. Park, move now.'

Owen's sector sweep breaks when two civilians surge simultaneously from opposite ends of the line. She crosses to intercept the nearer one and the idol hits her full. She goes into the east wall hard enough to crack the drywall. Her comms are live—Torah can hear her breathing—but she does not speak. Park is still at the door. The offering line is moving again.

operation_plans.label: Saye: Call the Window
operation_plans.primary_skill: remote emission analysis and surge-window timing
operation_plans.tactical_step: Saye reads the emission pattern from the van and calls the exact surge window, giving Torah and Owen the timing to coordinate suppress, bound, and breach without being caught in the peak
operation_plans.intent: Saye uses her real-time emission read to call the four-minute window between surges, giving the squad a timed lane to move and breach without the broadcast at peak strength
operation_plans.yield: Squad has a confirmed window; Owen's suppress plan and Park's breach are coordinated; the idol cannot hit at full strength during the window; base success of the resolution phase improves
operation_plans.risk: If Saye's read is wrong or her comms are cut by the broadcast, the squad moves on a false window and hits the surge at full strength; Park is exposed at the core during peak emission

lines: Saye's voice is flat and exact on comms: 'Window opens in eight seconds. It runs forty-two seconds. Do not be at the core when it closes.' Torah relays it to Owen. Owen relays it to Park. For forty-two seconds the hum drops half a register and the pull on Torah's badge eases. It is enough.

Saye calls the window but the broadcast clips her comms at the twenty-second mark. Torah gets half the timing. Owen moves on the partial call and reaches the core with twelve seconds left in the window. Park is ten steps behind. 'I'm not going to make it in the window,' Park says, not panicking, just stating it. She makes it in eleven seconds. The surge hits as she seals the first containment unit. Her hands shake afterward but the seal holds.

Saye's monitoring equipment pulls a false read—the anomalous scrap in the core is interfering with her baseline. She calls the window four seconds early. Owen and Park move. The surge hits at full strength while Park is at the core housing. The pressure wave throws Park back six feet. She keeps hold of one containment unit. The kit is on the floor, open, three units cracked. Saye says, quietly, on comms: 'I had the wrong baseline. I'm sorry.' She does not say anything else for two minutes.

Owen's voice cuts through the hum: 'Sectors set. Nobody moves.' The fire team's lights sweep the offering line and the civilians stop, blinking, some of them looking at their own hands like they forgot what they were carrying. The hum does not stop but it does not climb. Park is already moving.

Saye's voice is flat and exact on comms: 'Window opens in eight seconds. It runs forty-two seconds. Do not be at the core when it closes.' Torah relays it to Owen. Owen relays it to Park. For forty-two seconds the hum drops half a register and the pull on Torah's badge eases. It is enough.

intercept:success.queued_line: Owen fixes the offering line. Park has a clear breach lane before the next surge.

intercept:partial.queued_line: Owen is hurt but the line is held for a narrow breach. Park has to move before the partial fix collapses.

intercept:failure.queued_line: The offering line is moving again and Owen is down at the east wall. The core breach is no longer clean.

torah_speaks:success.queued_line: Saye calls the window cleanly. Park and Owen can move on the core before the next surge.

torah_speaks:partial.queued_line: Saye lost half the timing call. Park has a first seal down, but the breach window is closing around her.

torah_speaks:failure.queued_line: Saye called the window early. Park is thrown back at the core with cracked containment units on the floor.

intercept.queued_line: The window between surges is closing; Owen needs the line cleared before she can move on the core.

torah_speaks.queued_line: The window between surges is closing; Owen needs the line cleared before she can move on the core.

room_censers_and_seams.speaker: SITREP
room_censers_and_seams.line_1: Sub-Level 3 command floor logs active faction orders. Two officers hold terminals, Seam C-7 is widening, and an egress seam is forming.
room_censers_and_seams.line_2: Ross has the command log. Timestamps put the seam eleven seconds after the first unauthorized order cleared, while Owen holds overwatch at the junction door.

buttons.label: Ross: Run the Audit
buttons.preview: Ross wants to reach the terminals, hard-shut the command system to stop new orders, pull the...

buttons.label: Owen: Cordon and Halt
buttons.preview: Owen wants to fix the faction at the terminals by holding the door and cutting off...

operation_plans.label: Ross: Run the Audit
operation_plans.primary_skill: evidence custody and authorization chain procedure
operation_plans.tactical_step: Bound across the seam under Owen's overwatch, reach the terminals, suppress the faction's ability to issue further commands by pulling the power conduit, then run the credential revocation audit with Torah's authority anchoring the record
operation_plans.intent: Ross wants to reach the terminals, hard-shut the command system to stop new orders, pull the faction's tokens, log the revocation with Torah's credential, and seal the tokens in the evidence bag before the egress seam opens
operation_plans.yield: The authorization record is corrected, the tokens are in custody, the seams stop widening, and the squad can evacuate through the north corridor before it closes
operation_plans.risk: If Ross is crossing when the seam jumps, he drops; if the audit is rushed and the evidence seal is incomplete, the record stays contested and the anomaly flag persists on the Institute's command chain

lines: Ross crossed the seam on Owen's call, moving fast and low with the safety line trailing. He pulled the power conduit behind the server rack and the terminals went dark. The faction officers stood up. Ross did not look at them. He opened the audit kit on the nearest workstation, logged the revocation with Torah's credential anchoring the entry, and bagged the faction's tokens one at a time, reading each badge number aloud for the record. Owen called the seam width from the door: holding at 9 cm. Saye confirmed the D-8 sensor had stopped moving. The strip lights steadied. Ross sealed the evidence bag and held it up without turning around. 'Chain is clean,' he said. 'Log is closed.'

Ross made it across and pulled the conduit, but the audit ran long. Saye called the D-8 seam at 14 cm before the revocation entry closed. Ross sealed the bag with one token still unlogged; he made the call to close the entry anyway and flag the gap for review. The seams stopped widening but did not hold steady. Owen got the squad out through the north corridor with 60 seconds to spare. The evidence bag is sealed but the chain-of-custody flag will follow the case. Ross has not said anything since the debrief started.

Ross was two steps past the seam when it jumped. The south side dropped 30 cm in under a second and the safety line went taut and then slack. Owen pulled him back by the harness. The audit kit went into the void. The faction's terminals were still active. Saye called the D-8 seam at 16 cm and the north corridor was gone. Ross was on his hands and knees at the seam edge, looking down, not moving. Torah had to call his name twice before he stood up.

operation_plans.label: Owen: Cordon and Halt
operation_plans.tactical_step: Establish a cordon at the command floor door to fix the faction in place and prevent further orders, then evacuate non-essential personnel through the north corridor before the egress seam opens, buying time for the audit to run under controlled conditions
operation_plans.intent: Owen wants to fix the faction at the terminals by holding the door and cutting off their ability to call for support, evacuate anyone not needed for the audit, and hold the cordon until Ross can run the credential revocation under less time pressure
operation_plans.yield: The egress corridor stays open, non-essential personnel are out, and the audit runs without the squad racing the D-8 seam
operation_plans.risk: If the faction's third member is behind the cordon line, Owen's position is compromised; if the cordon holds too long, the C-7 seam widens past safe crossing width and Ross cannot reach the terminals

lines: Owen took the door frame and held it. She did not announce the cordon; she just stood in the gap with her hand on the frame and looked at the two faction officers until they stopped typing. The third faction member came out of the server room corridor with his badge in his hand, already talking. Owen pointed at the floor between them. He looked down at the seam and stopped talking. She evacuated the two support staff through the north corridor herself, one at a time, then came back to the door. 'Floor is yours,' she told Ross over comms. 'Seam is holding. Move.'

Owen held the door and got the support staff out, but the third faction member had already sent one more order before she reached him. Saye called the C-7 seam at 12 cm. Owen got the cordon established but the crossing window for Ross had narrowed. He made it across but the safety line was under tension the whole way. Owen watched the seam the entire time Ross was running the audit. She did not look at the faction officers once.

The third faction member was not in the server room. He came through the south access door behind Owen's position and reached the nearest terminal before she could close the gap. Three more orders cleared the system in the time it took Owen to cross the room. The C-7 seam jumped to 14 cm. The south side dropped again. One of the support staff was still on the wrong side of the egress corridor when the D-8 seam opened past 15 cm. Owen got her out through the server room ladder but it took eleven minutes and the audit had not started yet.

Ross crossed the seam on Owen's call, moving fast and low with the safety line trailing. He pulled the power conduit behind the server rack and the terminals went dark. The faction officers stood up. Ross did not look at them. He opened the audit kit on the nearest workstation, logged the revocation with Torah's credential anchoring the entry, and bagged the faction's tokens one at a time, reading each badge number aloud for the record. Owen called the seam width from the door: holding at 9 cm. Saye confirmed the D-8 sensor had stopped moving. The strip lights steadied. Ross sealed the evidence bag and held it up without turning around. 'Chain is clean,' he said. 'Log is closed.'

Owen took the door frame and held it. She did not announce the cordon; she just stood in the gap with her hand on the frame and looked at the two faction officers until they stopped typing. The third faction member came out of the server room corridor with his badge in his hand, already talking. Owen pointed at the floor between them. He looked down at the seam and stopped talking. She evacuated the two support staff through the north corridor herself, one at a time, then came back to the door. 'Floor is yours,' she told Ross over comms. 'Seam is holding. Move.'

artifact_options.label: Issue local command cards
artifact_options.effect_line: Torah uses the Meridian cards to split authority before the false command chain can feed the seams.

lines: Torah writes local authority cards before anyone crosses the seam. Owen gets one. Ross gets one. Saye logs them as separate cells, not one command.
The floor still opens under forged tokens, but it no longer treats the squad's own commands as one larger claim.

full_disclosure:success.queued_line: Ross gets the terminals dark, seals the faction tokens, and leaves a clean revocation entry ready for Torah.

full_disclosure:partial.queued_line: Ross closes the audit with one token unlogged. The seams slow, but the revocation record has a gap notation.

full_disclosure:failure.queued_line: Ross loses the audit kit when the seam jumps. The faction terminals are still active and the north corridor is gone.

intercept:success.queued_line: Owen holds the cordon and keeps the crossing window open. Ross can run the revocation under control.

intercept:partial.queued_line: Owen gets the cordon set late. Ross can still cross, but the seam is already at twelve centimeters.

intercept:failure.queued_line: The third faction member clears three more orders and the egress route fails. The audit has not started.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The squad has two options before the egress seam cuts off the north exit: reach the terminals and pull the faction credentials, or establish a cordon and force a halt before the audit can run.

intercept.queued_line: The squad has two options before the egress seam cuts off the north exit: reach the terminals and pull the faction credentials, or establish a cordon and force a halt before the audit can run.

chamber_seven_accusation_field.speaker: SITREP
chamber_seven_accusation_field.line_1: Chamber Seven is live. Two analysts are down inside, and the system has added Brooks and Torah to the evaluation queue from door-contact biometrics.
chamber_seven_accusation_field.line_2: Iyad says the analyst on the floor has been inside for ninety minutes. The system log has fifty-one entries, each one citing the last.

buttons.label: Iyad: Medical Halt
buttons.preview: Iyad uses her clinical credentials to formally suspend the evaluation on grounds of immediate physical risk...

buttons.label: Brooks: Force the Panel
buttons.preview: Brooks bypasses the system's authority entirely by physically destroying the environmental control panel inside the chamber...

operation_plans.label: Iyad: Medical Halt
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix—Iyad invokes Institute medical halt protocol to suspend the active evaluation, then breaches the door to triage the downed analyst while Brooks holds the threshold
operation_plans.intent: Iyad uses her clinical credentials to formally suspend the evaluation on grounds of immediate physical risk, then enters the chamber to stabilize the analyst on the floor before the next temperature cycle
operation_plans.yield: The system recognizes the medical halt, pauses escalation, and Iyad reaches the downed analyst before hypothermia becomes critical; the evaluation queue is frozen
operation_plans.risk: The system rejects the halt and logs Iyad's credentials as evidence of interference, adding her to the active file and escalating the pressure tone toward 22Hz

lines: Iyad keys her credentials into the door panel and says, flatly, 'Medical halt. Grounds: immediate physical risk to evaluation subjects. File 7-C is suspended pending clinical review.' There is a pause—three seconds, four—and then the display changes. EVALUATION SUSPENDED — MEDICAL OVERRIDE LOGGED. She is through the door before it finishes cycling, kit in hand, already on her knees beside the analyst on the floor. The pressure tone drops half a register. Brooks is in the doorway, watching the display. 'It accepted it,' he says, like he is not sure he believes it.

The system accepts the halt but does not pause the environmental controls—the temperature keeps cycling and the pressure tone holds at 18Hz. Iyad gets to the downed analyst and begins triage on a floor that is nine degrees and vibrating. She works without looking up. 'I need two minutes,' she says. 'Hold the door.' Brooks holds the door. The display reads: MEDICAL OVERRIDE LOGGED — EVALUATION CONTINUES PENDING REVIEW. The file is still open.

The system rejects the halt in under a second. The display reads: OVERRIDE ATTEMPT LOGGED — FILE 7-C UPDATED — CREDENTIAL HOLDER ADDED TO EVALUATION QUEUE. Iyad's name appears on the screen. She stares at it for a moment, then closes her kit with a precise, controlled motion that means she is angry and will not show it. 'It used my credentials as evidence,' she says. 'My own credentials.' The pressure tone rises. Brooks takes a step back from the door frame.

operation_plans.label: Brooks: Force the Panel
operation_plans.tactical_step: breach and clear—Brooks forces the door, moves to the environmental control panel, and physically disables it to stop the pressure tone and temperature cycling, then assists extraction of both analysts
operation_plans.intent: Brooks bypasses the system's authority entirely by physically destroying the environmental control panel inside the chamber, stopping the immediate physical hazard regardless of what the log says
operation_plans.yield: The pressure tone stops, the temperature cycling stops, and both analysts can be extracted safely; the physical threat is neutralized
operation_plans.risk: The breach triggers a system alert that escalates the log and begins formally recording Brooks's biometric data as an active subject; the file grows and the system transmits a preliminary report to Institute command before shutdown

lines: Brooks hits the door with his shoulder at the hinge side, not the lock, and it gives on the second impact. He is across the floor in four steps, past the analyst on the ground, and his hand finds the environmental control panel by the far wall. He does not look for a switch. He pulls the panel cover off and cuts the primary feed with the tool from his belt. The pressure tone stops mid-cycle. The temperature holds where it is—cold, but not cycling. The analyst against the wall slides down to sitting, slowly, like she has been holding herself up for a long time. Brooks looks at the display. It still reads EVALUATION IN PROGRESS. 'Physical hazard is down,' he says. 'File's still open.'

Brooks gets through the door and reaches the panel, but the system triggers the alert before he can cut the feed—the display flashes BREACH LOGGED — SUBJECT BROOKS ADDED TO EVALUATION QUEUE and the pressure tone spikes for three seconds before he pulls the panel. It drops back to 18Hz, not silent, but lower. The analyst on the floor is breathing. The one against the wall looks at Brooks and does not speak. The file now has Brooks in it. He does not mention this.

The door does not give on the first impact or the second. On the third, the system escalates—the display reads FORCED ENTRY ATTEMPT — ESCALATION AUTHORIZED and the pressure tone jumps to 22Hz. Torah feels it in his back teeth. Brooks stops hitting the door and steps back, breathing hard. Inside, the analyst on the floor makes a sound. Iyad is already moving to the intercom. 'We need another approach,' she says. 'Now.'

Iyad keys her credentials into the door panel and says, flatly, 'Medical halt. Grounds: immediate physical risk to evaluation subjects. File 7-C is suspended pending clinical review.' There is a pause—three seconds, four—and then the display changes. EVALUATION SUSPENDED — MEDICAL OVERRIDE LOGGED. She is through the door before it finishes cycling, kit in hand, already on her knees beside the analyst on the floor. The pressure tone drops half a register. Brooks is in the doorway, watching the display. 'It accepted it,' he says, like he is not sure he believes it.

Brooks hits the door with his shoulder at the hinge side, not the lock, and it gives on the second impact. He is across the floor in four steps, past the analyst on the ground, and his hand finds the environmental control panel by the far wall. He does not look for a switch. He pulls the panel cover off and cuts the primary feed with the tool from his belt. The pressure tone stops mid-cycle. The temperature holds where it is—cold, but not cycling. The analyst against the wall slides down to sitting, slowly, like she has been holding herself up for a long time. Brooks looks at the display. It still reads EVALUATION IN PROGRESS. 'Physical hazard is down,' he says. 'File's still open.'

artifact_options.label: Cite the duty log
artifact_options.effect_line: Iyad cites the Aldermoor log as proof that hidden injury is evidence for care, not proof of guilt.

lines: Iyad places the Aldermoor duty log beside File 7-C and reads the custody note aloud: condition, witness, responsibility. No verdict.
The evaluation system logs the citation but cannot classify it as an accusation. The pressure tone drops one register.

clinic:success.queued_line: Iyad gets the medical halt accepted. The system is suspended long enough for Saye and Torah to contest the file cleanly.

clinic:partial.queued_line: Iyad reaches the downed analyst, but the environmental controls are still cycling and the file remains open.

clinic:failure.queued_line: The system rejects Iyad's halt and adds her credentials to File 7-C. The pressure tone is rising.

intercept:success.queued_line: Brooks cuts the physical hazard, but File 7-C remains open and must be contested before it transmits.

intercept:partial.queued_line: Brooks lowers the physical hazard, but the breach alert adds him to the evaluation queue and the tone is not fully silent.

intercept:failure.queued_line: The forced entry fails. The system authorizes escalation and the analysts are still inside.

clinic.queued_line: Someone has to go through that door, and whoever does goes into the file.

intercept.queued_line: Someone has to go through that door, and whoever does goes into the file.

room_seven_times_over.speaker: lt_mara_owen
room_seven_times_over.line_1: Owen reports a halt at the partition glass. The shape in the corner office has moved to the window wall, and it is eating.
room_seven_times_over.line_2: Park reports the jammer ready. Saye reports three hours and twenty minutes remaining, plus a withdrawal statement no one has requested yet.

buttons.label: Breach and fix Voss
buttons.preview: Owen wants to get Voss physically contained before the degradation spreads further and before the seventh...

buttons.label: Jam the broadcast now
buttons.preview: Park wants to cut the audio before the breach so Voss is not actively stimulated by...

operation_plans.label: Breach and fix Voss
operation_plans.primary_skill: tactical entry, threat control under contact
operation_plans.tactical_step: React to contact: breach corner office, suppress and fix Voss against the window wall, establish security on all glass panels, evacuate compromised staff while Voss is held
operation_plans.intent: Owen wants to get Voss physically contained before the degradation spreads further and before the seventh hour makes containment irrelevant
operation_plans.yield: Voss is fixed against the window wall, compromised staff are moved to the elevator lobby, the floor is clear for the refutation step
operation_plans.risk: Voss in full degradation is faster than expected. If Owen's entry is not clean, he exits the office into the open floor and the two compromised staff members are in his path

lines: Owen goes through the door low and fast. Voss is at the window wall, both hands flat on the glass, head down. He does not turn. She gets a shoulder into him before he can move and pins him against the frame. He makes a sound that is not a word. Park is already pulling the first seated staff member toward the elevator lobby. Brooks confirms stairwell clear on comms. Owen does not let go. Her voice, when she calls the floor clear, is completely level.

Owen gets through the door. Voss turns faster than the briefing suggested. She gets hold of one arm but he pulls the planter box off the sill and the ceramic goes across the floor. Park takes a cut above the knee from a shard — not deep, but she goes down for a moment. Owen gets Voss against the wall on the second attempt. The staff evacuation is slower. One of the seated individuals has to be carried. Brooks leaves the stairwell to help and the perimeter is open for four minutes.

Owen breaches clean but Voss is already moving when the door opens. He goes past her into the corridor. The two compromised staff members do not move out of his path. Owen gets a hand on his collar and he pulls her off her feet. Brooks abandons the stairwell and intercepts at the elevator lobby. It takes all three of them to establish control and by the time the floor is clear, Saye's clock has lost forty minutes. Park is sitting against the wall pressing her sleeve to her forearm. She says she's fine. Owen does not argue with her.

operation_plans.label: Jam the broadcast now
operation_plans.primary_skill: signal intercept, technical suppression under pressure
operation_plans.tactical_step: Suppress and fix: arm the portable jammer to kill broadcast audio across the floor, slowing new degradation onset and reducing Voss's agitation before breach, then hold for Owen's entry
operation_plans.intent: Park wants to cut the audio before the breach so Voss is not actively stimulated by his own voice when the door opens, and to stop the two compromised staff members from worsening
operation_plans.yield: Broadcast audio drops. Voss's agitation visibly decreases. The two compromised staff members stop their repetitive behavior. Owen has a cleaner entry window.
operation_plans.risk: Park's jammer has a known interference pattern with the building's fire suppression sensors. If the sensor trips, the floor goes into automated lockdown and the service elevator is disabled. The squad is sealed in with no extraction route until Brooks can override from the stairwell panel, which takes time they may not have.

lines: Park arms the jammer and the audio cuts mid-sentence. The silence on the floor is immediate and strange. The nearest screen's captions keep running but there is no voice behind them. Through the frosted glass, the shape in the corner office goes still. Park watches it for three seconds, then says to Owen: 'You have a window.' Owen moves.

The jammer kills the audio but the fire sensor trips two seconds later. A soft alarm begins cycling. The automated lockdown drops a secondary door across the elevator lobby — not the service elevator, but the main corridor. Brooks is on the wrong side of it. He gets to the stairwell panel but the override takes six minutes. Park holds the jammer signal the entire time, watching the clock. When Brooks gets the door up, he looks at Park and she looks at the floor.

The jammer arms and the audio cuts, but the interference hits the fire suppression system harder than the spec sheet suggested. The sprinklers on the east side of the floor activate. The two compromised staff members stand up when the water hits them — not recovered, just reactive — and one of them walks directly toward the corner office door. Owen has to redirect to intercept the civilian before she can breach. The window closes. Voss, stimulated by the movement and the noise, puts his shoulder through the frosted glass panel beside the door. Park shuts the jammer down and the audio comes back. Saye's voice on comms is very quiet: 'I'm adjusting the clock. You have two hours and fifty minutes.'

Owen goes through the door low and fast. Voss is at the window wall, both hands flat on the glass, head down. He does not turn. She gets a shoulder into him before he can move and pins him against the frame. He makes a sound that is not a word. Park is already pulling the first seated staff member toward the elevator lobby. Brooks confirms stairwell clear on comms. Owen does not let go. Her voice, when she calls the floor clear, is completely level.

Park arms the jammer and the audio cuts mid-sentence. The silence on the floor is immediate and strange. The nearest screen's captions keep running but there is no voice behind them. Through the frosted glass, the shape in the corner office goes still. Park watches it for three seconds, then says to Owen: 'You have a window.' Owen moves.

Park arms the jammer and the audio cuts mid-sentence. The silence on the floor is immediate and strange. The nearest screen's captions keep running but there is no voice behind them. Through the frosted glass, the shape in the corner office goes still. Park watches it for three seconds, then says to Owen: 'You have a window.' Owen moves.

intercept:breach-and-fix-voss:success.queued_line: Owen has Voss fixed at the window wall. The floor is clear enough for Saye's refutation to be read on record.

intercept:breach-and-fix-voss:partial.queued_line: Owen gets Voss contained on the second attempt, but Park is cut and the evacuation is slower than planned.

intercept:breach-and-fix-voss:failure.queued_line: Voss gets onto the open floor. The team loses forty minutes and the refutation window is no longer comfortable.

intercept:jam-the-broadcast-now:success.queued_line: Park cuts the audio and gives Owen a clean entry window. The captions still run, so the refutation has to happen on the floor.

intercept:jam-the-broadcast-now:partial.queued_line: The jammer cuts audio but trips a sensor. Brooks is briefly separated by lockdown and the clock loses six minutes.

intercept:jam-the-broadcast-now:failure.queued_line: The jammer triggers sprinklers, the window closes, and Voss breaks through the office glass as the audio returns.

intercept.queued_line: The office door has to open before anything else can happen.

corpus_anchor_points.source_fingerprint: The feast where sacred vessels are used for self-glorification, the hand writing on the wall, the king who will not be humbled until the night of judgment
corpus_anchor_points.playable_transform: The broadcast loop is the feast: Voss's declaration plays on every screen, using the floor's own infrastructure as a monument to his authority. The captions are the writing on the wall.
required_visible_details: Every screen on the ceiling grid running the same looped video with live captions
The caption line WE ARE THE FINAL AUTHORITY ON WHAT WE ARE visible on the nearest screen
The broadcast audio filling the corridor in Voss's own flat, even voice
corpus_anchor_points.followup_payoff: Destroying the screens without the refutation mirrors the desecration of the vessels: it accelerates judgment rather than closing it

corpus_anchor_points.source_fingerprint: The great tree that sheltered all flesh, whose height reached the sky, cut down until the stump remains bound with iron and bronze
corpus_anchor_points.playable_transform: Voss's public image — the company's reach, the staff who depended on him, the floor designed to project his authority — is the tree. The degradation is the cutting. The stump is what remains if the squad closes the case: Voss alive, diminished, accountable.
required_visible_details: The ornamental planter boxes demolished, soil and ceramic shards across the floor
The corner office designed for maximum visibility: glass walls, elevated platform, city view on three sides
Staff members who depended on Voss's authority now seated on the floor, unable to function
corpus_anchor_points.followup_payoff: A partial closure leaves Voss as the stump: alive, stripped of command, with a remainder that the Institute will need to monitor

corpus_anchor_points.source_fingerprint: Effective team communication under time pressure requires updating members, anticipating needs, and redirecting strategy before the window closes
corpus_anchor_points.playable_transform: The squad's coordination under the seven-hour clock is the procedural spine. Saye anticipates the refutation need before anyone asks. Park holds the jammer pending Owen's call. Brooks holds the perimeter without the full picture. The mission fails if communication breaks down between these roles.
required_visible_details: Saye's comms line giving the countdown unprompted: she is already anticipating the team's needs
Park waiting for Owen's signal before arming the jammer: the redirect moment
Brooks operating on secondhand information: the communication gap that can cost time
corpus_anchor_points.followup_payoff: If the squad's communication fractures under pressure, the seven-hour window closes before the refutation is on record

room_devoted_property.speaker: SITREP
room_devoted_property.line_1: Ross reports the evidence corridor active. Three contractors are injured, and Iyad reports eleven items missing from the custody log.
room_devoted_property.line_2: Owen reports pressure expanding upward. The cordon has fifteen minutes before the stairwell door becomes the threshold.

buttons.label: Brooks holds the line
buttons.preview: Brooks takes the stairwell door as a hold position, physically bracing the threshold against the pursuit-front's...

buttons.label: Iyad, run the gap
buttons.preview: Iyad reads the discrepancy record aloud on comms—item by item, date by date—creating an evidentiary record...

operation_plans.label: Brooks holds the line
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix: establish security at the stairwell threshold, hold the pursuit-front's advance, buy time for the symbolic close
operation_plans.intent: Brooks takes the stairwell door as a hold position, physically bracing the threshold against the pursuit-front's advance while Torah works Ross
operation_plans.yield: The pursuit-front is slowed; the ground floor stays clear; Owen does not have to pull the cordon; Ross has no exit pressure to hide behind
operation_plans.risk: The pursuit-front is stronger than expected; Brooks takes direct contact and is injured or driven back, collapsing the threshold and forcing an early evacuation decision

lines: Brooks puts his back to the stairwell door and sets his feet. The groaning drops half a register—not gone, but held. He does not look away from the door. He says, flat: 'You have time. Use it.' The door frame creaks but does not give.

Brooks gets to the door and holds it for ninety seconds before the pressure spikes. He braces, absorbs it, stays upright—but the door buckles outward an inch and does not go back. He calls over his shoulder without turning: 'Whatever you're doing, do it faster.' His voice is steady. His hands are not.

The pursuit-front hits the door before Brooks reaches threshold position. He is driven back three steps, shoulder into the wall, and the door swings open. Cold pressure fills the ground floor. Brooks gets up without a word, but he is moving carefully, and Owen's voice on comms goes very quiet.

operation_plans.label: Iyad, run the gap
operation_plans.tactical_step: symbolic close: use the custody log gap as formal evidence to force Ross toward confession before physical entry
operation_plans.intent: Iyad reads the discrepancy record aloud on comms—item by item, date by date—creating an evidentiary record that makes Ross's silence operationally untenable
operation_plans.yield: Ross cracks under the weight of the documented record; he names the items before the squad has to descend; the confession starts the closure sequence
operation_plans.risk: Ross holds. Iyad's recitation hardens him instead of breaking him; the delay costs structural time and the pursuit-front advances while Torah waits for a confession that does not come

lines: Iyad's voice comes through the earpiece without inflection: item designation, date, Ross's signature, item count: blank. Item designation, date, Ross's signature, item count: blank. Eleven times. By the eighth entry Ross has uncrossed his arms. By the eleventh he is looking at the floor. He says, quietly: 'Storage unit four. Lower level. Left side, behind the secondary rack.' The stairwell door stops rattling.

Iyad gets through six entries before Ross cuts her off. 'I know what the forms say.' He does not confess. But he stops lying: 'Unit four. Lower level.' He will not say the rest. The pursuit-front does not collapse—but it pauses, like something waiting to hear the rest of the sentence.

Ross listens to all eleven entries without moving. When Iyad finishes, he says: 'I want Institute counsel before I say anything.' The pursuit-front does not care about counsel. The door blows open. Owen's voice on comms: 'Pressure just hit the lobby glass. I'm pulling the outer perimeter back.'

Brooks puts his back to the stairwell door and sets his feet. The groaning drops half a register—not gone, but held. He does not look away from the door. He says, flat: 'You have time. Use it.' The door frame creaks but does not give.

Iyad's voice comes through the earpiece without inflection: item designation, date, Ross's signature, item count: blank. Item designation, date, Ross's signature, item count: blank. Eleven times. By the eighth entry Ross has uncrossed his arms. By the eleventh he is looking at the floor. He says, quietly: 'Storage unit four. Lower level. Left side, behind the secondary rack.' The stairwell door stops rattling.

room_roofline_aldren_court.speaker: lt_mara_owen
room_roofline_aldren_court.line_1: Owen reports from the fourth floor: cameras are tracking her team, Dolan's work order is on the floor, and Reyes's radio is transmitting from inside 4F.
room_roofline_aldren_court.line_2: Brooks reports the hidden drive secured. Security monitors are still looping, and Owen needs a corridor order before the mounts swing again.

buttons.label: Clear the corridor now
buttons.preview: Get Dolan and Reyes out of the sightline corridor before the camera mounts cause further injury...

buttons.label: Secure the drive first
buttons.preview: Lock down the hidden drive as admissible evidence before anything else moves, so the disclosure has...

operation_plans.label: Clear the corridor now
operation_plans.tactical_step: React to contact: Owen's two-element team bounds down the corridor, one element fixed on the camera arc to suppress tracking while the other moves to 4F door; breach and clear 4F to recover Reyes
operation_plans.intent: Get Dolan and Reyes out of the sightline corridor before the camera mounts cause further injury, and breach 4F to confirm Reyes's status
operation_plans.yield: Both workers recovered; Owen's team establishes security on the corridor; the physical threat is neutralized and the squad is consolidated for disclosure
operation_plans.risk: The camera mounts are faster than expected in the narrow corridor; one element takes a strike during the bound; Reyes is found disoriented inside 4F and requires clinic support

lines: Owen's voice on the radio is flat and exact: 'Corridor clear. Dolan's got a laceration on his forearm—camera mount caught him when he tried to redirect it. Reyes is in 4F, sitting on the floor, not injured, not responsive. We're bringing them both down.' A pause. 'The cameras stopped tracking when we breached the door. They're still pointing at the window, but they're not moving.' Brooks exhales slowly in the security office and does not comment.

Owen gets Dolan out. Reyes is harder—the door to 4F resists in a way that has nothing to do with the lock, and by the time they breach it, one of Owen's team has taken a glancing hit from a pan-tilt mount swinging hard through its arc. Reyes is inside, uninjured but fixed on the window, not responding to his name. Owen gets a hand on his shoulder and he moves, but slowly. 'We're out,' she radios. 'One of mine needs a look. Reyes is ambulatory.' She doesn't say what Reyes was looking at.

The bound goes wrong in the narrow corridor. The camera mount on the east end swings faster than Owen's team anticipated and catches the lead element across the shoulder—not a killing blow, but enough to drop them. Owen calls a break contact immediately, pulling her team back to the stairwell. Dolan and Reyes are still in the corridor. 'I'm not sending anyone back in there without a different approach,' Owen says on the radio. Her voice is controlled. That's the part that's hard to hear.

operation_plans.label: Secure the drive first
operation_plans.tactical_step: Fix the evidence: Brooks photographs the drive in situ, logs its position, bags it under chain-of-custody protocol, and confirms the date range before Owen moves—establishing the evidentiary basis for disclosure before the corridor is cleared
operation_plans.intent: Lock down the hidden drive as admissible evidence before anything else moves, so the disclosure has a physical record to cite and cannot be challenged
operation_plans.yield: Drive secured with full chain of custody; date range and Vess's credentials confirmed on record; Brooks has the evidentiary package Torah needs for the naming
operation_plans.risk: The delay gives the camera mounts more time to track Owen's team; the monitors in the security office begin showing a second image alongside the 4F loop—Brooks's own reflection, from an angle no camera covers

lines: Brooks works without looking at the monitors. He photographs the drive in place, logs the rack position, notes the cable routing, bags it, and reads the label aloud into his field recorder: 'Vess employee ID, date range beginning six weeks prior to system installation.' He sets the bag on the desk. 'Chain of custody is clean,' he says. 'Torah, you have what you need.' He still hasn't looked at the monitors. The second image—his reflection—is gone.

Brooks gets the drive bagged and logged, but the date range on the label is partially overwritten—someone tried to scratch it out, recently, with something sharp. He can read enough. 'Credentials are Vess's. Date range is approximate but consistent,' he says. 'It'll hold.' He glances at the monitors once, by accident. His reflection is there for a moment, then gone. He doesn't mention it on the radio.

Brooks is halfway through the in-situ photography when the monitors shift. All nine screens show the same image: Brooks, standing at the rack, from directly above—an angle that requires a camera in the ceiling, and there is no camera in the ceiling. He freezes. The drive is in his hand but the chain-of-custody log is open on the desk and he has not written anything in it yet. 'I need a minute,' he says on the radio. Torah can hear that he means more than a minute.

Owen's voice on the radio is flat and exact: 'Corridor clear. Dolan's got a laceration on his forearm—camera mount caught him when he tried to redirect it. Reyes is in 4F, sitting on the floor, not injured, not responsive. We're bringing them both down.' A pause. 'The cameras stopped tracking when we breached the door. They're still pointing at the window, but they're not moving.' Brooks exhales slowly in the security office and does not comment.

Brooks works without looking at the monitors. He photographs the drive in place, logs the rack position, notes the cable routing, bags it, and reads the label aloud into his field recorder: 'Vess employee ID, date range beginning six weeks prior to system installation.' He sets the bag on the desk. 'Chain of custody is clean,' he says. 'Torah, you have what you need.' He still hasn't looked at the monitors. The second image—his reflection—is gone.

room_pit_dothan.speaker: lt_mara_owen
room_pit_dothan.line_1: Owen reports gantry section three separated. Reiss is responsive on the shaft floor, and each time he speaks, the frame shudders.
room_pit_dothan.line_2: Brooks flags the evidence file: the timestamp predates the malfunction by six hours. Three signatures remain in play, and the colleagues are still in the break room.

buttons.label: Owen runs the rope
buttons.preview: Get Reiss out of the shaft before the next section drops

buttons.label: Brooks stops the vehicle
buttons.preview: Prevent the colleagues from leaving before the repudiation is filed; the closure condition requires a signatory...

operation_plans.label: Owen runs the rope
operation_plans.tactical_step: React to contact: Owen establishes secondary anchor, fixes Reiss on the line, two-person relay extraction while Brooks holds the brake from the platform edge
operation_plans.intent: Get Reiss out of the shaft before the next section drops
operation_plans.yield: Reiss extracted alive; Owen demonstrates the shaft can be beaten by direct action, which shifts the symbolic pressure toward the paperwork problem
operation_plans.risk: A pressure pulse hits during the descent; the gantry drops a section with Owen on the line

lines: Owen goes over the edge without ceremony. The shaft pulses twice while she descends and both times she locks the rope and waits, pressed flat against the concrete wall, until the frame stops moving. When she reaches Reiss he looks up at her like he stopped believing in this possibility hours ago. She clips him in without a word. Brooks works the brake. The extraction takes nine minutes. When Reiss clears the lip, Owen is already looking at the break room door.

Owen gets Reiss on the line and halfway up before the gantry drops another section. The brake jams. Brooks hauls by hand for the last four meters, and Reiss comes over the edge hard, one shoulder taking the concrete lip. Saye's voice on comms: 'He's breathing. Get him flat.' Owen pulls herself up last, moving carefully, and does not look at her hands.

The pulse hits when Owen is eight meters down. The secondary anchor holds but the primary frame shears and the rope line whips. Owen arrests the fall but the shock load tears the brake assembly. Brooks grabs the line bare-handed and holds it. Reiss is still below. Owen is hanging in the dark, and the shaft is very quiet now, which is worse than the noise.

operation_plans.label: Brooks stops the vehicle
operation_plans.primary_skill: evidence custody and site perimeter control
operation_plans.tactical_step: Break contact from the entrance post, move to the site gate, intercept the contractor vehicle and the colleagues before they can leave the perimeter, establish a cordon at the gate
operation_plans.intent: Prevent the colleagues from leaving before the repudiation is filed; the closure condition requires a signatory on-site
operation_plans.yield: Colleagues are held at the gate; the contractor vehicle is stopped; the exit route is closed, which removes the caravan-vector escape and forces the symbolic close
operation_plans.risk: Brooks leaves the brake position on the gantry; if Owen needs the brake during the intercept, there is no one on it

lines: Brooks walks to the gate at a pace that does not look like running. The contractor driver sees him coming and cuts the engine without being asked. Brooks stands at the vehicle door and says, quietly, 'Nobody leaves this site until I have a signature.' The colleagues in the break room hear this through the window. One of them stands up. Brooks does not move from the gate.

Brooks reaches the gate as the contractor vehicle is already rolling. He steps into the lane. The driver stops. Two of the three colleagues are already in the cab. The third is still in the break room. Brooks has two of them. He radios Owen: 'I need someone on the brake. Now.' There is a pause on the line before she answers.

Brooks is twenty meters from the gate when the contractor vehicle pulls out. He radios the plate. The colleagues are gone. He stands at the empty gate for a moment, then turns back toward the shaft. The false report is still in his hand. He looks at it like it has changed shape.

Brooks walks to the gate at a pace that does not look like running. The contractor driver sees him coming and cuts the engine without being asked. Brooks stands at the vehicle door and says, quietly, 'Nobody leaves this site until I have a signature.' The colleagues in the break room hear this through the window. One of them stands up. Brooks does not move from the gate.

room_forty_day_window.speaker: SITREP
room_forty_day_window.line_1: Owen reports Floor 3 active. Six casualties show sword-pattern lacerations, no attacker appears on camera, and one survivor is behind press unit four.
room_forty_day_window.line_2: Ross is ready to bound. The aisle cabinet is padlocked, and Saye needs wound data to confirm the targeting pattern.

buttons.label: Ross: bound to survivor
buttons.preview: Get the survivor out of the kill zone before the echo completes its next pass

buttons.label: Owen: hold the aisle
buttons.preview: Keep the echo from completing its pass while Ross extracts the survivor; buy Torah time to...

operation_plans.label: Ross: bound to survivor
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix then bound: Owen fixes the echo with suppressive action on its last known position while Ross bounds to press unit four, extracts the survivor, and moves her to the stairwell door
operation_plans.intent: Get the survivor out of the kill zone before the echo completes its next pass
operation_plans.yield: Survivor extracted; Ross relays wound measurements to Saye; Saye confirms targeting pattern; squad has confirmed data for the symbolic close
operation_plans.risk: If Owen's suppression breaks early, Ross is in the open during the bound and takes contact

lines: Ross moves the moment Owen calls clear. He keeps low, uses the press housings as cover, and reaches the worker in four seconds. She is conscious, one hand pressed to a shallow laceration on her forearm. He gets her up and moving. Behind him, Owen holds the aisle, and the repeating impact sound tracks toward her position and then stops, as if it has lost the scent. Ross gets the worker to the stairwell door and keys his comms: 'Saye, I'm sending you measurements now. Left forearm, seven centimeters, clean edge. Same as the others?' Saye's answer comes back flat: 'Same as the others. It's not selecting. It's just moving through.'

Ross makes the bound but the echo reorients faster than Owen can track. It catches him in the open for one step. He takes a glancing contact on his left shoulder, a shallow laceration that burns immediately, and he keeps moving. He gets the worker out. He does not mention the shoulder until Saye asks why his breathing changed. Owen, from the aisle: 'I had it. I had it for three seconds.' She does not say anything else.

Owen's suppression holds for two seconds and then the echo splits its path. Ross is halfway to press unit four when it finds him. The laceration opens across his right forearm, deep enough to matter. He goes down to one knee. The worker is still behind the press unit. Torah calls Owen back and Ross pulls himself to cover. Saye, over comms, very quiet: 'Ross. Tell me your depth.' Ross tells her. There is a pause. 'You need to close that before you do anything else.' The survivor is still in the kill zone.

operation_plans.label: Owen: hold the aisle
operation_plans.tactical_step: fix the echo's movement on the center aisle using the press units as cover positions; deny the echo a clear pass while Ross bounds; maintain position until Ross calls clear
operation_plans.intent: Keep the echo from completing its pass while Ross extracts the survivor; buy Torah time to reach the archive terminal
operation_plans.yield: Echo's movement is fixed; Ross extracts safely; Torah reaches the supervisor's desk and opens the HR archive
operation_plans.risk: If Owen holds too long without relief, she takes contact on the echo's second pass; the echo does not tire

lines: Owen does not move from the aisle. She tracks the sound, adjusts her position twice, and each time the echo's path deflects around her. It is not avoiding her. It is moving to the next available target and she is denying it the angle. When Ross calls clear, she steps back one position and keys Torah: 'Archive is open. Cabinet key is on the supervisor. Second body, left coat pocket.' She found it while holding. She does not explain when she had time to look.

Owen holds for one full pass and most of a second. On the second pass the echo finds a gap in her coverage and she takes contact on her left side, a shallow cut below the ribs that she does not report immediately. She holds the position anyway. When Ross calls clear she steps back and her voice on comms is level but slower than usual. Torah notices. Owen does not acknowledge it.

The echo does not behave the way Owen expects. It does not follow a straight path. It angles around her second position and she has to move, and when she moves she loses the fix. The sound is suddenly behind her. She turns and it has already passed. Ross is not yet at the press unit. Torah calls the bound off. Owen, back at the stairwell door, is looking at the center aisle with an expression that is not fear exactly. It is the look of someone recalculating a problem they thought they understood.

Owen does not move from the aisle. She tracks the sound, adjusts her position twice, and each time the echo's path deflects around her. It is not avoiding her. It is moving to the next available target and she is denying it the angle. When Ross calls clear, she steps back one position and keys Torah: 'Archive is open. Cabinet key is on the supervisor. Second body, left coat pocket.' She found it while holding. She does not explain when she had time to look.
