room_enough_until_morning.speaker: lt_mara_owen
room_enough_until_morning.line_1: Owen reports forty-one people served, one hundred nine counted at the door, and the cold-store warming while the canteen door moves.
room_enough_until_morning.line_2: Ross reports fourteen patients who will not stop eating. Two EMTs have joined them, and he needs an entry plan before crossing the canteen floor.

buttons.label: Vent the store outward
buttons.preview: Ross takes the forklift and drives the cold-store door outward into the loading bay, venting the...

buttons.label: Isolate patients, pull log
buttons.preview: Owen coordinates kitchen staff to clear all food from the canteen tables while she secures the...

operation_plans.label: Vent the store outward
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress/fix the cold-store door threat by breaching it outward with the loading bay forklift before it fails inward; establish security on the discharge zone in the open bay
operation_plans.intent: Ross takes the forklift and drives the cold-store door outward into the loading bay, venting the pressurized contents away from the canteen and the seated group
operation_plans.yield: Door threat neutralized; discharge contained in the open bay; squad has time to work the log and the patients without the door-failure clock
operation_plans.risk: If Ross misjudges the angle, the discharge hits the canteen wall and aerosolizes; if he hesitates, the door fails on its own timeline

lines: Ross does not announce it. He walks to the forklift, checks the forks, and drives it straight at the cold-store door at low speed. The door goes outward with a sound like a held breath releasing. The discharge—dense, pale, warm—spreads across the loading bay floor and stops. The smell is overwhelming for about four seconds and then it is just cold air. Ross parks the forklift across the bay entrance and gets off. 'Secure,' he says. 'Work fast.'

The door goes, but the angle is wrong. A portion of the discharge hits the canteen wall and runs down it in slow sheets. Ross gets the forklift across the bay entrance before more follows. Three of the seated patients turn their heads toward the wall. Iyad moves between them and it immediately. 'Do not let them touch that,' she says, and her voice is completely flat. The clock is shorter now.

The door fails before Ross reaches the forklift. The discharge enters the canteen at pressure. Ross is hit across the left arm and shoulder. He does not sit down—he backs against the wall and keeps his arm away from his face—but Iyad is already moving toward him, and the seated group has grown by two. Ross says, through his teeth, 'I'm not sitting. I'm not sitting.' He means it as information.

operation_plans.label: Isolate patients, pull log
operation_plans.tactical_step: fix the seated group by removing all food sources from their reach; breach the director's office for the master inventory; secure the distribution log under chain of custody before Voss can access it
operation_plans.intent: Owen coordinates kitchen staff to clear all food from the canteen tables while she secures the log and forces the director's office; isolating the patients from food sources slows the seated group's growth
operation_plans.yield: Patients stabilized without extraction; log secured; Voss's office opened; squad has the evidence needed for the closure condition
operation_plans.risk: If kitchen staff panic or refuse, the food sources cannot be cleared fast enough; if Voss is in his office, he may destroy records before Owen reaches him

lines: Owen tells the two remaining kitchen volunteers exactly what to do and does not wait for agreement. They clear the tables in under three minutes—trays, bread baskets, everything—while Owen photographs the log and bags it. The director's office door is hollow-core; she opens it with a fire extinguisher on the second hit. Voss is inside, standing at the shredder. The machine is running. Owen puts her hand on the feed tray and says, 'Step back.' He does. The shredder basket has maybe forty pages in it. She bags those too.

The tables are cleared but one volunteer freezes at the far end of the canteen and will not move. The patient nearest him reaches for the volunteer's jacket pocket—there is a wrapped granola bar in it. Owen gets there before contact, takes the bar, and walks it out the loading bay door. The volunteer sits down in an empty chair. He does not pick up any food. Owen watches him for ten seconds, decides he is not joining the group, and goes for the office. The shredder is already running when she gets there. She gets most of it.

Voss is not in his office. He has the master inventory binder with him and he is in the loading bay, moving toward his car. Brooks sees him from the roof and calls it down. Owen runs. She gets the binder but Voss has already torn out the summary pages—the ones with the cumulative totals. What remains is enough to show the pattern but not enough to prove the number. Owen stands in the parking lot holding a binder with ragged edges and does not say anything for a moment. Then: 'Brooks, I need you to tell me what you saw from up there. All of it. Start from when you arrived.'

Ross does not announce it. He walks to the forklift, checks the forks, and drives it straight at the cold-store door at low speed. The door goes outward with a sound like a held breath releasing. The discharge—dense, pale, warm—spreads across the loading bay floor and stops. The smell is overwhelming for about four seconds and then it is just cold air. Ross parks the forklift across the bay entrance and gets off. 'Secure,' he says. 'Work fast.'

Owen tells the two remaining kitchen volunteers exactly what to do and does not wait for agreement. They clear the tables in under three minutes—trays, bread baskets, everything—while Owen photographs the log and bags it. The director's office door is hollow-core; she opens it with a fire extinguisher on the second hit. Voss is inside, standing at the shredder. The machine is running. Owen puts her hand on the feed tray and says, 'Step back.' He does. The shredder basket has maybe forty pages in it. She bags those too.

room_station_kaph_door.speaker: SITREP
room_station_kaph_door.line_1: Station Kaph blast door is cycling shut. Ross and Vasek are inside with eleven weeks of falsified watch logs on the duty desk, and the door is on increment three.
room_station_kaph_door.line_2: Park reports ant-pattern interference on the terminal link. Saye has the incident form open, and Ross has the buried report.

buttons.label: Hold the door, send Brooks
buttons.preview: Owen commands Brooks through the gap to retrieve the report while she physically holds the door...

buttons.label: Suppress the interference node
buttons.preview: Park suppresses the ant-pattern interference before it severs the comms link, keeping Saye's filing channel open...

operation_plans.label: Hold the door, send Brooks
operation_plans.tactical_step: Suppress/fix: Owen holds the door gap while Brooks bounds through to reach Ross and physically recover the buried report
operation_plans.intent: Owen commands Brooks through the gap to retrieve the report while she physically holds the door open for extraction
operation_plans.yield: Brooks reaches Ross, recovers the report, and both exit through the held gap with time remaining for filing
operation_plans.risk: The door advances during the bound; Brooks or Ross is caught in the gap; Owen is injured holding the frame

lines: Owen plants her shoulder against the door frame and does not move. 'Brooks. Go. Now.' He goes. The door grinds against her position—she does not acknowledge it. Brooks comes back through the gap with Ross behind him, the report clutched in Ross's left hand, water-stained and rubber-banded. Owen steps back. The door keeps moving. 'Park,' Owen says. 'Get Saye on the line.'

Brooks gets through. The door advances one increment while he is inside—the gap narrows to the width of a turned shoulder. Ross passes the report through first. Brooks comes through sideways, scraping the frame, and Owen catches his arm. He is breathing hard. 'I'm fine,' he says, which means he is not. The report is intact. Vasek is still inside.

The door advances mid-bound. Brooks stops at the gap, reads the clearance, and pulls back. 'I can't make that.' Owen's jaw tightens. She does not argue with him. Inside, Ross's relay goes to static. The report is still in there. Owen turns to Park. 'Options.'

operation_plans.label: Suppress the interference node
operation_plans.tactical_step: Suppress/fix: Park targets the interference node at the terminal to arrest signal degradation and preserve the remote filing link to Saye
operation_plans.intent: Park suppresses the ant-pattern interference before it severs the comms link, keeping Saye's filing channel open and buying time for the report to be transmitted
operation_plans.yield: Interference arrested; comms link stable; Ross can transmit the report page by page without a physical bound through the gap
operation_plans.risk: Suppression attempt accelerates the interference; node destruction cuts the filing link; Saye is unreachable and symbolic closure becomes harder

lines: Park isolates the interference node and drives a targeted signal spike through it. The ant-pattern stutters—channels clear one by one, inward to outward, like something reversing its own work. Ross's voice comes through clean for the first time. 'I have the report. All of it. Transmitting now.' Park watches the pages come through on her screen. She does not look relieved. She looks like she is counting.

Park arrests the interference on three channels but the node reroutes through a fourth. The filing link holds—barely. Ross can transmit, but the connection is unstable. Every page takes twice as long. The door moves again while they wait. Park does not look up from the terminal. 'It's going to be close,' she says, which is the most she will say about it.

The interference node absorbs the suppression spike and accelerates. Every channel goes to ant-pattern simultaneously. Park's terminal link severs. The screen shows a single line of output—the same sensor entry, repeating, from week three of Holt's buried log—and then goes dark. Saye's relay drops. Park sits back. 'I cut us off,' she says. She says it once, flat, and does not say it again.

Park isolates the interference node and drives a targeted signal spike through it. The ant-pattern stutters—channels clear one by one, inward to outward, like something reversing its own work. Ross's voice comes through clean for the first time. 'I have the report. All of it. Transmitting now.' Park watches the pages come through on her screen. She does not look relieved. She looks like she is counting.

corpus_influences.room_application: Holt's falsified logs are the sluggard's sleep. The ant-pattern interference is the ant's leaderless, purposeful work—the station executing what Holt refused to do. The door closing is the harvest window shutting.
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: Holt's falsified logs are the sluggard's sleep. The ant-pattern interference is the ant's leaderless, purposeful work—the station executing what Holt refused to do. The door closing is the harvest window shutting.

ant-pattern signal interference crawling inward on every sensor channel simultaneously
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: Filing the buried report is the act of finally doing the work—the harvest gathered too late to prevent the door from moving, but in time to stop it from sealing

corpus_influences.followup_payoff: Partial closure: the report is filed, the door releases, but Holt is still unaccounted for—the talent was buried by someone who is now gone

corpus_influences.source_moment: Squad enrichment; commitment to team; newly assigned members vs. established team

pressure_command_review.speaker: operations
pressure_command_review.line_1: Command review opens on the secure channel before the next deployment packet clears. The questions are procedural at first: who authorized contact, who filed the language, who marked the anomaly closed.
pressure_command_review.line_2: Brooks stays standing at the back wall. Saye has three documents open and none of them are the one command asked for. Torah has to choose what kind of record the Institute receives.
outcomes: The complete record enters command channels. Nobody thanks Torah for it. The file is harder to bury, and the next review will have names attached.
Oversight adds a second authorization check to Torah's next deployment. Owen does not argue, but she reads the restriction twice before signing.
The narrow report protects the squad today. It also teaches command that Torah can be managed through paperwork.

pressure_artifact_custody.speaker: evidence_control
pressure_artifact_custody.line_1: Evidence Control refuses to release the latest custody item to general storage. The container has warmed twice since intake, both times when the mission record was opened.
pressure_artifact_custody.line_2: Park wants it sealed. Saye wants a supervised read. Ross wants the chain clean before anyone touches it again.
outcomes: The artifact goes cold behind a double seal. The lab loses a chance to understand it. Ross's chain remains clean.
Saye runs the read with Park watching the clock. The data is useful. The container does not stay cold.
Command accepts the exhibit and immediately asks why field custody retained it this long. Ross writes the transfer memo by hand.

pressure_symbolic_contamination.speaker: medical
pressure_symbolic_contamination.line_1: Medical holds Torah after debrief for a resonance check. The meter response is inside acceptable range, but the response comes before the meter is switched on.
pressure_symbolic_contamination.line_2: Iyad records the result without looking at Torah. Brooks waits outside the clinic door and does not ask for the number.
choices: Submit Torah to observation before redeployment
outcomes: Observation clears the worst reading, but Torah misses the informal pre-brief. The squad notices the empty chair.
The warning note follows Torah into the next packet. It is not a restriction yet.
The squad test finds minor exposure markers on two personnel. Nobody says the word contamination in the hallway.

pressure_squad_fracture.speaker: brooks
pressure_squad_fracture.line_1: The squad misses the first transport call. Nobody mutinies. Nobody refuses an order. They just do not move until Brooks tells them which door they are using.
pressure_squad_fracture.line_2: Owen sees it. Torah sees Owen see it. The next mission packet is ready, but the room has stopped pretending this is only a scheduling delay.
outcomes: Brooks takes six minutes and gets them moving. Nobody says morale. They do not need to.
Torah names the hesitation plainly. It costs comfort and buys honesty.
The squad moves. The silence moves with them.

pressure_closure_debt.speaker: operations
pressure_closure_debt.line_1: The unresolved-case board gains another red tag. It is not an emergency by Institute definition. It is only a list of things that were contained without being understood.
pressure_closure_debt.line_2: Saye stands in front of the board with her coffee untouched. The next packet is ready. The old ones are not quiet.
choices: Keep the team on new deployments
outcomes: Saye pulls one file back into active review. The next mission loses some analytical slack, but the red board gets shorter.
The new deployment proceeds. The old case remains on the board and in the room.
Command denies the window in writing. The denial becomes part of the record.

pressure_clinic.speaker: medical
pressure_clinic.line_1: The clinic has more field gear than chairs. Someone has left blood on the intake counter and cleaned around it instead of through it.
pressure_clinic.line_2: Iyad has three personnel marked fit with limitations. Owen wants them back. Brooks wants the truth. Torah has to decide whose version of ready counts.
outcomes: The team deploys short. Nobody likes it. Medical signs the hold without apology.
The restrictions are clear and inconvenient. Brooks reads them once and folds the paper into his vest.
Owen accepts the authority and the burden. If this goes wrong, it will be hers in writing.

pressure_public_exposure.speaker: public_affairs
pressure_public_exposure.line_1: Public Affairs sends a clipped summary: two civilian videos, one local reporter, one witness using the word miracle on a live feed.
pressure_public_exposure.line_2: Ross can bury the perimeter record for a few hours. Saye says the language in the comments is already changing the search results.
outcomes: The safety statement reduces panic and gives the Institute a public lie it can defend.
The denial is clean. It is also fragile. Everyone in the room knows both facts.
The Institute watches the language spread instead of stopping it. Saye says this may be useful. Ross does not answer.

captain_initial_report.speaker: Institute Operations
captain_initial_report.line_1: Symbolic incidents are no longer isolated. The first playable packet is ready for field deployment.
captain_initial_report.line_2: Torah deploys with the assigned squad. Current packet: Iron & Covenant Fitness — Bay 3.

lines: The first packet opens: Iron & Covenant Fitness — Bay 3. A commercial gym mid-renovation. One patron. One wig. One load-bearing column developing cracks at a rate that should take years.

story_thread.role: establish Institute procedure and Torah asset status

corpus_influences.source_moment: Institute symbolic asset deployment premise.

corpus_influences.room_application: Operations assigns Torah to a supervised field squad.
corpus_influences.followup_application: First mission packet tests the command vocabulary restriction.
corpus_influences.interlude_application: Later command-corridor beats can revisit Torah clearance.

character-owned plan with a personal risk
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: Follow-ups carry the chosen character risk into debrief, clinic, or field consequence.

resolution_borrowed_separation.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation.line_1: SITREP consolidates the first intervention: Reiss is contained, wig evidence is cut or burned, and Column C-4 is shored but not trusted.
resolution_borrowed_separation.line_2: The Separation Athletics packaging is on the bench, with the protocol guide open to a highlighted page. Torah has to decide what happens to the evidence and what happens to Reiss.

buttons.label: Saye: Full Disclosure
buttons.preview: Saye comes off remote comms and enters the site to take custody of the evidence personally...

buttons.label: Torah: Release the Vow
buttons.preview: Torah speaks the release directly—not as a religious performance but as the symbolic authority the anomaly...

operation_plans.label: Saye: Full Disclosure
operation_plans.primary_skill: anomaly pattern documentation, symbolic evidence analysis
operation_plans.tactical_step: evidence custody of wig remains and Separation Athletics materials, formal anomaly report with manufacturer identification, CASEVAC handoff to Institute medical with full disclosure of anomaly mechanism
operation_plans.intent: Saye comes off remote comms and enters the site to take custody of the evidence personally. She documents the wig remains, the protocol guide, and the manufacturer's materials as a complete anomaly package. She gives Reiss a full account of what happened to him—not a cover story. She files the manufacturer's identity with the Institute for follow-up action.
operation_plans.yield: Complete evidence chain. Reiss understands what was done to him. The Institute has the manufacturer's identity. The anomaly is documented in a way that prevents recurrence. Saye's silence during the mission is explained by what she found in the data—she was building the disclosure package.
operation_plans.risk: Full disclosure means the Institute's involvement is on record. The manufacturer has resources. The department head who denied the wig remains request is now on notice. Saye's report creates institutional pressure that may not resolve in the squad's favor.

lines: Saye is in Bay 3 within eight minutes of the all-clear. She does not look at the column. She looks at the bench—the packaging, the guide, the crushed water bottle. She photographs everything before she touches anything. When she sits down next to Reiss, she does not use clinical language. She tells him what the product was, what it did, and why it is not his fault. Reiss listens. He does not say anything for a long time. Then he asks who made it. Saye tells him. She has already filed the name. On the drive back, she tells Torah what she found in the thermal data that made her go quiet—a second heat signature, consistent with a second unit of the product, sold to a second buyer, location unknown. She says it the way you say something you have been holding for two hours.

Saye takes custody of the evidence. The disclosure to Reiss is partial—she tells him enough to explain the physical symptoms, not enough to explain the full anomaly mechanism. She is not lying; she is managing what he can absorb right now. The manufacturer's identity goes into the Institute file. The department head's denial of the wig remains request is noted but not escalated. The second heat signature is in Saye's personal notes, not the official report. She will decide later whether to include it. That decision will follow her.

Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them. The wig remains are in the evidence container but the chain of custody has a gap. Saye files what she has. The manufacturer's identity is in the report but the physical evidence is incomplete. Reiss is in Institute medical and has not been told anything. Saye sits in the vehicle for a long time after filing. Ross finds her there an hour later. He does not ask what she is thinking. He already knows.

operation_plans.label: Torah: Release the Vow
operation_plans.primary_skill: symbolic authority, vow acknowledgment, anomaly closure
operation_plans.tactical_step: symbolic close—Torah formally acknowledges the false vow's end in the presence of the wig remains, establishing that the separation is completed and the obligation discharged, followed by CASEVAC handoff and building clearance
operation_plans.intent: Torah speaks the release directly—not as a religious performance but as the symbolic authority the anomaly requires to close. The false vow was imposed without consent; Torah's acknowledgment that the separation is ended, in the presence of the remains, is the condition the anomaly's logic requires. This is the closure condition that force cannot provide.
operation_plans.yield: The anomaly closes completely. Any residual strength in the wig remains dissipates. Reiss's body begins to recover. The column fracture stops propagating. The squad has a clean close. Torah carries the weight of having spoken into something they do not fully understand.
operation_plans.risk: If Torah speaks the release and the anomaly does not close—if the wig was not sufficiently destroyed, or if the burn was incomplete—the discharge pulse reads Torah's authority as a challenge rather than a completion. The residual force redirects. The squad is inside the building when it does.

lines: Torah stands at the column base with the evidence container open in front of them. The wig remains are inside—cut, partially burned, still warm. Owen is at the breach point. Brooks is outside. Park is watching the column. Torah does not perform anything. They say, plainly, that the separation is ended, that the obligation is discharged, that what was taken without consent is returned. The warmth in the container goes out. The column does not make another sound. Reiss, on the stretcher at the east entrance, stops shaking. Ross reports from the perimeter that the instrument readings have dropped to baseline. Saye, on comms, exhales. It is the first sound she has made in twenty minutes. Owen does not say anything. She marks the building clear and starts the CASEVAC handoff. She will not talk about this part of the debrief.

Torah speaks the release. The anomaly responds—the warmth in the container drops, the column fracture stops—but the close is incomplete. A section of the wig's base is still adhered to Reiss's scalp, in Institute medical custody now, and the obligation has not fully discharged. Reiss's tremor continues at a lower level. The instrument readings drop but do not reach baseline. The anomaly is suppressed. It is not closed. Saye flags the residual in her report. The evidence container goes into restricted storage. Torah knows, without being told, that this will come back.

Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot. Not warm—hot, the kind of heat that makes Park step back and Owen call everyone to the breach point. The discharge pulse moves through the floor. The temporary column props hold but one of them buckles. The building alarm triggers. Everyone exits. The container is left inside—too hot to carry. Ross seals the east entrance. The instrument readings spike and then drop to a level that Saye, on comms, describes as 'not baseline.' She does not elaborate. The building is condemned. The container is inside it. Torah stands at the cordon line and does not speak for the rest of the operation. Owen files the after-action report. She leaves Torah's role in the resolution section blank and sends it to Saye to complete.

Saye is in Bay 3 within eight minutes of the all-clear. She does not look at the column. She looks at the bench—the packaging, the guide, the crushed water bottle. She photographs everything before she touches anything. When she sits down next to Reiss, she does not use clinical language. She tells him what the product was, what it did, and why it is not his fault. Reiss listens. He does not say anything for a long time. Then he asks who made it. Saye tells him. She has already filed the name. On the drive back, she tells Torah what she found in the thermal data that made her go quiet—a second heat signature, consistent with a second unit of the product, sold to a second buyer, location unknown. She says it the way you say something you have been holding for two hours.

Torah stands at the column base with the evidence container open in front of them. The wig remains are inside—cut, partially burned, still warm. Owen is at the breach point. Brooks is outside. Park is watching the column. Torah does not perform anything. They say, plainly, that the separation is ended, that the obligation is discharged, that what was taken without consent is returned. The warmth in the container goes out. The column does not make another sound. Reiss, on the stretcher at the east entrance, stops shaking. Ross reports from the perimeter that the instrument readings have dropped to baseline. Saye, on comms, exhales. It is the first sound she has made in twenty minutes. Owen does not say anything. She marks the building clear and starts the CASEVAC handoff. She will not talk about this part of the debrief.

cooldown_borrowed_separation.speaker: brooks
cooldown_borrowed_separation.line_1: Brooks is sitting on the vehicle's rear step with her forearm wrapped, watching Ross redirect a news crew away from the cordon. She has not filed her report.
cooldown_borrowed_separation.line_2: Park comes out of the building with the evidence container and sets it in the cargo bay without looking at Brooks. Brooks watches Park do it and does not ask what is in her expression.

lines: Brooks says, to no one in particular: 'He wasn't trying to hurt anyone.' Park does not answer. Ross comes back from the cordon and sits on the bumper next to Brooks. He says: 'Did Reiss know what he bought?' Brooks looks at her wrapped arm. 'He knew what it promised.' Ross nods. He does not ask the follow-up question. Owen is inside the vehicle, writing. She has been writing for forty minutes. She has not come out. Saye's channel has been quiet since the close. Park opens the cargo bay, checks the container seal, closes it again. She does this three times before she stops. Nobody mentions it.

visible_text: Brooks says, to no one in particular: 'He wasn't trying to hurt anyone.' Park does not answer. Ross comes back from the cordon and sits on the bumper next to Brooks. He says: 'Did Reiss know what he bought?' Brooks looks at her wrapped arm. 'He knew what it promised.' Ross nods. He does not ask the follow-up question. Owen is inside the vehicle, writing. She has been writing for forty minutes. She has not come out. Saye's channel has been quiet since the close. Park opens the cargo bay, checks the container seal, closes it again. She does this three times before she stops. Nobody mentions it.
outcomes: If you ask Brooks: she tells you, flatly, that it felt like pushing against something that had already decided. She does not elaborate. She goes back to watching Ross.

If you ask Park: she shows you her tablet. The second signature is there—same thermal profile, different location, timestamp from six days ago. She does not know where it is. She filed it in her personal notes. She has not told Saye yet. She is telling you now.

If you let it go: Owen comes out of the vehicle with her report. She hands it to Torah without comment. The resolution section has one line: 'Anomaly closed. Remainder unresolved. Manufacturer identity filed for follow-up.' She goes back inside.
followup_hooks: The second heat signature in Park's personal notes: a second Covenant Lock unit, sold to an unknown buyer, active six days ago at an unidentified location
The department head who denied the wig remains request: Saye's full disclosure report will reach them; their response is not yet known
Reiss's recovery: he is in Institute medical and has been told something, but not everything; what he does with what he knows is open
The Separation Athletics manufacturer: the Institute has the name; what the Institute does with it, and how fast, is not Torah's decision

babel_resolution_scatter_authority.speaker: Torah
babel_resolution_scatter_authority.line_1: The command floor terminal is down. Saye documents the syntax loop while civilians evacuate past walls that have stopped growing.
babel_resolution_scatter_authority.line_2: Torah files handwritten authority cards for each cell. The scatter must be documented before any responder uses the standard net.

buttons.label: Torah: scatter authority
buttons.preview: Torah writes and distributes authority cards by hand, speaks to each group leader in plain non-approved...

buttons.label: Saye: fragment the signal
buttons.preview: Saye uses the Institute's hardline access to push fragmented channel noise across the hub's dispatch frequency...

operation_plans.label: Torah: scatter authority
operation_plans.tactical_step: symbolic close: distribute handwritten local authority cards to each civilian group leader and squad member, verbally confirm in non-syntax language, then break contact
operation_plans.intent: Torah writes and distributes authority cards by hand, speaks to each group leader in plain non-approved language, and confirms that no single phrase routes through the hub anymore
operation_plans.yield: The syntax residue dissipates; Saye confirms the loop signature is gone; the building is inert; civilians evacuate under their own local authority; the Institute's unification protocol is formally broken at this site
operation_plans.risk: If Torah uses any phrase that matches the approved syntax—even accidentally—a final wall rises and the residue locks in; if a civilian group leader panics and keys a radio, the scatter fails

lines: Torah writes the cards at the dispatch counter, pressing hard on the notepad. He hands the first one to the civilian group leader on the second floor through the gap Ross made, and says, in plain words with no Institute cadence: 'You are in charge of your group. No one else.' The man looks at the card. He nods. Torah does the same for the platform group. Saye's voice on the hardline, after a long pause: 'The signature is gone. I'm not reading the loop anymore.' Torah puts the last card in his own pocket. Owen is already at the door.

Torah distributes the cards but one civilian group leader—a transit worker who has been listening to the terminal loop for two hours—repeats a fragment of the approved phrase back to Torah when confirming his instructions. It is not a full transmission. It is not routed through the hub. But a thin line of mortar appears at the base of the lobby door, and Saye says, carefully: 'It's not gone. It's quiet. Those are different things.' The building is passable. The residue is not resolved. Torah pockets his card and says nothing.

Torah is halfway through the second card when a first responder outside, unaware of the protocol, transmits a full approved-syntax check-in on the Institute frequency. The lobby wall rises in four seconds—floor to ceiling, solid brick, cutting Torah off from Owen and the stairwell. Torah is on the platform side with one civilian group. Owen and Ross are in the lobby. Saye is saying something on the hardline but the signal is breaking up. Torah looks at the wall. He looks at the card in his hand. He writes on the back of it: find another way out. He slides it under the wall.

operation_plans.label: Saye: fragment the signal
operation_plans.primary_skill: remote technical analysis and syntax disruption
operation_plans.tactical_step: suppress the residual syntax signature from the hardline by broadcasting fragmented non-routable channel noise across the hub's frequency range, then confirm scatter
operation_plans.intent: Saye uses the Institute's hardline access to push fragmented channel noise across the hub's dispatch frequency, breaking the syntax residue into non-routable pieces before any outside responder can trigger it
operation_plans.yield: The frequency is fragmented; no approved-syntax phrase can complete a circuit through the hub; the building is permanently inert at this site; Torah's card distribution is confirmed and backed by technical closure
operation_plans.risk: If Saye's fragmentation is too broad, it kills all Institute radio in the district for an unknown period, which may strand other squads; if it is too narrow, the residue finds a clean channel and the loop restarts

lines: Saye does not ask for permission. Torah hears her on the hardline say, 'I'm going wide,' and then the terminal housing—already dark—emits one short burst of static and goes silent in a different way than before. Not off. Empty. Ross looks up from the floor where he has been sitting with his arm against his chest. He says, 'What did she do?' Torah says, 'She scattered it.' Saye's voice returns: 'You have a clean exit. Move now. I'll explain the frequency gap to command in the morning.' She does not sound like she is looking forward to that conversation.

Saye fragments the signal but the channel noise is uneven—she got three of the four dispatch frequencies and missed the emergency override band. The building is mostly inert. Mostly. Owen finds a hairline crack in the lobby wall that was not there before she finished, and points at it without speaking. The override band is still live. Any full approved-syntax transmission on that frequency will restart the residue. Saye says, from the hardline: 'I need someone to physically cut the override antenna on the roof before you leave. It's a gray box. You'll know it.' She pauses. 'I'm sorry. I should have caught all four.'

Saye's fragmentation attempt triggers a feedback loop in the hub's dispatch system. The terminal housing is dark but the frequency is now broadcasting the approved syntax on all four channels simultaneously, louder than before. The walls that had stopped rising begin again. Owen is at the door. Ross is on his feet. Torah is looking at the ceiling. Saye's voice on the hardline is steady but Torah can hear her typing fast: 'I made it worse. Get out. Get everyone out now. I will find another way to close this but not from here and not tonight.' Torah does not argue. He signals Owen: evacuate.

Torah writes the cards at the dispatch counter, pressing hard on the notepad. He hands the first one to the civilian group leader on the second floor through the gap Ross made, and says, in plain words with no Institute cadence: 'You are in charge of your group. No one else.' The man looks at the card. He nods. Torah does the same for the platform group. Saye's voice on the hardline, after a long pause: 'The signature is gone. I'm not reading the loop anymore.' Torah puts the last card in his own pocket. Owen is already at the door.

Saye does not ask for permission. Torah hears her on the hardline say, 'I'm going wide,' and then the terminal housing—already dark—emits one short burst of static and goes silent in a different way than before. Not off. Empty. Ross looks up from the floor where he has been sitting with his arm against his chest. He says, 'What did she do?' Torah says, 'She scattered it.' Saye's voice returns: 'You have a clean exit. Move now. I'll explain the frequency gap to command in the morning.' She does not sound like she is looking forward to that conversation.

babel_cooldown_paper_authority.speaker: agent_caleb_ross
babel_cooldown_paper_authority.line_1: Ross is at the debrief table with his left arm in a field wrap, the cracked tablet in front of him, and a handwritten authority card he did not give away sitting next to his coffee.
babel_cooldown_paper_authority.line_2: Owen has not spoken since the building. Saye is on the line from the annex, waiting to be asked what she knew about the protocol before tonight.

lines: Ross turns the authority card over in his good hand. On the back, in Torah's handwriting: find another way out. He does not know when Torah wrote that or how it ended up in his pocket.
Owen is writing her after-action by hand. She has not asked for a form. She found paper in her kit and is using that.

Saye says, from the hardline: 'The protocol was flagged eighteen months ago. Someone at command decided the flag was a calibration error.' She stops. 'I read the flag. I filed a note. I did not escalate it.' Nobody answers her for a long time.
Ross finally says: 'How many other buildings have the platform installed?' Saye tells him. He puts the card down and does not pick it up again.

visible_text: Ross turns the authority card over in his good hand. On the back, in Torah's handwriting: find another way out. He does not know when Torah wrote that or how it ended up in his pocket.
Owen is writing her after-action by hand. She has not asked for a form. She found paper in her kit and is using that.

outcomes: If Torah asks Saye what she will tell command, she says the truth, and her relationship with the Institute's oversight division becomes a live thread
If Owen's silence is left alone, she returns to field duty but writes everything by hand from now on and does not explain why
If Ross documents the flag, it becomes evidence in a follow-up inquiry that command cannot easily suppress
followup_hooks: The Institute's dispatch platform is installed in eleven other transit and emergency facilities; each one is a potential Babel site
The overnight operator who transmitted the six evacuation orders is still unreachable; the wall on the second floor has not been breached
Saye's unfiled escalation is now on record; command will want to know who else saw the flag
The override antenna on the roof may still be live if Saye's partial fragmentation was the resolution outcome

resolution_aldermoor_naming.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_naming.line_1: The log is in Room 14. Iyad documents the ink discrepancy over comms. Torah stands at the east wall above the outline.
resolution_aldermoor_naming.line_2: The floor still moves. Torah documents the name test at the corridor edge before the seepage reaches the hall.

operation_plans.label: Torah names the keeper
operation_plans.primary_skill: credible witness, command authority, symbolic close
operation_plans.tactical_step: symbolic close—speak the keeper's name and the specific act of denial aloud inside Room 14 with the falsified document present, completing the ground's testimony
operation_plans.intent: Torah reads Iyad's timestamp finding aloud, names Voss, names the act—struck the resident, left him, filed a false clearance—and states it as fact in the room where it was denied
operation_plans.yield: The seepage stops. The threshold releases. The outline fixes in place as evidence. The manifestation closes locally.
operation_plans.risk: If Torah's statement is incomplete—missing the specific act, or naming the wrong element of the falsification—the floor does not stop and the seepage accelerates into the corridor; the window closes and the team must evacuate

lines: Torah reads from Iyad's notes without inflection. Voss's name. The time gap. The ballpoint line. What was done to the resident and what was filed instead. The room is very quiet while Torah speaks. The fluid stops moving before the last sentence is finished. It does not recede—it fixes, dries at the edges, holds the outline exactly as it was. Ross photographs it without being asked. Brooks stands at the door and does not move until Torah says they are done.

Torah gets through most of it but the specific act—what Voss did before he filed the report—is not in Iyad's notes, only implied. The floor slows but does not stop. The outline holds but the seepage continues at the edges, thin and patient. Iyad's voice comes over comms: 'You need the action, not just the time. What he did, not when.' Torah has one more attempt before the window closes.

Torah speaks but the statement is built on Iyad's preliminary read, and one timestamp is wrong—the gap is forty-one minutes, not thirty-eight, and the floor knows the difference. The seepage surges outward, fast, and the threshold slams shut. Brooks is inside. Ross is outside. Torah is in the corridor with the door between them and the outline spreading toward the walls. Iyad is already pulling the original nurse call log. 'Get Brooks out,' she says. 'I need four minutes.'

operation_plans.label: Ross forces Voss to the room
operation_plans.tactical_step: force option—physically move Voss to the threshold of Room 14 and compel him to hear the naming; this is the violent resolution option and carries escalation risk
operation_plans.intent: Ross retrieves Voss from wherever he has been moved, brings him to the threshold under physical control, and forces him to stand at the door while Torah names the act—the keeper's proximity to the testimony is the force option
operation_plans.yield: Voss at the threshold amplifies the naming; the floor responds faster and the closure is more complete; Voss cannot deny what the room shows him
operation_plans.risk: If Voss resists and the struggle happens at the threshold, the door anomaly treats the physical conflict as a new act of violence at the same location and spikes—the door can injure both Ross and Voss; the manifestation feeds on the violence and the seepage accelerates rather than stopping; Voss must be brought under control before he crosses the threshold or the option backfires

lines: Ross finds Voss in the stairwell, already trying to leave the building. He does not explain. He puts Voss against the wall, gets his arm, and walks him back down the corridor to Room 14. Voss sees the floor through the door panel and stops talking. Ross holds him at the threshold—not inside, just at the line—while Torah speaks. When the floor stops moving, Voss is looking at the outline and his face has gone the color of old concrete. Ross documents the custody transfer in his field log before he does anything else.

Ross gets Voss to the corridor but Voss pulls back at the threshold and the door slams between them—the anomaly reads the struggle as another act at the door and the frame shudders. Ross holds Voss against the opposite wall, away from the door, and calls to Torah: 'Do it now. He's here but I can't hold the threshold.' Torah speaks with the door between them and Voss. The floor slows. The outline holds. It is not a clean close—the seepage at the corridor edge does not fully stop—but the room releases Brooks.

Voss fights Ross at the threshold and the door hits them both. Ross takes it across the back and goes down. Voss runs. The floor inside Room 14 surges—the outline expands to the walls and the fluid reaches the corridor in a wide dark line. Brooks is inside, standing in it, and the drag sensation hits him hard enough that he grabs the windowsill. Torah calls the evacuation. Ross gets up slowly, one hand on the wall, and does not say anything for a long time.

Torah reads from Iyad's notes without inflection. Voss's name. The time gap. The ballpoint line. What was done to the resident and what was filed instead. The room is very quiet while Torah speaks. The fluid stops moving before the last sentence is finished. It does not recede—it fixes, dries at the edges, holds the outline exactly as it was. Ross photographs it without being asked. Brooks stands at the door and does not move until Torah says they are done.

Ross finds Voss in the stairwell, already trying to leave the building. He does not explain. He puts Voss against the wall, gets his arm, and walks him back down the corridor to Room 14. Voss sees the floor through the door panel and stops talking. Ross holds him at the threshold—not inside, just at the line—while Torah speaks. When the floor stops moving, Voss is looking at the outline and his face has gone the color of old concrete. Ross documents the custody transfer in his field log before he does anything else.

resolution_aldermoor_door_failed.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_door_failed.line_1: Brooks is in the corridor with his shoulder numb. Room 14 is not held. The duty log is still west with Ross, and the fluid is at the threshold.
resolution_aldermoor_door_failed.line_2: Iyad corrects the door timing over comms. Torah has one cycle to either reopen the room or make the naming through the glass before the outline reaches the hall.

buttons.label: Brooks takes the second cycle
buttons.preview: Brooks times the corrected pressure drop and holds the threshold for Ross.

buttons.label: Torah names through the glass
buttons.preview: Torah speaks the corrected naming through glass with the sealed log visible.

lines: Brooks waits for Iyad's corrected count. When the pressure drops, he hits the panel with his good shoulder and stops as soon as the latch gives. He does not enter deep this time. He plants a boot at the threshold and holds the door open with his body while Ross brings the bagged log forward. Torah names Voss from the corridor. The fluid reaches Brooks's boot and stops there.

Torah does not send Brooks into the door again. Ross reaches the corridor with the sealed log and holds the evidence bag flat against the reinforced glass. Torah reads Iyad's corrected finding aloud through the panel: Voss, the false clearance, the abandoned resident, the time gap. The room answers the document, not the doorway. The fluid slows, fixes, and dries at the threshold line.

resolution_aldermoor_evidence_delay.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_evidence_delay.line_1: Ross has the duty log at the threshold, but facility management followed him down the corridor and challenged the custody transfer.
resolution_aldermoor_evidence_delay.line_2: The delay cost two minutes. The seepage has crossed the threshold edge. Torah has to secure the chain before the room treats the document as another denial.

buttons.label: Torah asserts custody
buttons.preview: Torah records custody aloud and names the falsified clearance from the sealed log.

buttons.label: Ross holds the chain
buttons.preview: Ross keeps the sealed log clean while forcing management to witness custody.

lines: Torah records the custody transfer aloud before anyone touches the bag: Ross's name, the log number, the time, the fact that the seal is intact. The manager steps back when Torah names the threshold as an active hazard. Iyad supplies the corrected timestamp. Torah names Voss and the false clearance from the document. The seepage stops with one dark line already over the sill.

Ross does not argue with the manager. He turns the evidence bag so the seal faces the corridor camera and says the log number twice. Then he steps around the wet line, not over it, and puts the bag in Torah's hand. The naming happens with the document still sealed. The floor dries around the bag first, as if the room recognizes the chain before it recognizes the words.

resolution_aldermoor_voss_moved.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_voss_moved.line_1: Voss has been moved out of the manager's office and into the east-wing corridor. Ross has the log, but the floor between him and Room 14 has gone dark.
resolution_aldermoor_voss_moved.line_2: The room is pulling the suspect, the document, and the threshold into one place. Ross must stop Voss short of the door, or Torah must name the act before Voss reaches it.

buttons.label: Ross secures Voss short
buttons.preview: Ross detains Voss short of the threshold before the room can use him.

buttons.label: Torah names before contact
buttons.preview: Torah names Voss from Iyad's corrected record before contact at the door.

lines: Ross reaches Voss before the threshold does. He puts Voss against the corridor wall, away from the door, and keeps the log bag pinned under his own arm where Voss can see it. Torah speaks from the dry side of the line. Voss hears his name, the false clearance, and the abandoned resident before he can turn the struggle into another event at the door. The floor stops moving toward them.

Torah starts before Ross closes the distance. Iyad gives the corrected timestamp over comms, and Torah names the act while Voss is still in the corridor and the log is still sealed in Ross's hand. Voss stops walking. The dark fluid reaches the first tile in front of him, then dries there. Ross lowers his hand from Voss's shoulder without needing to push.

intercept.queued_line: Aldermoor closes under degraded conditions. The debrief has to preserve what went wrong, not smooth it into a clean sequence.

torah_speaks.queued_line: Aldermoor closes under degraded conditions. The debrief has to preserve what went wrong, not smooth it into a clean sequence.

cooldown_aldermoor_debrief.speaker: brooks
cooldown_aldermoor_debrief.line_1: Brooks is at the nurse's station with his jacket off. His right arm has a pressure bruise from the door frame, documented now, not before.
cooldown_aldermoor_debrief.line_2: Ross is still in the corridor outside Room 14 with the evidence bag. He has not filed the chain-of-custody form. Torah asked him twice.

lines: Brooks holds the ice pack against his arm and looks at the wall. He says: 'The door had a rhythm. I should have read it faster.'
Ross finally fills out the chain-of-custody form but he writes Voss's name in the wrong field first and has to cross it out. He does not explain why.
Iyad sends a memo at 0600: 'Confirm mission audio captured the naming statement in full. Required for evidentiary record.' She does not sign off on the mission log until Torah confirms.
The outline in Room 14 is dry now. Facilities has been told not to clean it. Nobody argues.

visible_text: Brooks holds the ice pack against his arm and looks at the wall. He says: 'The door had a rhythm. I should have read it faster.'
Ross finally fills out the chain-of-custody form but he writes Voss's name in the wrong field first and has to cross it out. He does not explain why.
Iyad sends a memo at 0600: 'Confirm mission audio captured the naming statement in full. Required for evidentiary record.' She does not sign off on the mission log until Torah confirms.
The outline in Room 14 is dry now. Facilities has been told not to clean it. Nobody argues.
outcomes: If Torah checks on Brooks: Brooks says the arm is fine. Then, after a pause, he says the door felt like it was waiting for him specifically. He does not elaborate. He puts his jacket back on.

If Torah leaves Ross alone: Ross files the form eventually and slides it under the evidence bag. When Torah picks it up later, the handwriting on the last line is different from the rest—smaller, pressed harder into the paper.

If Iyad's naming was the instrument of closure: she is not on comms for the debrief. She sends one message at 0700: 'Timestamp confirmed. Forty-one minutes. I had it wrong the first time.' Nothing after that until the next shift.
followup_hooks: Voss's custody transfer to local authority generates a formal inquiry; the Institute's involvement in the naming procedure will require explanation
The fluid outline in Room 14 is now a documented anomalous evidence artifact; Iyad requests a site return for material sampling
Brooks's door-timing error is in his own field notes but not in the official report; Ross knows and has not said anything
The facility has two other residents whose incident reports were filed by Voss; Iyad flags them in her 0600 memo without comment

resolution_offering_line_partial.speaker: SITREP
resolution_offering_line_partial.line_1: Owen has the offering line partially fixed, but she took the pressure wave and is holding her left side. Three civilians remain close to the line.
resolution_offering_line_partial.line_2: Park has a narrow lane to the core. Torah can either send Park through the unstable lane or sever the offering behavior before anyone else moves.

buttons.label: Park breaches under partial cover
buttons.preview: Park breaches through Owen's partial lane and seals fragments under pressure.

buttons.label: Torah breaks the offering first
buttons.preview: Torah uses Owen's pause to break the offering before Park touches the idol.

lines: Park moves on Owen's partial cover. A civilian starts forward and Owen stops him with one hand on her ribs, not crossing the line this time. Park gets the core housing open and seals the first fragment before the hum climbs again. Powder reaches one tile past the mat before she captures it. The broadcast stops, but Saye marks the drain as suspect.

Torah steps into the lane Owen is holding and names the command silence before Park breaches the idol. The contractor in the vest lowers his hands first. Three civilians follow. The offering line loosens enough for Park to move without Owen crossing it again. The core is still live, but the room is no longer feeding it bodies.

destroy.queued_line: The breach closes the room, but residue at the drain keeps Saye in the van with the emission log open.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The offering line breaks before the breach, and the debrief can start with the command-failure disclosure on record.

corpus_influences.source_moment: People surrender gold rings, craftsman fashions image, community assigns deliverance to it
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: People surrender gold rings, craftsman fashions image, community assigns deliverance to it

civilians actively walking toward the offering line during the squad's entry
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: If the offering behavior is not severed before breach, the idol's assignment transfers to the fragments

corpus_influences.source_moment: Calf burned, ground to powder, scattered on water; noise mistaken for safety
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: Calf burned, ground to powder, scattered on water; noise mistaken for safety
corpus_influences.room_application: The idol's broadcast is mistaken for safety by worshippers; destruction is viable but must be procedural—isolate, breach, reduce, seal powder before it reaches water or ventilation
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: The idol's broadcast is mistaken for safety by worshippers; destruction is viable but must be procedural—isolate, breach, reduce, seal powder before it reaches water or ventilation

corpus_influences.followup_payoff: Careless breach scatters powder into the floor drain; Saye flags a secondary emission from the building water system within forty-eight hours

resolution_offering_line_failed.speaker: SITREP
resolution_offering_line_failed.line_1: Owen is down at the east wall. The offering line is moving again. Park is still at the stairwell door with the containment kit unopened.
resolution_offering_line_failed.line_2: The core breach window is gone. Torah can pull Owen and force evacuation, or Saye can try to kill the broadcast from a bad baseline while Park recovers the kit.

buttons.label: Torah orders forced evacuation
buttons.preview: Torah pulls Owen clear and orders civilians moved off the line by force.

buttons.label: Saye calls an emergency cut
buttons.preview: Saye gives Park an emergency emission cut from the van even though the baseline is contaminated.

lines: Torah calls the clean breach off. Park leaves the kit shut and moves to Owen while the fire team drags civilians back from the line one at a time. The idol keeps humming. Nobody calls it containment. By the time the room is clear, the offering pile has taken three more badges and one child's bracelet.

Saye gives Park the emergency cut with a warning attached: the baseline is dirty. Park opens the kit on the floor instead of at the core and throws a containment field over the idol from too far away. The hum buckles but does not stop cleanly. Two containment units crack from the feedback. The room becomes quiet enough to evacuate, not safe enough to clear.

intercept.queued_line: The room is evacuated under force with the idol not cleanly closed.

torah_speaks.queued_line: The emergency cut quiets the room, but Saye reads the signal moving somewhere else.

resolution_offering_window_clipped.speaker: SITREP
resolution_offering_window_clipped.line_1: Saye lost the timing call halfway through. Park has the first containment unit sealed, but the surge is already returning around the core.
resolution_offering_window_clipped.line_2: The squad can finish the breach under a clipped window, or Torah can stop the movement long enough to reset civilian distance from the offering line.

buttons.label: Park finishes the seal
buttons.preview: Park stays through the returning surge and finishes enough seal to stop spread.

buttons.label: Torah resets the line
buttons.preview: Torah interrupts the breach and gets civilians back from the line before Park continues.

lines: Park stays at the core as the surge returns. The second containment unit chatters against the floor, then locks. She gets the original scrap sealed separately but leaves gold dust in the drain lip. Saye reads the room dropping, not cleanly, not to zero. Park says she can live with not-zero until everyone is out.

Torah stops Park before the second cut and turns to the civilians instead. He names the six-day command failure while Park keeps both hands on the containment field. The line backs up in uneven steps. When Park resumes, the core is still active but no one is moving toward it. The seal finishes late and holds.

destroy.queued_line: The clipped window leaves residue near the drain and keeps the emission log open.

full_disclosure.queued_line: Torah resets the line before Park finishes the seal, and the property inventory can proceed.

resolution_offering_false_window.speaker: SITREP
resolution_offering_false_window.line_1: Saye called the window four seconds early. Park is on the floor near the core. Three containment units are cracked open.
resolution_offering_false_window.line_2: The surge has already hit. The decision is no longer clean closure; it is whether to recover the kit under pressure or evacuate before the offering pile claims more property.

buttons.label: Park recovers the cracked kit
buttons.preview: Park recovers the cracked units and seals what she can before transfer.

buttons.label: Torah pulls the room back
buttons.preview: Torah abandons the breach and admits command failure while pulling civilians back.

lines: Park crawls back to the kit and closes two cracked units with her forearm across the lids. The third is gone into the drain grate as powder. The core goes quiet in the room, but Saye is already reading a faint echo from the water system. Park looks at the grate and says she knows.

Torah calls Park off the core and turns the failed timing into the disclosure: command was late, command was wrong, and the squad will not take one more thing from this room to hide it. It works only enough to move people. The idol keeps humming behind them as the evacuation becomes forced. Vasquez keeps her welfare report in her hand the entire way out.

destroy.queued_line: The room quiets, but the signal is now in the building water system.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The civilians leave under pressure while the idol remains an active containment problem.

resolution_offering_floor.speaker: SITREP
resolution_offering_floor.line_1: Park reports the core breach window open. The floor gauge logs the idol stripping small metal from squad gear.
resolution_offering_floor.line_2: Fragments must be sealed before the floor drain or ventilation pulls powder. Disclosure to civilians must happen before they are evacuated.

buttons.label: Park: Breach and Seal
buttons.preview: Park breaches the idol core in a controlled sequence—cut housing, apply thermal charge at low yield...

buttons.label: Torah: Break the Offering
buttons.preview: Torah uses his presence as the ranking Institute authority to sever the offering behavior—not by force...

operation_plans.label: Park: Breach and Seal
operation_plans.primary_skill: anomalous material breach and fragment containment
operation_plans.tactical_step: breach the core housing with cutting tool and thermal charge, reduce the fused metal, seal all fragments and powder in non-conductive containment units before any particle reaches the floor drain or ventilation, identify and separately seal the original anomalous scrap fragment
operation_plans.intent: Park breaches the idol core in a controlled sequence—cut housing, apply thermal charge at low yield, collect all fragments and powder into sealed non-conductive units, identify the original anomalous scrap by its emission signature, seal it separately
operation_plans.yield: Core is destroyed and fully contained; no powder reaches water or ventilation; the broadcast stops; the offering behavior has no object to return to; the room is clearable
operation_plans.risk: If Park rushes the breach or a containment unit fails, powder scatters toward the floor drain; Saye flags a secondary emission from the building water system; a follow-up containment mission is queued and squad contamination state rises

lines: Park does not announce the breach. She cuts the housing in two passes, applies the charge, and the core comes apart in a controlled collapse—gold-bright fragments dropping into the containment unit like coins into a box. The hum stops between one breath and the next. The civilians near the offering line look up, confused, the way people look when a sound they stopped noticing suddenly ends. Park seals the last unit, labels it, sets it aside, and picks up the anomalous scrap fragment with tongs. 'Got the original,' she says. 'Sealing separately.' Her hands are steady.

The breach goes clean but one containment unit's seal fails on the third fragment load—a hairline crack Park did not catch in the low light. Powder reaches the floor. Park drops to her knees and sweeps it manually into a backup unit, working fast, not looking up. She gets most of it. Saye, on comms, says the emission is dropping but not to zero. 'There's residue in the drain,' Saye says. 'Not enough to propagate on its own. Probably.' Park stands up. There is gold-colored dust on her gloves. She does not take them off.

The thermal charge yield is wrong—Park set it for the housing mass, not accounting for the anomalous scrap's density. The core does not collapse; it fractures outward. Fragments hit the floor, the wall, the drain grate. The hum spikes for three seconds and then drops to silence, but the silence is wrong—too complete, like the broadcast moved rather than stopped. Park seals what she can reach. Saye's voice on comms is careful: 'I'm reading a secondary emission from the building water system. It's faint. It's there.' Park looks at the drain. 'I know,' she says.

operation_plans.label: Torah: Break the Offering
operation_plans.tactical_step: Torah addresses the civilians directly before evacuation, names the six-day command failure, acknowledges what was surrendered and why, documents all offered property for return or sealed custody, and files the command-failure disclosure report as part of the formal closure record
operation_plans.intent: Torah uses his presence as the ranking Institute authority to sever the offering behavior—not by force but by accountability; he names the failure that caused it, acknowledges the community's fear as legitimate, and formally documents the surrendered property so the offering loop has no object and no unacknowledged debt
operation_plans.yield: Offering behavior is formally severed; civilians cooperate with evacuation; surrendered property is documented; the command-failure disclosure is on record; the idol has no community to enforce its assignment; closure condition is fully satisfied
operation_plans.risk: If Torah's disclosure is incomplete or the civilians do not accept it, some of them refuse to leave the offering line; the evacuation stalls; Owen has to physically move people; the idol's pull intensifies on anyone still near the pile; Torah's symbolic authority in future missions is noted as contested

lines: Torah does not raise his voice. He stands at the midpoint of the room, badge still vibrating, and he says: 'Institute command did not answer you for six days. That failure is documented and it is mine to carry. What you built here, you built because we were absent. I am asking you to step back from the line.' He pauses. 'Your property will be logged and returned or sealed. Nothing you gave will be lost without record.' The man in the contractor vest—the one Owen had to block—looks at Torah for a long moment. Then he steps back. The others follow, not quickly, but they follow. Vasquez picks up her welfare report from the floor and hands it to Torah without speaking.

Torah gets through the disclosure but stumbles on the six-day figure—command's actual response log shows five days and eighteen hours, and a civilian near the back catches it. 'You said six days,' she says. 'The log says five.' Torah does not correct himself fast enough. Three civilians stay at the line. Owen has to move them physically, carefully, one at a time. The evacuation takes eleven minutes longer than planned. Torah files the disclosure report with the corrected figure. He does not look at Owen when he hands it to Park to courier.

Torah begins the disclosure and the idol's pull spikes—his badge tears free of his jacket and hits the offering pile with a sound like a struck bell. The civilians near the line go still, then several of them step forward again, reading it as a sign. Torah's voice does not break but it does not carry either. Owen, from across the room, says quietly on comms: 'Sir. You need to get your badge back or get off the floor.' Torah retrieves the badge. The disclosure is filed but three civilians will not leave voluntarily. The evacuation is forced. Vasquez does not hand Torah the welfare report. She folds it and puts it in her pocket.

Park does not announce the breach. She cuts the housing in two passes, applies the charge, and the core comes apart in a controlled collapse—gold-bright fragments dropping into the containment unit like coins into a box. The hum stops between one breath and the next. The civilians near the offering line look up, confused, the way people look when a sound they stopped noticing suddenly ends. Park seals the last unit, labels it, sets it aside, and picks up the anomalous scrap fragment with tongs. 'Got the original,' she says. 'Sealing separately.' Her hands are steady.

Torah does not raise his voice. He stands at the midpoint of the room, badge still vibrating, and he says: 'Institute command did not answer you for six days. That failure is documented and it is mine to carry. What you built here, you built because we were absent. I am asking you to step back from the line.' He pauses. 'Your property will be logged and returned or sealed. Nothing you gave will be lost without record.' The man in the contractor vest—the one Owen had to block—looks at Torah for a long moment. Then he steps back. The others follow, not quickly, but they follow. Vasquez picks up her welfare report from the floor and hands it to Torah without speaking.

destroy:success.queued_line: Park seals the core and Torah files the command-failure disclosure. The property inventory can begin cleanly.

destroy:partial.queued_line: Park contains most of the powder, but Saye still reads residue near the drain.

destroy:failure.queued_line: The core fractures outward. The broadcast leaves the room through the building water system.

full_disclosure:success.queued_line: The civilians step back, the disclosure is filed, and the property inventory can begin cleanly.

full_disclosure:partial.queued_line: The disclosure is corrected after a civilian catches the timing error, and the evacuation drags under visible strain.

full_disclosure:failure.queued_line: Torah recovers his badge, but the disclosure fails to carry. Three civilians leave only under force.

destroy.queued_line: If the core is breached clean and the offering behavior is severed, the broadcast stops; if fragments escape, Saye flags a secondary emission within forty-eight hours.

full_disclosure.queued_line: If the core is breached clean and the offering behavior is severed, the broadcast stops; if fragments escape, Saye flags a secondary emission within forty-eight hours.

cooldown_offering_floor.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
cooldown_offering_floor.line_1: Saye is in the staging van with the containment log open. Park is outside on the step. Owen has not come out yet.
cooldown_offering_floor.line_2: The command-failure disclosure is filed. The six-day log is attached. Nobody has said what happens to the officer who did not answer.

lines: Saye closes the emission log and opens the property inventory. Forty-three items. Rings, badges, a child's bracelet, two Institute-issue lanyards. She starts entering them one by one.

Park comes to the van door and leans against the frame. She still has the gold-colored dust on one glove. She has not taken it off. She does not say anything for a while. Then: 'It felt like it was waiting for me to give it something.'
Saye does not look up from the inventory. 'It was.' She enters another item. 'That's what it was built to do.'

Owen comes out last. She is holding her left side but not favoring it visibly. She looks at the van, at Park, at the street. She says: 'The disclosure names the blackout. It doesn't name who caused it.' She is not asking a question.
Saye saves the inventory file. 'No,' she says. 'It doesn't.'
Owen nods once and walks to the far end of the staging area. She does not come back to the van.

Saye saves the inventory file. 'No,' she says. 'It doesn't.'
Owen nods once and walks to the far end of the staging area. She does not come back to the van.
outcomes: If the supplemental report is filed: institute.accountability rises sharply; a command review is queued; Owen's loyalty to Institute command is noted as under pressure
If logged as systemic: the accountability record is incomplete; a follow-up mission involving the same command gap is more likely; Park's fixation on the dust on her glove does not resolve
If a command review is requested: the review is acknowledged but not scheduled; Saye notes the acknowledgment timestamp in the property inventory file as the last entry
followup_hooks: Secondary emission from building water system if Park's breach was partial
Command review triggered by Torah's disclosure filing
Vasquez's welfare report, if retained by her, surfaces in a later mission as evidence in an unrelated accountability case
The child's bracelet in the property inventory is not claimed; it remains in Institute custody

cooldown_offering_residue.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
cooldown_offering_residue.line_1: Saye keeps the emission log open after the room goes quiet. Park is outside the van with gold-colored dust still on one glove.
cooldown_offering_residue.line_2: The core is sealed, but the drain is flagged as suspect. The command-failure disclosure is attached after the containment note, not before it.

lines: Saye marks the room mechanically quiet and chemically dirty. Park does not remove the glove until Saye photographs it.
Owen asks whether the residue can propagate. Saye says probably not, then deletes probably from the field note and writes insufficient mass observed.
The property inventory begins late. Every item is logged, but the drain sample sits beside the inventory tray like another offering.

cooldown_offering_water_signal.line_1: The room is quiet, but Saye reads a faint secondary emission from the building water system.
cooldown_offering_water_signal.line_2: Park has sealed the fragments she could reach. Nobody says the word contained while the water-system trace is still climbing.

lines: Saye moves the water trace into a separate incident file before she starts the property inventory.
Park writes the drain grate number on her glove because she does not trust herself to remember it later.
Owen keeps civilians two blocks back until facilities shuts water to the building. The mission report calls the room cleared and the building not cleared.

cooldown_offering_contested.line_1: The civilians are out, but the disclosure record has a visible correction in it. Vasquez saw the timing error before Torah did.
cooldown_offering_contested.line_2: The command-failure report is filed with an amendment. Owen signs the evacuation as delayed but voluntary.

lines: Saye attaches both versions of the timing line to the evidence record.
Torah initials the correction himself. Vasquez watches him do it before she hands over the welfare report.
Owen files the evacuation delay without commentary, which is how Saye knows she is angry.

cooldown_offering_forced_evacuation.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
cooldown_offering_forced_evacuation.line_1: The civilians are out because the squad moved them, not because the offering behavior broke cleanly.
cooldown_offering_forced_evacuation.line_2: The command-failure disclosure is incomplete or rejected. Vasquez keeps her welfare report, and Owen will not call the evacuation voluntary.

lines: Saye opens the property inventory and leaves the first line blank because she does not know whether the items are evidence, surrendered property, or still part of the anomaly.
Owen reports three civilians removed by force and refuses to soften the phrase.
Park seals the visible fragments, but nobody in the van treats the silence as closure.

visible_text: Saye opens the property inventory and leaves the first line blank because she does not know whether the items are evidence, surrendered property, or still part of the anomaly.
Owen reports three civilians removed by force and refuses to soften the phrase.
Park seals the visible fragments, but nobody in the van treats the silence as closure.
outcomes: The mission record keeps the degraded close condition visible
Future rooms can read the residue, forced evacuation, or water-system hook
The squad carries the consequence into later availability and morale checks
followup_hooks: Forced evacuation damages civilian trust in future Institute scenes
Vasquez keeping the welfare report preserves an external accountability hook
The idol may remain symbolically active even if the room is quiet

resolution_censers_audit_gap.speaker: SITREP
resolution_censers_audit_gap.line_1: Ross has the terminals dark and most tokens sealed, but the third faction token is unlogged. The seams slowed without fully stabilizing.
resolution_censers_audit_gap.line_2: The revocation can be closed with a gap notation, or Ross can force evacuation and leave command to finish the record from outside the floor.

buttons.label: Torah closes with gap
buttons.preview: Torah anchors the flawed revocation entry himself, preserving the gap notation instead of hiding it.

buttons.label: Ross evacuates with flagged bag
buttons.preview: Ross seals the flagged bag and gets the squad out before drift widens.

lines: Torah reads the gap notation aloud before he enters his credential. Ross flinches at the word unlogged but does not correct it. The log closes with the missing token named as missing, not clean. The C-7 seam stops, but Saye keeps D-8 on screen because it is still moving one millimeter at a time.

Ross does not let Torah put his credential on the gap. He seals the bag, photographs the missing-token notation, and sends the incomplete record to command review from the north corridor. The floor holds long enough for evacuation. It does not forgive the missing token.

torah_speaks.queued_line: The record closes, but the command-chain flag remains attached to the gap notation.

seal.queued_line: The squad exits with an incomplete record and command review still pending.

corpus_influences.source_moment: Faction presents credentials as proof of right to command authority in a consecrated space
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: Faction presents credentials as proof of right to command authority in a consecrated space
corpus_influences.room_application: The faction's forged authorization tokens function as the censers: physical objects that embody the claim to authority and that the symbolic layer of the building reads as an offering it will not accept
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: The faction's forged authorization tokens function as the censers: physical objects that embody the claim to authority and that the symbolic layer of the building reads as an offering it will not accept
required_visible_details: Faction badges that have warped slightly from heat by the time the squad arrives
Command log timestamps that align exactly with the first seam movement
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: The tokens must be preserved as evidence rather than destroyed, mirroring the censers hammered into a memorial plate

corpus_influences.source_moment: Ground opens under the rebels; surviving evidence becomes a permanent warning
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: Ground opens under the rebels; surviving evidence becomes a permanent warning

The safety line clipped to the door frame on the north side
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: If Owen is injured or the crossing fails, the rally point procedure determines who is trapped and who can still reach the terminals
