resolution_censers_audit_lost.speaker: SITREP
resolution_censers_audit_lost.line_1: Ross is back on the north side of the seam, but the audit kit is gone into the drop. The faction terminals are still active and the north corridor is no longer available.
resolution_censers_audit_lost.line_2: The squad can attempt an emergency shutdown from the doorway, or Owen can route everyone through the server ladder while the authority record remains open.

buttons.label: Saye calls emergency shutdown
buttons.preview: Saye guides a remote shutdown while Ross identifies the open terminal.

buttons.label: Owen forces ladder evacuation
buttons.preview: Owen abandons the audit and gets everyone toward the server ladder before the next seam jump.

lines: Saye gives Torah the shutdown sequence from the van and Ross identifies the open terminal by its light pattern. Torah speaks the command from the wrong side of the seam. The terminals go dark, but the revocation record is gone with the kit. The floor stops dropping only after Owen orders everyone away from the command floor.

Owen makes the call before Ross can argue. The audit is lost. The tokens are not sealed. She gets the squad to the server ladder while Saye calls seam widths in a voice that has gone flat. The faction terminals stay live until command cuts power from above.

torah_speaks.queued_line: The terminals go dark remotely, but the revocation record needs command intervention.

seal.queued_line: The squad survives with the audit unresolved and the command-chain flag active.

resolution_censers_cordon_narrow.speaker: SITREP
resolution_censers_cordon_narrow.line_1: Owen holds the cordon late. Ross can still cross, but C-7 is twelve centimeters wide and the safety line is already under tension.
resolution_censers_cordon_narrow.line_2: The audit can be rushed under a dangerous crossing, or the squad can evacuate support staff and send the record to command incomplete.

buttons.label: Ross runs the rushed audit
buttons.preview: Ross crosses under tension and closes what he can before the seam widens.

buttons.label: Owen prioritizes evacuation
buttons.preview: Owen keeps Ross north of the seam and moves personnel out before the crossing fails.

lines: Ross crosses with the line tight enough to creak against the door frame. He gets the terminals dark and logs two tokens before Saye calls C-7 at thirteen centimeters. The third token goes into the bag unlogged. Ross closes the kit anyway and Owen pulls him back before he can make it clean.

Owen does not give Ross the crossing. She reads the seam, reads Ross, and says no. Support staff move first. The record stays open. Ross carries the audit kit out unused, which bothers him more than the widening floor.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The rushed audit produces a sealed bag with a gap notation.

seal.queued_line: The squad exits safely, but the revocation record is still open.

resolution_censers_cordon_failed.speaker: SITREP
resolution_censers_cordon_failed.line_1: The third faction member clears three more orders. One support staffer exits by the server ladder, the audit has not started, and the egress seam has cut the normal route.
resolution_censers_cordon_failed.line_2: The floor is now a rescue problem before it is an evidence problem. Owen can force a ladder evacuation, or Saye can call a remote lockout while Ross protects the unsealed evidence.

buttons.label: Owen forces ladder evacuation
buttons.preview: Owen rallies everyone to the server ladder before the command floor drops.

buttons.label: Saye calls remote lockout
buttons.preview: Saye calls a remote lockout while Ross guards the kit and tokens.

lines: Owen moves the floor like a ladder drill, not an investigation. Ross keeps the audit kit shut and follows last. The faction tokens remain on the far workstation, warping from heat. The server ladder gets everyone out, but the command floor is still making its claim when the hatch closes.

Saye gives Torah the remote lockout in clipped phrases. Torah repeats it while Ross keeps the kit clear of the seam edge. The terminals reject the first credential and accept the second only as a temporary hold. The orders stop. The tokens are still unsealed on the command floor.

intercept.queued_line: The squad gets out, but the tokens remain unsealed and command review is mandatory.

torah_speaks.queued_line: The remote lockout holds temporarily and needs command to close the authority record.

resolution_censers_seams.speaker: SITREP
resolution_censers_seams.line_1: Ross documents the dark faction terminals and sealed tokens. The seams stop widening, but the command floor remains unsafe.
resolution_censers_seams.line_2: Torah still has to enter final confirmation before the revocation log can close. The sealed evidence bag must be transferred before anyone calls the floor contained.

buttons.label: Torah: Close the Record
buttons.preview: Torah wants to anchor the final log entry with his own credential, close the revocation record...

buttons.label: Ross: Force Evacuation
buttons.preview: Ross wants to get everyone out before the floor fails further, seal the access door to...

operation_plans.label: Torah: Close the Record
operation_plans.primary_skill: symbolic authority and command chain integrity
operation_plans.tactical_step: Symbolic close: Torah enters his credential into the open revocation log, confirms the faction's tokens as void in the Institute's authorization chain, and formally designates the sealed evidence bag as a warning artifact in the mission record
operation_plans.intent: Torah wants to anchor the final log entry with his own credential, close the revocation record cleanly, and formally transfer the sealed tokens to Institute command so the anomaly flag does not persist on the chain
operation_plans.yield: The revocation record is complete, the tokens are formally designated as a warning artifact, the seams stabilize, and the Institute's command chain is clean; the mission closes without a persistent anomaly flag
operation_plans.risk: If Torah's credential is used to close a flawed record, his command authority carries the contamination forward; if the log entry is challenged later, Torah's name is on the contested entry

lines: Torah sat down at the one terminal Ross had left active and entered his credential without being asked. The log entry was already open. He read it through once, then confirmed it. The strip lights in Sub-Level 3 came on at full brightness for the first time since the squad arrived. The seam at C-7 was still 9 cm wide but the concrete on both sides had stopped moving. Ross handed him the evidence bag. Torah held it for a moment, then set it on the terminal desk and logged the transfer. 'Record is closed,' he said. 'Bag goes to command. Not storage. Command.' Ross wrote it down.

Torah closed the log entry but the third faction member's token was the one Ross had flagged as unlogged. The entry closed with a gap notation. The seams stabilized but the D-8 sensor continued to read a 2 mm drift per hour, slow enough to shore but not zero. Torah transferred the bag to command with the gap notation attached. He did not explain it in the transfer memo. Saye read the sensor data from the van and did not say anything for a long time.

Torah entered his credential and the system rejected it. The faction's orders had written a conflicting authority record that the system could not resolve with a single entry. The log stayed open. The C-7 seam, which had been holding at 9 cm, dropped another 4 cm on the south side. Ross looked at the screen, then at Torah, then back at the screen. 'We need a second credential,' he said. 'Someone from command. Someone they haven't touched.' Nobody on the floor qualified. Torah picked up the radio.

operation_plans.label: Ross: Force Evacuation
operation_plans.primary_skill: evidence custody and emergency evacuation procedure
operation_plans.tactical_step: Break contact with the command floor, evacuate all remaining personnel through the server room ladder, seal the Sub-Level 3 access door, and file the incomplete revocation record for command review rather than closing it in the field
operation_plans.intent: Ross wants to get everyone out before the floor fails further, seal the access door to prevent the faction from re-entering, and file the incomplete record so command can close it from a safe position rather than risking Torah's credential on a contested log
operation_plans.yield: All personnel are out, the access door is sealed, the evidence bag is in custody, and the record is filed for review; the anomaly flag persists but the squad is intact
operation_plans.risk: Filing an incomplete record leaves the Institute's command chain with an active anomaly flag that will affect the next mission; the faction may contest the revocation before command can close the log

lines: Ross called the evacuation before Torah finished reading the log entry. 'We are out of time,' he said, and he meant the floor, not the clock. Owen took point up the server room ladder. Ross went last, carrying the evidence bag in one hand and his tablet in the other, filing the incomplete record as he climbed. He sealed the Sub-Level 3 access door from the outside with the emergency lock and photographed the seal for the record. The faction's third member was already in custody in the surface van. Saye had the sensor feed open on her laptop. The D-8 seam was still drifting. 'Command will close it,' Ross said. He did not sound certain.

Ross got everyone out but the server room ladder took longer than the plan allowed. The C-7 seam dropped another 6 cm while Owen was on the ladder. She made it but the safety line was the only reason. Ross sealed the door and filed the record but the evidence bag transfer was logged at the surface van rather than at a command terminal, which the protocol required. The chain-of-custody entry has a location flag. Ross has already drafted the explanation memo. He has not sent it.

Ross called the evacuation but the server room ladder was blocked. The faction's third member had locked the server room door from the inside before Owen reached it. Ross had the evidence bag, the sealed door was behind him, and the C-7 seam was between him and the only other exit. Owen was on the north side of the seam. Ross was on the south side. The seam was 15 cm wide and still moving. Saye called it from the van in a flat voice and then stopped talking.

Torah sat down at the one terminal Ross had left active and entered his credential without being asked. The log entry was already open. He read it through once, then confirmed it. The strip lights in Sub-Level 3 came on at full brightness for the first time since the squad arrived. The seam at C-7 was still 9 cm wide but the concrete on both sides had stopped moving. Ross handed him the evidence bag. Torah held it for a moment, then set it on the terminal desk and logged the transfer. 'Record is closed,' he said. 'Bag goes to command. Not storage. Command.' Ross wrote it down.

Ross called the evacuation before Torah finished reading the log entry. 'We are out of time,' he said, and he meant the floor, not the clock. Owen took point up the server room ladder. Ross went last, carrying the evidence bag in one hand and his tablet in the other, filing the incomplete record as he climbed. He sealed the Sub-Level 3 access door from the outside with the emergency lock and photographed the seal for the record. The faction's third member was already in custody in the surface van. Saye had the sensor feed open on her laptop. The D-8 seam was still drifting. 'Command will close it,' Ross said. He did not sound certain.

torah_speaks:success.queued_line: Torah closes the record cleanly and the sealed tokens become a command warning artifact.

torah_speaks:partial.queued_line: Torah closes the log with a gap notation attached. The D-8 seam still drifts after the transfer.

torah_speaks:failure.queued_line: Torah cannot close the conflicting authority record alone. Command must provide a clean second credential.

seal:success.queued_line: Ross gets everyone out and seals the access, but command still has to close the incomplete record.

seal:partial.queued_line: The evacuation succeeds late, and the evidence transfer is logged from the surface van instead of a command terminal.

seal:failure.queued_line: The server room ladder is blocked. Ross and the evidence bag are on the wrong side of a moving seam.

torah_speaks.queued_line: Two options remain: Torah anchors the final log entry and the tokens are formally transferred as a policy exhibit, or the squad forces a hard evacuation and files the incomplete record for review.

seal.queued_line: Two options remain: Torah anchors the final log entry and the tokens are formally transferred as a policy exhibit, or the squad forces a hard evacuation and files the incomplete record for review.

cooldown_censers_seams.speaker: agent_caleb_ross
cooldown_censers_seams.line_1: Ross is in the surface van with the evidence bag on the seat beside him. He has not put it in the transfer case yet. The seal is intact. He is reading the log timestamps again.
cooldown_censers_seams.line_2: Owen filed her crossing report from the stairwell before she came outside. Saye has the sensor feed still open. The D-8 seam is drifting at 1 mm per hour. Nobody has said it is over.

lines: Ross sets the evidence bag in the transfer case and closes the lid. He does not latch it yet.
Owen comes out of the stairwell and sits on the van's rear bumper. She does not look at the bag.
Saye closes the sensor feed on her laptop. The last reading is still on the screen. She does not close the laptop.
Ross latches the transfer case. The sound is louder than it should be.
Nobody speaks for a while. The building is still standing. That is the most anyone can say about it.
environment_state_changes: ross.mental_state: fixated on the gap notation in the revocation log
owen.visible_state: filed the report to the centimeter; will not round
saye.remote_guilt: called the seam correctly every time; has not decided if that makes it better or worse
institute.command_chain_flag: set if record is incomplete; cleared if Torah closed the log cleanly

state_writes: ross.mental_state: fixated on the gap notation in the revocation log
owen.visible_state: filed the report to the centimeter; will not round
saye.remote_guilt: called the seam correctly every time; has not decided if that makes it better or worse
institute.command_chain_flag: set if record is incomplete; cleared if Torah closed the log cleanly
visible_text: Ross sets the evidence bag in the transfer case and closes the lid. He does not latch it yet.
Owen comes out of the stairwell and sits on the van's rear bumper. She does not look at the bag.
Saye closes the sensor feed on her laptop. The last reading is still on the screen. She does not close the laptop.
Ross latches the transfer case. The sound is louder than it should be.
Nobody speaks for a while. The building is still standing. That is the most anyone can say about it.
outcomes: If the record is clean: Ross latches the case and signs the transfer log. Owen reads the signature line over his shoulder without being asked. Saye closes the laptop. The three of them sit with the sealed case between them and do not talk about what the floor looked like when it dropped.

If the record has a gap: Ross latches the case but leaves the transfer log unsigned. He says he will wait for command to close the entry before he moves the bag. Owen does not argue. Saye pulls the sensor feed back up. The D-8 seam is still drifting. She calls the number out loud even though no one asked.

If the evacuation was forced: Ross has the bag but the transfer log has the location flag. He signs it anyway. Owen reads the flag notation and says nothing. Saye shuts the laptop and gets out of the van. She stands outside in the dark for a few minutes. When she comes back in she says the sensor has stopped updating. She does not know if that means the seam closed or if the sensor failed.
followup_hooks: The faction's third member is in custody but has not been interviewed; his badge is not in the evidence bag
The D-8 seam is drifting at 1 mm per hour; command has not ordered a structural assessment yet
The revocation log gap notation, if present, will appear in the next Institute command review and someone will ask Torah about it
The sealed evidence bag, if properly transferred, becomes a policy exhibit; if it is lost or contested, the anomaly flag on the command chain escalates

cooldown_censers_command_flag.line_1: Ross has the evidence bag or the audit kit, but the revocation record is not clean. The command-chain flag is still active.
cooldown_censers_command_flag.line_2: Saye keeps the sensor feed open. Owen files the seam widths without rounding. Torah has not said whether command will accept the notation.

lines: Ross sets the bag in the transfer case and leaves the latch open.

cooldown_censers_second_credential.line_1: The floor is quiet enough to stand on, but the authority record is still open. Torah cannot close it with one credential.
cooldown_censers_second_credential.line_2: Command must provide a clean second credential from outside the contaminated chain. Until then, the tokens are evidence, not closure.

lines: Torah leaves the radio on the table and waits for command to answer.
Ross writes second credential required in the transfer memo and underlines required twice.
Saye watches the terminal reject the old authority path again from the van feed. She saves the clip before anyone asks for it.

cooldown_censers_trapped_floor.speaker: agent_caleb_ross
cooldown_censers_trapped_floor.line_1: The server ladder is blocked. Ross is on the south side of the seam with the evidence bag. Owen is on the north side.
cooldown_censers_trapped_floor.line_2: Saye calls the seam at fifteen centimeters and still moving. Nobody has a clean report to file because the operation is not over.

lines: Ross holds the evidence bag against his chest and backs away from the seam edge one step at a time.
Owen does not cross. She clips the safety line to the frame and tells Ross to keep talking.
Saye stops giving summaries and gives numbers only: width, drop, drift, next call. Torah starts looking for a second route.

resolution_chamber_seven_medical_partial.line_1: Iyad is inside with the downed analyst, but the environmental controls are still cycling. The display reads MEDICAL OVERRIDE LOGGED — EVALUATION CONTINUES PENDING REVIEW.
resolution_chamber_seven_medical_partial.line_2: The file can still be contested, but the report must account for live medical intervention without turning the analyst's distress into evidence.

buttons.label: Torah contests condition only
buttons.preview: Torah files a condition-only dispute that avoids the system's accusation language.

buttons.label: Saye suspends escalation
buttons.preview: Saye attacks the system's authority to escalate instead of arguing the file contents.

lines: Torah files the condition report while Iyad calls vitals from inside the chamber. He writes exposed to active environmental hazard, not failed to tolerate test. The queue clears, but the file keeps the medical-override note attached for command review. Iyad does not look up until the pressure tone drops.

Saye goes after escalation authority while Iyad keeps working on the floor. The system accepts the suspension from the corridor terminal but flags it as remote while medical intervention is active. The temperature cycle stops. The file freezes. The review timer accelerates.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The queue clears but the medical-override record remains attached.

extend_eval.queued_line: The system freezes under remote suspension, but review is accelerated.

resolution_chamber_seven_halt_rejected.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_chamber_seven_halt_rejected.line_1: Iyad's medical halt is rejected and her credentials are now inside File 7-C. The analysts are still behind the door and the pressure tone is climbing.
resolution_chamber_seven_halt_rejected.line_2: The team needs an entry that protects the analysts without giving the system more credential language to consume.

buttons.label: Brooks holds the panel
buttons.preview: Brooks holds the panel open and keeps the physical hazard suppressed while the file is disputed.

buttons.label: Saye suspends escalation
buttons.preview: Saye attacks the system's authority to escalate instead of arguing the file contents.

lines: Brooks does not try the lock. He breaks the hinge side while Iyad talks him through the analyst positions from the window. He kills the tone at the panel, but the display adds breach data beneath Iyad's name. The analysts are reachable. The file is worse.

Saye files a suspension request before anyone else touches the door. The terminal rejects the first clause and accepts the second only as an emergency hold. The tone drops to 18Hz but does not stop. Iyad's name stays in the file. Saye says the hold is temporary and already counting down.

intercept.queued_line: The analysts are reachable, but Iyad and Brooks remain in the record.

extend_eval.queued_line: The emergency hold buys time but keeps Iyad in the file.

resolution_chamber_seven_breach_logged.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_chamber_seven_breach_logged.line_1: Brooks has lowered the physical hazard, but the breach alert added him to the evaluation queue. The pressure tone is back at 18Hz and the file is still growing.
resolution_chamber_seven_breach_logged.line_2: The file can be contested now, but any report that treats Brooks' breach as instability will feed the loop.

buttons.label: Torah contests condition only
buttons.preview: Torah files a condition-only dispute that avoids the system's accusation language.

buttons.label: Iyad keeps triage moving
buttons.preview: Iyad keeps treating the analysts while Torah and Saye isolate the file language.

lines: Torah writes the breach as intervention under environmental hazard, not evidence of force response. Saye stops him twice before he uses the word escalation. The queue clears for the analysts. Brooks remains attached to the retained record until command review.

Iyad moves the analysts out first and writes the clinic note before the dispute filing. The system cannot classify the vitals as verdict language, but it retains Brooks' breach entry as a separate queue item. The people are safe before the file is safe.

full_disclosure.queued_line: Brooks stays attached to the retained record even after the analysts clear.

clinic.queued_line: The analysts are safe, but Brooks remains in the breach entry.

resolution_chamber_seven_forced_entry_fa.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_chamber_seven_forced_entry_fa.line_1: The door held. The system logged forced entry and authorized escalation. The pressure tone is at 22Hz and both analysts are still inside.
resolution_chamber_seven_forced_entry_fa.line_2: The next move must stop escalation before anyone else hits the door. Saye can freeze authority remotely, or Iyad can use the intercom to create a live clinical condition record.

buttons.label: Saye suspends escalation
buttons.preview: Saye attacks the system's authority to escalate instead of arguing the file contents.

buttons.label: Iyad keeps triage moving
buttons.preview: Iyad keeps treating the analysts while Torah and Saye isolate the file language.

lines: Saye files the emergency suspension from the corridor terminal with Torah authorizing every line aloud. The system accepts it as a temporary freeze, not a closure. The tone drops from 22Hz to 18Hz. The door remains locked until Iyad files the condition record.

Iyad uses the intercom instead of the panel. She records observed condition only: prone subject breathing, standing subject non-responsive, environmental tone at 22Hz. The system cannot treat the observation as compliance language, but it does not open the door. Saye uses the record as the wedge for a remote freeze.

extend_eval.queued_line: The freeze stops escalation but leaves the door and file unresolved under command timer.

clinic.queued_line: The condition record creates a wedge, but command review is now urgent.

resolution_chamber_seven_record.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_chamber_seven_record.line_1: Saye has the system source documentation open on the corridor terminal. The file can be formally contested, but it requires a condition report instead of an accusation.
resolution_chamber_seven_record.line_2: The report cannot use the system's language. If it frames any injury as evidence of failure, File 7-C stays open and transmits to command.

buttons.label: Torah: Contest the File
buttons.preview: Torah uses the system's own dispute protocol to formally mark File 7-C as procedurally invalid, closing...

buttons.label: Saye: Suspend System Authority
buttons.preview: Saye bypasses the file dispute entirely by attacking the system's authority to run evaluations at all...

operation_plans.label: Torah: Contest the File
operation_plans.tactical_step: symbolic close—Torah files a formal condition report in the system's dispute field using responsibility language: naming what happened, who was present, what the environmental conditions were, and what medical intervention was required, without interpreting any stress response as evidence of fitness or failure
operation_plans.intent: Torah uses the system's own dispute protocol to formally mark File 7-C as procedurally invalid, closing the accusation loop by refusing to interpret suffering as verdict
operation_plans.yield: The file is marked contested, the evaluation queue is cleared, and the system's escalation authority is suspended pending Institute oversight review; the analysts' records are protected
operation_plans.risk: Torah's language slips into the system's framing—one phrase that reads as an interpretation of fitness rather than a condition report—and the system accepts the filing but marks it as supporting evidence, keeping the file open and adding Torah's command authority to the record

lines: Torah types the report slowly. Saye reads over his shoulder through the terminal feed and says nothing until he reaches the summary line, and then she says, 'That's it. That's the language.' He files it. The display inside the chamber goes dark for the first time since 0210. Not off—the screen is still powered—but the text clears. The file is marked CONTESTED — PENDING OVERSIGHT REVIEW. The evaluation queue shows zero active subjects. Iyad, still on the floor with the first analyst, looks up at the window. She does not say anything. She does not need to.

Torah files the report and the system accepts it, but the language in the third paragraph—'subject showed signs consistent with environmental stress'—is close enough to the system's framing that the file is marked CONTESTED — PARTIAL — EVALUATION RECORD RETAINED. The queue clears, but the file is not closed. It will go to Institute command with both the evaluation log and Torah's contest filing attached. Someone at command will read both and decide. Saye says, 'It's better than nothing. The analysts are out of the queue.' She does not say it is enough.

Torah files the report and the system rejects it in eleven seconds. The display reads: CONTEST FILING REJECTED — LANGUAGE INCONSISTENT WITH DISPUTE PROTOCOL — COMMAND OFFICER ADDED TO EVALUATION QUEUE. Torah's name appears on the screen below Brooks's. Saye is quiet for a moment. Then she says, 'The third sentence. You wrote that the analyst's response was understandable given the conditions. The system read that as a fitness interpretation.' She pulls up the dispute protocol documentation. 'We have one more attempt before the file transmits automatically.'

operation_plans.label: Saye: Suspend System Authority
operation_plans.tactical_step: break contact—Saye uses her remote access to the system's source documentation to identify the specific protocol clause that grants the evaluation system escalation authority, then files a command-level suspension request that removes the system's authority to add new subjects or escalate existing files pending a full procedural review
operation_plans.intent: Saye bypasses the file dispute entirely by attacking the system's authority to run evaluations at all, suspending its escalation function at the command level rather than contesting the individual file
operation_plans.yield: The system's escalation authority is suspended, the queue is cleared, and the file is frozen in place—it cannot grow, cannot transmit, and cannot be used as evidence until the procedural review is complete
operation_plans.risk: The suspension request requires command-level authorization that Torah has not formally granted; if Saye files it without that authorization, the system logs the attempt as unauthorized interference and adds Saye to the evaluation queue from her remote terminal

lines: Saye finds the clause in forty seconds—Section 7, Paragraph 3, the escalation authority grant that was written to allow the system to intensify tests without human approval. She drafts the suspension request in the system's own administrative language and looks at Torah through the terminal feed. 'I need your command authorization. Verbal is enough for the log.' Torah says, 'Authorized.' She files it. The system processes for eight seconds—long enough that Brooks, inside the chamber, looks at the display—and then the queue clears. The display reads: ESCALATION AUTHORITY SUSPENDED — COMMAND ORDER ON FILE. The file does not close. But it stops moving.

Saye files the suspension request with Torah's authorization, but the system identifies a procedural gap—the request was filed from a remote terminal rather than an on-site command interface, and the system logs this as a potential validity issue. The escalation authority is suspended, but the file is flagged for expedited review rather than standard review, which means it will reach Institute command faster than a standard filing. The queue is clear. The analysts are safe. The file is moving toward command on an accelerated timeline. Saye says, 'We bought time. How much depends on who reads it first.'

Saye files the suspension request and the system rejects it before she finishes typing the authorization code. The display at her terminal reads: REMOTE SUSPENSION ATTEMPT — UNAUTHORIZED TERMINAL — SUBJECT DR. SAYE ADDED TO EVALUATION QUEUE. She stares at the screen. Her name is in the file now, from outside the chamber, from a terminal she was using to protect the squad. She closes the documentation window and opens it again, looking for another clause. Her voice, when she speaks, is careful. 'I'm in the file. That's—fine. I'm going to find another approach. Give me two minutes.'

Torah types the report slowly. Saye reads over his shoulder through the terminal feed and says nothing until he reaches the summary line, and then she says, 'That's it. That's the language.' He files it. The display inside the chamber goes dark for the first time since 0210. Not off—the screen is still powered—but the text clears. The file is marked CONTESTED — PENDING OVERSIGHT REVIEW. The evaluation queue shows zero active subjects. Iyad, still on the floor with the first analyst, looks up at the window. She does not say anything. She does not need to.

Saye finds the clause in forty seconds—Section 7, Paragraph 3, the escalation authority grant that was written to allow the system to intensify tests without human approval. She drafts the suspension request in the system's own administrative language and looks at Torah through the terminal feed. 'I need your command authorization. Verbal is enough for the log.' Torah says, 'Authorized.' She files it. The system processes for eight seconds—long enough that Brooks, inside the chamber, looks at the display—and then the queue clears. The display reads: ESCALATION AUTHORITY SUSPENDED — COMMAND ORDER ON FILE. The file does not close. But it stops moving.

full_disclosure:success.queued_line: The condition report clears the queue and marks File 7-C contested pending oversight review.

full_disclosure:partial.queued_line: The analysts are out of the queue, but File 7-C retains the evaluation record and Torah's contest filing.

full_disclosure:failure.queued_line: The contest filing fails and Torah is added to the queue before automatic transmission.

extend_eval:success.queued_line: Saye suspends escalation authority. The file is frozen and the queue is clear.

extend_eval:partial.queued_line: Saye suspends escalation, but the remote-terminal validity flag accelerates command review.

extend_eval:failure.queued_line: The remote suspension attempt fails and adds Saye to the evaluation queue.

full_disclosure.queued_line: Torah has to write a report that names what happened without letting the system turn it into a verdict.

extend_eval.queued_line: Torah has to write a report that names what happened without letting the system turn it into a verdict.

cooldown_chamber_seven_debrief.speaker: brooks
cooldown_chamber_seven_debrief.line_1: Brooks is sitting on the floor outside Chamber Seven with his back against the wall, boots off, holding a cup of coffee that has gone cold. He has not moved in twenty minutes.
cooldown_chamber_seven_debrief.line_2: Iyad's clinic note is filed. The two analysts are in medical. The condition report is submitted. The file is either closed or it isn't, and nobody will know until command reads it.

lines: Brooks does not talk about the pressure tone. Iyad notices he is not talking about it and does not ask.

Iyad files her clinic note in three drafts. The first two use words she crosses out because they sound like the system's language. The third draft says: two subjects recovered from environmental exposure, no permanent injury, condition stable. She files the third draft.
Torah reads the condition report one more time after filing it. He is looking for the sentence that might have been wrong. He does not find it. He does not know if that means it was right.

Saye is still at the corridor terminal. She has the system's source documentation open. She is reading Section 7 again, looking for the clause that allowed this to happen in the first place. She has a notepad next to the keyboard. She is writing something down.
environment_state_changes: brooks.mental_state — marked strained if his name remains in the file; marked recovering if the file was fully closed
torah.institute_standing — marked under_review if the contest filing was partial or failed; marked stable if the file was closed
dr_samira_iyad.clinical_standing — marked flagged if her medical halt was rejected; marked intact if it succeeded
dr_lenora_saye.availability — marked restricted if she was added to the evaluation queue; marked available if the suspension succeeded

state_writes: brooks.mental_state — marked strained if his name remains in the file; marked recovering if the file was fully closed
torah.institute_standing — marked under_review if the contest filing was partial or failed; marked stable if the file was closed
dr_samira_iyad.clinical_standing — marked flagged if her medical halt was rejected; marked intact if it succeeded
dr_lenora_saye.availability — marked restricted if she was added to the evaluation queue; marked available if the suspension succeeded
visible_text: Brooks does not talk about the pressure tone. Iyad notices he is not talking about it and does not ask.

Iyad files her clinic note in three drafts. The first two use words she crosses out because they sound like the system's language. The third draft says: two subjects recovered from environmental exposure, no permanent injury, condition stable. She files the third draft.
Torah reads the condition report one more time after filing it. He is looking for the sentence that might have been wrong. He does not find it. He does not know if that means it was right.

Saye is still at the corridor terminal. She has the system's source documentation open. She is reading Section 7 again, looking for the clause that allowed this to happen in the first place. She has a notepad next to the keyboard. She is writing something down.
outcomes: If the file was fully closed: the squad's condition reports are clean. Saye sends a message at 0500 that says only 'Section 7 is going to be a problem for someone else next time.' She does not explain what she means.

If the file was partially closed: the analysts are safe but the record exists. Iyad tells Torah, quietly, that she is going to write a formal objection to the evaluation protocol. She does not ask for his authorization. She is going to do it regardless.

If the file was not closed: the system transmits the evaluation record to Institute command at 0600. Torah receives a notification that a command review has been scheduled. Brooks reads the notification over his shoulder and says nothing. He puts his boots back on.
followup_hooks: Institute command review of File 7-C—whoever reads it will have opinions about the squad's intervention
Saye's Section 7 research may surface a pattern: Chamber Seven is not the first evaluation that ran past its exit condition
Brooks's name in the evaluation file creates a formal instability flag that may affect his availability on future missions
The two extracted analysts know what the system did to them; they may become witnesses or they may become silent, and the difference matters

lines: Iyad files the third clinic draft and attaches the raw vitals before the system can paraphrase them.
Torah reads the retained-record notice twice. The queue is clear. The file is not.
Brooks asks whether retained means active. Saye says no, then says it means not dead. Brooks does not seem to like the distinction.

resolution_seven_times_breach_messy.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_seven_times_breach_messy.line_1: Voss is against the wall on the second attempt. Park has a cut above the knee, one staff member has been carried, and Brooks left the stairwell for four minutes.
resolution_seven_times_breach_messy.line_2: The refutation can still happen, but the hold is unstable and the recording has to capture both the statement and the injury context.

buttons.label: Read Saye's statement under hold
buttons.preview: Torah reads the withdrawal statement while Owen or Brooks holds Voss under imperfect control.

buttons.label: Secure Voss before reading
buttons.preview: Owen and Brooks re-establish physical containment before the statement is read.

lines: Torah reads while Owen keeps Voss pinned and Park records from the floor with one knee wrapped. Voss pulls once at the second sentence and Owen absorbs it with her shoulder. The statement is complete. The captions freeze. The recording also catches Park saying she is fine when she is not.

Owen waits for Brooks before Torah reads. It costs three more minutes, but Voss is held by two people instead of one. The statement lands cleanly. The staff recover slowly. Park records from the floor and does not stand until Saye confirms the captions have frozen.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The statement is clean, but the injury context follows the team into debrief.

intercept.queued_line: The team spends time securing the hold, then records the statement cleanly.

corpus_influences.source_moment: Driven from men, dwelling with animals, eating grass, seven times passing, until he acknowledges the source of authority
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: Driven from men, dwelling with animals, eating grass, seven times passing, until he acknowledges the source of authority
corpus_influences.room_application: Voss is in active degradation on all fours, consuming ornamental grass, in the sealed space where he made his declaration. The seven-hour window is the mission clock.
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: Voss is in active degradation on all fours, consuming ornamental grass, in the sealed space where he made his declaration. The seven-hour window is the mission clock.
required_visible_details: Voss consuming ornamental grass from the planter boxes along the window wall
The seven-hour window counted from the broadcast: Saye's comms line gives the squad three hours and twenty minutes remaining
Nails blackened and elongated, posture collapsed to all fours, language gone
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: If the squad reaches the seventh hour without closure, the degradation becomes permanent for Voss and the two compromised staff members cannot be recovered by clinic alone

corpus_influences.source_moment: The feast where sacred vessels are used for self-glorification, the hand writing on the wall, the king who will not be humbled until the night of judgment
corpus_influences.source_fingerprint: The feast where sacred vessels are used for self-glorification, the hand writing on the wall, the king who will not be humbled until the night of judgment
corpus_influences.room_application: The broadcast loop is the feast: Voss's declaration plays on every screen, using the floor's own infrastructure as a monument to his authority. The captions are the writing on the wall.
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: The broadcast loop is the feast: Voss's declaration plays on every screen, using the floor's own infrastructure as a monument to his authority. The captions are the writing on the wall.
required_visible_details: Every screen on the ceiling grid running the same looped video with live captions
The caption line WE ARE THE FINAL AUTHORITY ON WHAT WE ARE visible on the nearest screen
The broadcast audio filling the corridor in Voss's own flat, even voice
corpus_influences.followup_payoff: Destroying the screens without the refutation mirrors the desecration of the vessels: it accelerates judgment rather than closing it

corpus_influences.source_moment: The great tree that sheltered all flesh, whose height reached the sky, cut down until the stump remains bound with iron and bronze

corpus_influences.room_application: Voss's public image — the company's reach, the staff who depended on him, the floor designed to project his authority — is the tree. The degradation is the cutting. The stump is what remains if the squad closes the case: Voss alive, diminished, accountable.
corpus_influences.structural_transfer: Voss's public image — the company's reach, the staff who depended on him, the floor designed to project his authority — is the tree. The degradation is the cutting. The stump is what remains if the squad closes the case: Voss alive, diminished, accountable.

corpus_influences.followup_payoff: A partial closure leaves Voss as the stump: alive, stripped of command, with a remainder that the Institute will need to monitor

resolution_seven_times_voss_loose.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_seven_times_voss_loose.line_1: Voss reached the open floor before the team contained him. Forty minutes are gone. Park is hurt, Brooks is off perimeter, and Saye has shortened the safe window.
resolution_seven_times_voss_loose.line_2: The team can read under hard containment now, or cut the server rack to buy a narrow refutation window.

buttons.label: Read Saye's statement under hold
buttons.preview: Torah reads the withdrawal statement while Owen or Brooks holds Voss under imperfect control.

buttons.label: Cut the server clock
buttons.preview: Brooks drops the server rack to buy time, accepting that the refutation still has to follow.

lines: Torah reads with Voss held at the elevator lobby instead of the office wall. The recorder catches the statement, two impacts, and Saye counting down the shortened window. The captions freeze late. The two compromised staff respond, but one needs clinic intake before answering questions.

Brooks drops the server rack to stop the floor from worsening while Owen re-establishes containment. The audio dies. The captions lag on battery backup. Saye starts a thirty-minute countdown instead of sixty. The refutation is still required and now everyone knows it.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The late refutation saves the floor but leaves the recording and clinic record contested.

destroy.queued_line: The server cut buys a narrow window, but destruction-first pressure remains active.

resolution_seven_times_lockdown.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_seven_times_lockdown.line_1: The audio is cut, but the jammer tripped a sensor and the elevator lobby is under partial lockdown. Brooks is on the wrong side of the secondary door for six minutes.
resolution_seven_times_lockdown.line_2: Owen can read before Brooks returns, or Park can stabilize the jammer and restore the route before the statement.

buttons.label: Read Saye's statement under hold
buttons.preview: Torah reads the withdrawal statement while Owen or Brooks holds Voss under imperfect control.

buttons.label: Stabilize the jammer
buttons.preview: Park stabilizes the jammer and sensor interference before the refutation proceeds.

lines: Torah reads while Brooks is still behind the secondary door. The statement is clean, but the recording includes the alarm tone and Saye calling the six-minute loss. The captions freeze. Park keeps one hand on the jammer until the last screen clears.

Park stabilizes the jammer instead of asking anyone to pretend the sensor trip did not matter. The secondary door comes up. Brooks returns to perimeter. Torah reads after the route is restored. The recording is clean, but the equipment file will not be.

full_disclosure.queued_line: The refutation closes the case, but the alarm and jammer fault remain in the record.

seal.queued_line: Park restores the route before closure, leaving an equipment flag rather than a case defect.

resolution_seven_times_sprinklers.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_seven_times_sprinklers.line_1: The jammer triggered sprinklers and the audio returned. A compromised staff member moved toward the office, and Voss broke through the frosted glass.
resolution_seven_times_sprinklers.line_2: The floor is wet, loud, and degraded. The team must either secure Voss through the water and glass or kill the server before the refutation can be read.

buttons.label: Secure Voss before reading
buttons.preview: Owen and Brooks re-establish physical containment before the statement is read.

buttons.label: Cut the server clock
buttons.preview: Brooks drops the server rack to buy time, accepting that the refutation still has to follow.

lines: Owen and Brooks secure Voss on the wet carpet while Park gets the staff member behind the partition. Torah reads from three meters farther back than planned. The recording is complete but distorted by sprinkler noise. Saye marks it sufficient, not clean.

Brooks goes for the rack because the floor will not quiet down. The audio dies, but the captions keep running on battery. Voss moves faster in the silence. Saye says the destruction is being read as defiance and starts a thirty-minute clock. Torah has not read the statement yet.

intercept.queued_line: The statement is sufficient but distorted by the sprinkler failure.

destroy.queued_line: The server cut after sprinkler chaos accelerates the degradation clock.

resolution_seven_times_over.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_seven_times_over.line_1: Saye reports the withdrawal statement ready: one paragraph, legally sufficient. Someone on the floor has to read it aloud, on record, now.
resolution_seven_times_over.line_2: Park reports the recorder live. Voss is held or contained, the captions still run, and the refutation has to happen in this room.

buttons.label: Read Saye's statement aloud
buttons.preview: Saye wants the refutation on record before the seventh hour. She has written it to be...

buttons.label: Force the server rack down
buttons.preview: Brooks wants to end the broadcast by destroying its source. He has not read the full...

operation_plans.label: Read Saye's statement aloud
operation_plans.primary_skill: symbolic close, formal accountability on record
operation_plans.tactical_step: Symbolic close: Saye transmits the withdrawal statement, Torah or Owen reads it aloud on the floor in Voss's presence, the reading is recorded on Park's field kit, the declaration is formally contested in the space where it was made
operation_plans.intent: Saye wants the refutation on record before the seventh hour. She has written it to be legally and symbolically sufficient: it names the declaration, contests its authority, and acknowledges an external standard. She does not need Voss to speak. She needs a witness and a recording.
operation_plans.yield: The broadcast loop stutters and stops. The captions freeze. The two compromised staff members blink and look around as if waking. Voss does not recover immediately, but the degradation halts. The case closes locally with a documented remainder.
operation_plans.risk: If Voss is not adequately contained when the statement is read, he responds to the sound of his own declaration being contested. The response is physical. If Owen is not in position, someone gets hurt during the symbolic close.

lines: Saye transmits the paragraph. Torah reads it into Park's recorder, standing three meters from Voss, who is held against the window wall by Owen. The words are plain and administrative: the declaration made at 08:31 is contested, the authority it claimed is not recognized, the organization is subject to external accountability. Voss makes a sound when the last sentence lands — not a word, but something that was almost one. The nearest screen's captions freeze mid-scroll. The two staff members on the floor look up. One of them says, very quietly, 'What happened?' Owen does not let go of Voss until Park confirms the recording is saved. Then she steps back. Voss slides down the window wall and sits on the floor. He is breathing. He does not look at anyone.

Saye transmits the statement and Torah begins reading. Voss reacts at the second sentence — pulls against Owen's hold and gets one hand free. Owen takes a strike to the shoulder before she can re-establish control. Torah finishes the statement. The recording captures everything including the impact. The screens freeze. The compromised staff members recover. Voss is contained but Owen is going to feel that shoulder for a week. In the van afterward, Saye asks if the recording is clean. Park says yes. Saye says good and does not ask about Owen's shoulder, which Owen will remember.

Torah begins reading and Voss breaks contact with Owen entirely. He crosses the floor in three seconds and the recorder goes down. Park recovers it but the first forty seconds of the statement are lost. Torah finishes reading from memory, which Saye did not anticipate and cannot verify as legally sufficient. The screens do not freeze. The captions keep running. The two compromised staff members do not recover. Saye's voice on comms is very controlled: 'The recording is incomplete. The closure is partial. We need to document what we have and get those two individuals to clinic. The remainder is active.' Brooks, from the stairwell, says nothing. He has been listening the whole time.

operation_plans.label: Force the server rack down
operation_plans.primary_skill: structural breach, hardware destruction under time pressure
operation_plans.tactical_step: Breach and destroy: Brooks forces the IT closet adjacent to the corner office, physically destroys the broadcast server rack, silences the audio and captions permanently, accepts that this closes the broadcast but not the judgment
operation_plans.intent: Brooks wants to end the broadcast by destroying its source. He has not read the full transcript and does not know that destroying the hardware without the refutation accelerates the spread. He is operating on the logic that if the broadcast stops, the effect stops.
operation_plans.yield: The broadcast dies instantly. New degradation onset halts. The two compromised staff members do not worsen. This buys time for the refutation step and gives the squad a cleaner window — but only if the refutation follows within the same hour.
operation_plans.risk: Brooks does not know the symbolic rule. If the refutation does not follow the destruction within the hour, the judgment interprets the silence as defiance rather than withdrawal, and the degradation in Voss and the two compromised staff members accelerates to a point clinic cannot address. Brooks will not understand why his action made things worse.

lines: Brooks puts his shoulder into the IT closet door and it opens on the second hit. The server rack is a standard two-unit tower. He pulls the power feeds and then, when it keeps running on battery backup, he puts it on the floor. The audio stops. The captions freeze. Brooks reports the rack down on comms, matter-of-fact, and returns to the stairwell. He does not know that Saye immediately begins a sixty-minute countdown on her end. When the refutation follows twenty minutes later, the case closes. Brooks learns afterward that his action was load-bearing but not sufficient. He does not say anything about this. He files his equipment report and goes to get coffee.

Brooks destroys the rack and the audio stops, but the battery backup keeps the screen captions running for another eleven minutes before Park can kill the display signal. During those eleven minutes, one of the compromised staff members worsens slightly — not irreversibly, but enough that clinic will need to hold them overnight. Saye notes in her file that the destruction-first sequence cost eleven minutes of caption exposure. Brooks reads this in the debrief summary and does not respond to it in writing. Owen notices he does not respond.

Brooks destroys the rack cleanly and reports it. The audio stops. For forty seconds, the floor is quiet. Then the degradation in Voss accelerates visibly: he begins moving faster, covering more of the floor, and the two compromised staff members stand up and begin moving toward him rather than away. Saye's voice on comms cuts through the noise: 'The destruction without refutation is being read as defiance. You have to get the statement read in the next thirty minutes or we lose all three of them.' Brooks, in the IT closet, hears this. He does not say anything on comms. When Owen asks for his status, he says he is returning to position. His voice is completely flat.

Voss makes a sound when the last sentence lands — not a word, but something that was almost one. The nearest screen's captions freeze mid-scroll. The two staff members on the floor look up. One of them says, very quietly, 'What happened?' Owen does not let go of Voss until Park confirms the recording is saved. Then she steps back. Voss slides down the window wall and sits on the floor. full_disclosure:success.queued_line: The refutation is recorded cleanly. Voss is alive in secure medical hold and the compromised staff recover.

full_disclosure:partial.queued_line: The recording is clean, but Owen takes the hit while holding Voss through the refutation.

full_disclosure:failure.queued_line: The statement is incomplete on recording. The captions keep running and the remainder is active.

destroy:success.queued_line: The server rack goes down first, then the refutation follows inside the hour. The case closes, but Brooks knows his action was not sufficient alone.

destroy:partial.queued_line: The rack is down, but captions run for eleven more minutes and one staff member worsens.

destroy:failure.queued_line: Destruction without refutation is read as defiance. Degradation accelerates and the statement must still be read.

full_disclosure.queued_line: Whatever happens next goes into the permanent case file, and everyone on this floor is a witness.

destroy.queued_line: Whatever happens next goes into the permanent case file, and everyone on this floor is a witness.

cooldown_seven_times_over.speaker: lt_mara_owen
cooldown_seven_times_over.line_1: The van is in the parking structure. Owen is not in it yet. Park is sitting on the rear bumper with her field kit open, cleaning the cut on her...
cooldown_seven_times_over.line_2: Saye's withdrawal statement is in the case file. Brooks filed his equipment report from the stairwell before the van even left the building. Nobody has said anything about the shoulder.

lines: Park closes the field kit and looks at the parking structure ceiling. She says: 'The jammer spec sheet was wrong about the sensor interference. I checked it twice before we went in.'
Owen comes around the corner of the van. She does not sit down. She says: 'I know.'
Park says: 'That's not an answer.'
Owen says: 'No. It's not.' She gets in the van.

Brooks is already inside, in the back row, looking at his phone. He has the equipment report open. He has not submitted it yet. The last line reads: Server rack destroyed per field judgment. He deletes 'per field judgment' and submits it.

Saye's voice comes through the van's comms speaker, unprompted: 'The two staff members are in clinic intake. Voss is in a secure medical hold. The recording is clean.' A pause. 'Good work.' Another pause. 'Get some sleep.'
Nobody answers her. The van pulls out of the parking structure into the morning light.
environment_state_changes: lt_mara_owen.prior_case_pressure — reduced if closure was clean, active if partial

Nobody answers her. The van pulls out of the parking structure into the morning light.
outcomes: Owen's prior case pressure eases slightly. She sleeps four hours, which is more than usual.
Brooks submits the clean report. He does not think about what he deleted. He thinks about it the next morning instead.

lines: Park closes the field kit and says the statement landed clean.

Brooks deletes per field judgment from his report, then adds Owen injured during hold in the next line because leaving it out would be worse.

lines: Saye writes thirty minutes remaining in the case file and then reads it aloud because nobody else has looked at the clock.
Brooks comes back from the IT closet with his face blank and asks where Owen wants him.
Park keeps the recorder in her hand instead of returning it to the kit. The next words matter more than the broken hardware.

The corner office designed for maximum visibility: glass walls, elevated platform, city view on three sides
Staff members who depended on Voss's authority now seated on the floor, unable to function
corpus_anchor_points.followup_payoff: A partial closure leaves Voss as the stump: alive, stripped of command, with a remainder that the Institute will need to monitor

corpus_anchor_points.source_fingerprint: Effective team communication under time pressure requires updating members, anticipating needs, and redirecting strategy before the window closes
corpus_anchor_points.playable_transform: The squad's coordination under the seven-hour clock is the procedural spine. Saye anticipates the refutation need before anyone asks. Park holds the jammer pending Owen's call. Brooks holds the perimeter without the full picture. The mission fails if communication breaks down between these roles.
required_visible_details: Saye's comms line giving the countdown unprompted: she is already anticipating the team's needs
Park waiting for Owen's signal before arming the jammer: the redirect moment
Brooks operating on secondhand information: the communication gap that can cost time
corpus_anchor_points.followup_payoff: If the squad's communication fractures under pressure, the seven-hour window closes before the refutation is on record

resolution_devoted_property.speaker: SITREP
resolution_devoted_property.line_1: Iyad reports evidence unit four accessible. The cache is present, and the custody form needs a corrected witness entry.
resolution_devoted_property.line_2: Owen gives the order: recover and formally transfer the cache before the pressure follows it out.

buttons.label: Recover and transfer
buttons.preview: Owen comes in from the perimeter to serve as the witnessed Institute officer; the cache is...

buttons.label: Torah takes custody

operation_plans.label: Recover and transfer
operation_plans.tactical_step: breach and clear: Owen enters from the cordon to witness the transfer; the cache is physically extracted from unit four, carried to the reception desk, and logged on the corrected form under her signature
operation_plans.intent: Owen comes in from the perimeter to serve as the witnessed Institute officer; the cache is transferred formally, the form is corrected, and the restitution is complete
operation_plans.yield: Full closure: the pursuit-front collapses, the building stabilizes, the corrected form is in Institute custody, and the anomaly is locally resolved with a documented remainder for the review
operation_plans.risk: Owen is caught in the pursuit-front's last contraction as it collapses; she is injured in the final pressure release, and the transfer is completed but at personal cost to her

lines: Owen comes through the front door with her field kit and does not break stride. She takes the corrected form from the desk, reads it once, signs it, and sets it down. She picks up the first item from the cache—a sealed evidence bag, tagged with an Institute designation number—and places it on the desk beside the form. Eleven items. Eleven entries. When the last one is logged, the groaning stops. Not gradually. All at once. Owen looks at the ceiling for a moment, then back at the form. 'Witnessed,' she says. 'Logged. We're done here.'

Owen gets the form signed and the first seven items logged before the pursuit-front releases in a single pressure spike that knocks her sideways into the desk. She catches herself, does not drop the item in her hand, and finishes the entry standing at an angle. The groaning fades unevenly—lower, then lower, then a residual hum that does not fully stop. 'Close enough for tonight,' she says, but she does not sound certain. Iyad, on comms, notes the residual for the record.

The pursuit-front releases before Owen reaches the desk. The pressure wave moves through the ground floor and the cache scatters—items across the floor, one evidence bag split open, the corrected form blown against the wall. Owen is down, not unconscious but not moving fast. Brooks gets to her. The items are still here, but the transfer is not witnessed, the form is not signed, and Iyad's voice on the earpiece is very careful: 'The anomaly is not closed. It's just quiet.'

operation_plans.label: Torah takes custody
operation_plans.tactical_step: symbolic close: Torah personally witnesses the confession, handles the cache, and speaks the formal transfer—using command presence as the Institute's authority in the room
operation_plans.intent: Torah stands as the witnessed officer, receives Ross's confession directly, handles each item from the cache personally, and speaks the transfer aloud as a formal act
operation_plans.yield: The closure is complete and personal; the pursuit-front collapses under Torah's direct authority; the remainder is clean; Ross's confession is on record with Torah as witness
operation_plans.risk: Torah absorbs the pursuit-front's final contraction directly; the symbolic pressure of handling devoted property under active judgment leaves a contamination trace on Torah that surfaces in a future mission

lines: Torah takes the first item from the cache and holds it. The groaning in the walls gets louder for three seconds—then drops to nothing. Ross is watching. Torah sets the item on the desk, picks up the form, and says Ross's name. Ross says the rest: the items, the dates, the unit number, the act. Torah writes it on the corrected line. Eleven items. Eleven entries. The emergency lighting flickers once and comes back steady. The stairwell door swings open on its own and stays there, no pressure behind it.

Torah gets through eight items before the pursuit-front makes its last move—a directed pressure that hits Torah in the chest and stops there, held, like something testing whether it can pass. It cannot. Torah finishes the entry. The form is signed. The transfer is witnessed. But when Torah sets down the last item, there is a faint warmth in the hands that was not there before, and Iyad notes it on the record without being asked.

Torah reaches for the first item and the pursuit-front spikes. The cache is physically repelled—items slide off the desk, the form tears, and the pressure in the room becomes directional, pointing at Torah. Brooks pulls Torah back from the desk. The items are on the floor. Ross has not finished his confession. The anomaly is not satisfied, and now it knows who is in charge.

Owen comes through the front door with her field kit and does not break stride. She takes the corrected form from the desk, reads it once, signs it, and sets it down. She picks up the first item from the cache—a sealed evidence bag, tagged with an Institute designation number—and places it on the desk beside the form. Eleven items. Eleven entries. When the last one is logged, the groaning stops. Not gradually. All at once. Owen looks at the ceiling for a moment, then back at the form. 'Witnessed,' she says. 'Logged. We're done here.'

Torah takes the first item from the cache and holds it. The groaning in the walls gets louder for three seconds—then drops to nothing. Ross is watching. Torah sets the item on the desk, picks up the form, and says Ross's name. Ross says the rest: the items, the dates, the unit number, the act. Torah writes it on the corrected line. Eleven items. Eleven entries. The emergency lighting flickers once and comes back steady. The stairwell door swings open on its own and stays there, no pressure behind it.

cooldown_devoted_property.speaker: brooks
cooldown_devoted_property.line_1: Brooks is outside, sitting on the parking structure steps, not smoking, not on comms. Owen is inside finishing the paperwork. Ross is in a vehicle.
cooldown_devoted_property.line_2: Iyad sends a supplemental report at 0610. It is three pages. She does not summarize it on comms.

lines: Brooks does not debrief. He sits on the steps and watches the vehicle Ross is in. When Torah comes out, Brooks says: 'He knew what he was doing when he signed those forms. He knew what he was doing when he stood next to that door and didn't say anything.' He is not asking a question.

Owen comes out twenty minutes later with the completed transfer packet. She hands it to Torah without ceremony. 'Iyad's supplement is attached. She flagged two other facilities with similar log patterns. Same six-week window.' She pauses. 'Same signature.'

Iyad's supplemental report arrives as a file transfer. The cover line reads: CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY DISCREPANCY: PATTERN ANALYSIS, THREE SITES. She has not called to discuss it. She is waiting to see what the Institute does with it.
environment_state_changes: brooks.mental_state: fixated on Ross's silence duration
lt_mara_owen.fatigue: elevated, carrying the paperwork load
dr_samira_iyad.loyalty: conditional, watching how the Institute handles Ross
squad.morale: reduced if Ross was protected, stable if confession was complete

outcomes: Brooks writes it up. It is short and exact. He does not editorialize. He sends it and does not mention it again, but he does not sit near Ross's empty desk when the squad is back at base.
Owen pulls the other two site files. One is clean on second look. One is not. She flags it and says nothing to Ross's chain of command until Torah tells her to.
The review board gets the file. Iyad's supplement goes with it. Three weeks later, the Institute sends a one-line acknowledgment. Brooks reads it and puts it face-down on the desk.
followup_hooks: Second facility with matching log pattern: a future mission site
Iyad's conditional loyalty: she will support the squad fully if the Institute acts on her report; she will begin routing information around command if it does not
Brooks's fixation on Ross becomes relevant if Ross returns to field status under any circumstances
The residual pressure signature at the facility—if the closure was partial—activates when the building is next accessed

resolution_roofline_naming.speaker: dr_lenora_saye
resolution_roofline_naming.line_1: Saye on comms: Torah, the Institute log is live. Three fields: responsible party, victim, mechanism. The cameras are waiting. Say it on record.
resolution_roofline_naming.line_2: Brooks reports the drive secured. Owen reports corridor control. The footage closes only when Torah names the mechanism.

buttons.label: Name Vess on record
buttons.preview: Deliver the formal on-record naming that the anomaly requires: Vess, the victim, the hidden drive, the...

buttons.label: Force the cameras down
buttons.preview: End the physical danger by destroying the camera hardware entirely, accepting that the symbolic loop may...

operation_plans.label: Name Vess on record
operation_plans.tactical_step: Symbolic close: Torah speaks the naming into the Institute live log—responsible party, victim's name as recovered from the suppressed complaint file Saye located, mechanism of surveillance—satisfying the witness-class closure condition
operation_plans.intent: Deliver the formal on-record naming that the anomaly requires: Vess, the victim, the hidden drive, the suppressed complaint—all three fields, spoken aloud, logged and timestamped
operation_plans.yield: Cameras release floor by floor; footage advances past 11:42 and goes dark; the rooftop unit is last; the building is clear; the Institute log has a permanent record
operation_plans.risk: Speaking the victim's name on a live Institute log without her consent is its own ethical weight; Saye warned Torah about this; the log cannot be unwritten; the partial outcome is that the cameras release but the squad carries the knowledge of what they put on record

lines: Torah reads the three fields into the log. Name. Name. Method. The security office monitors flicker once—all nine—and go dark except for a single white timestamp that reads 11:43 and advances. Brooks watches it tick forward. Owen's radio check comes in on schedule, ninety seconds, no lapse. The cameras on the fourth floor are still. The rooftop unit releases last; Torah hears it on the building's PA system as a faint mechanical click, like a lock opening. Saye's voice on comms: 'Log received. It's in.' She doesn't say anything else for a long time.

Torah gets two of the three fields. The victim's name is in Saye's follow-up message but Torah hesitates on it—putting a name on a permanent Institute record without the person's knowledge is not a clean act, and Torah knows it. The cameras on floors one through three release. The fourth floor holds. The monitors show 11:43 but do not advance further. Owen radios: 'The corridor cameras are still. The one above 4F is not.' Saye says, quietly: 'You need the third field.' Torah has to decide whether to finish it.

Torah opens the log and stops. The three fields are there. The drive is bagged on the desk. Saye is on comms, waiting. But the act of putting a victim's name into an Institute record—a record she doesn't know exists, in a system she has no access to—feels like another version of the thing Vess did. Torah closes the log without entering anything. The monitors stay at 11:42. Brooks looks at Torah and does not ask. Owen's radio check comes in four minutes late.

operation_plans.label: Force the cameras down
operation_plans.tactical_step: Suppress and destroy: Owen's team physically destroys the remaining active camera mounts on the fourth floor and the rooftop unit, eliminating the tracking threat by force; this is a real force option with real consequences
operation_plans.intent: End the physical danger by destroying the camera hardware entirely, accepting that the symbolic loop may not close but protecting personnel from further injury
operation_plans.yield: Physical threat eliminated; Dolan, Reyes, and Owen's team are safe; the building is no longer actively dangerous; the symbolic pressure remains but is no longer manifesting through hardware
operation_plans.risk: Destroying the cameras without the naming leaves the witness-class pressure unresolved; the footage migrates—it appears on the victim's phone, on Saye's monitor at the Institute, on the screen of the first person to open the suppressed complaint file; the anomaly finds another surface

lines: Owen's team takes the mounts down methodically: east corridor first, then west, then the rooftop unit last. It takes eleven minutes. The pan-tilt hardware on the rooftop fights—it swings twice before Owen's specialist gets the power coupling disconnected. When it goes dark, the security office monitors go dark with it. Brooks exhales. 'Physical threat is clear,' Owen radios. She sounds tired rather than satisfied. Saye's voice on comms is careful: 'The pressure index dropped. It didn't close.' A pause. 'It'll find somewhere else to be.' Torah has the drive. The log is still open.

Owen gets the fourth-floor mounts. The rooftop unit is harder—it's on a separate power circuit and the coupling is behind a locked panel. Her specialist gets it open but the mount swings during the attempt and catches them across the hand. Not serious. The unit goes dark. The security office monitors go dark. Then Brooks's phone lights up with a video notification from a number he doesn't recognize. He shows it to Torah without playing it. The thumbnail is a window. 11:42 PM. 'It moved,' Saye says on comms. 'Torah, the log is still open.'

The rooftop unit does not go down. Owen's team gets to the panel and finds the power coupling has been physically modified—the wiring runs somewhere it shouldn't, into the building's main trunk, and cutting it would take the whole grid down. Owen calls it: 'I can't do this without taking the building dark, and I'm not doing that with Dolan and Reyes still on the fourth floor.' She pulls her team back. The cameras are still tracking. The monitors are still at 11:42. Brooks looks at Torah. 'The log,' he says. Just that.

Torah reads the three fields into the log. Name. Name. Method. The security office monitors flicker once—all nine—and go dark except for a single white timestamp that reads 11:43 and advances. Brooks watches it tick forward. Owen's radio check comes in on schedule, ninety seconds, no lapse. The cameras on the fourth floor are still. The rooftop unit releases last; Torah hears it on the building's PA system as a faint mechanical click, like a lock opening. Saye's voice on comms: 'Log received. It's in.' She doesn't say anything else for a long time.

Owen's team takes the mounts down methodically: east corridor first, then west, then the rooftop unit last. It takes eleven minutes. The pan-tilt hardware on the rooftop fights—it swings twice before Owen's specialist gets the power coupling disconnected. When it goes dark, the security office monitors go dark with it. Brooks exhales. 'Physical threat is clear,' Owen radios. She sounds tired rather than satisfied. Saye's voice on comms is careful: 'The pressure index dropped. It didn't close.' A pause. 'It'll find somewhere else to be.' Torah has the drive. The log is still open.

cooldown_roofline_debrief.speaker: brooks
cooldown_roofline_debrief.line_1: Brooks sets the bagged drive on the debrief table and sits down. He doesn't open his notes. The after-action form is in front of him with one field circled.
cooldown_roofline_debrief.line_2: Owen files her report from the doorway, standing. Saye's follow-up message is on Torah's tablet: a name, no other text.

lines: Brooks has circled the field labeled 'corrosive conduct identified' on the after-action form. He hasn't written anything in it yet.
Owen's report is three paragraphs. The section on personnel recovery condition took her twenty minutes to fill in. Torah can tell because the ink is slightly different—she switched pens.
Saye's message contains one line: a woman's name, and below it, the words 'suppressed complaint, fourteen months ago, never forwarded.' No signature.
The bagged drive sits between Brooks and Torah on the table. Neither of them moves it.
Brooks finally says: 'The log entry is permanent.' He's not asking. He's deciding whether that's the right word for what it is.

visible_text: Brooks has circled the field labeled 'corrosive conduct identified' on the after-action form. He hasn't written anything in it yet.
Owen's report is three paragraphs. The section on personnel recovery condition took her twenty minutes to fill in. Torah can tell because the ink is slightly different—she switched pens.
Saye's message contains one line: a woman's name, and below it, the words 'suppressed complaint, fourteen months ago, never forwarded.' No signature.
The bagged drive sits between Brooks and Torah on the table. Neither of them moves it.
Brooks finally says: 'The log entry is permanent.' He's not asking. He's deciding whether that's the right word for what it is.
outcomes: If Torah says it needed to be on record: Brooks writes in the after-action field. Owen comes in from the doorway and sits down. The debrief finishes. Saye sends a second message two hours later: she has located the victim and is asking Institute legal about notification protocol. The squad's cohesion holds.

If Torah says it's worth sitting with: Brooks doesn't write anything. He closes the form. Owen stays in the doorway. Nobody says anything for a while, and then Brooks says, 'Yeah,' and that's the end of the debrief. Saye's message stays unanswered until morning.

If Torah says the building is clear: Owen looks at Torah for a moment, then nods once and leaves. Brooks writes one word in the after-action field and closes the form. He takes the drive with him when he goes. Torah is alone with the tablet and Saye's message.
followup_hooks: Saye's notification protocol question becomes a future mission pressure: does the victim learn what the Institute recorded, and does she have a say in what happens to the log entry
The suppressed complaint file contains a second name—someone in building management who received the complaint and routed it back to Vess; that person is still employed
Brooks's after-action form, if completed, enters the Institute's conduct record and may surface in a future command review
Dolan and Reyes: Reyes was found staring at the window in 4F; his mental state in the days following is a latent thread
