root_devoted_property_intercept_partial_.speaker: SITREP
root_devoted_property_intercept_partial_.line_1: Brooks gets to the door and holds it for ninety seconds before the pressure spikes.
root_devoted_property_intercept_partial_.line_2: The evidence cache is still reachable, but the door is no longer a held boundary. Owen has to move the recovery team under pressure instead of treating the corridor as secure.

lines: Brooks gets to the door and holds it for ninety seconds before the pressure spikes. He braces, absorbs it, stays upright—but the door buckles outward an inch and does not go back. He calls over his shoulder without turning: 'Whatever you're doing, do it faster.' His voice is steady. His hands are not.
The evidence cache is still reachable, but the door is no longer a held boundary. Owen has to move the recovery team under pressure instead of treating the corridor as secure.

root_devoted_property_intercept_failure_.speaker: SITREP
root_devoted_property_intercept_failure_.line_1: The pursuit-front hits the door before Brooks reaches threshold position.
root_devoted_property_intercept_failure_.line_2: The cache remains inside the active corridor, and Brooks is outside the threshold. Any recovery order now starts as a rescue-and-custody problem, not a clean evidence transfer.

lines: The pursuit-front hits the door before Brooks reaches threshold position. He is driven back three steps, shoulder into the wall, and the door swings open. Cold pressure fills the ground floor. Brooks gets up without a word, but he is moving carefully, and Owen's voice on comms goes very quiet.
The cache remains inside the active corridor, and Brooks is outside the threshold. Any recovery order now starts as a rescue-and-custody problem, not a clean evidence transfer.

root_devoted_property_full_disclosure_pa.speaker: SITREP
root_devoted_property_full_disclosure_pa.line_1: Iyad gets through six entries before Ross cuts her off.
root_devoted_property_full_disclosure_pa.line_2: Iyad has enough to prove the custody break, but not enough to quiet the pressure front. Owen can move on the cache, but the missing entries will follow the report.

lines: Iyad gets through six entries before Ross cuts her off. 'I know what the forms say.' He does not confess. But he stops lying: 'Unit four. Lower level.' He will not say the rest. The pursuit-front does not collapse—but it pauses, like something waiting to hear the rest of the sentence.
Iyad has enough to prove the custody break, but not enough to quiet the pressure front. Owen can move on the cache, but the missing entries will follow the report.

root_devoted_property_full_disclosure_fa.speaker: SITREP
root_devoted_property_full_disclosure_fa.line_1: Ross listens to all eleven entries without moving.
root_devoted_property_full_disclosure_fa.line_2: The disclosure lands without changing Ross or the corridor. The team has to treat the cache as contested evidence under active pursuit.

lines: Ross listens to all eleven entries without moving. When Iyad finishes, he says: 'I want Institute counsel before I say anything.' The pursuit-front does not care about counsel. The door blows open. Owen's voice on comms: 'Pressure just hit the lobby glass. I'm pulling the outer perimeter back.'
The disclosure lands without changing Ross or the corridor. The team has to treat the cache as contested evidence under active pursuit.

root_roofline_contact_intercept_partial_.speaker: SITREP
root_roofline_contact_intercept_partial_.line_1: Owen gets Dolan out, but the corridor cameras keep tracking the team.
root_roofline_contact_intercept_partial_.line_2: Dolan is clear, but the corridor is not won. The cameras still own the route, and the naming record has to be made while the team moves.

lines: Owen gets Dolan out. Reyes is harder—the door to 4F resists in a way that has nothing to do with the lock, and by the time they breach it, one of Owen's team has taken a glancing hit from a pan-tilt mount swinging hard through its arc. Reyes is inside, uninjured but fixed on the window, not responding to his name. Owen gets a hand on his shoulder and he moves, but slowly. 'We're out,' she radios. 'One of mine needs a look. Reyes is ambulatory.' She doesn't say what Reyes was looking at.
Dolan is clear, but the corridor is not won. The cameras still own the route, and the naming record has to be made while the team moves.

root_roofline_contact_intercept_failure_.speaker: SITREP
root_roofline_contact_intercept_failure_.line_1: The bound goes wrong in the narrow corridor.
root_roofline_contact_intercept_failure_.line_2: The extraction attempt has become part of the hazard. Before Torah can name the mechanism, Owen has to recover the corridor or abandon that approach.

lines: The bound goes wrong in the narrow corridor. The camera mount on the east end swings faster than Owen's team anticipated and catches the lead element across the shoulder—not a killing blow, but enough to drop them. Owen calls a break contact immediately, pulling her team back to the stairwell. Dolan and Reyes are still in the corridor. 'I'm not sending anyone back in there without a different approach,' Owen says on the radio. Her voice is controlled. That's the part that's hard to hear.
The extraction attempt has become part of the hazard. Before Torah can name the mechanism, Owen has to recover the corridor or abandon that approach.

root_roofline_contact_recover_partial_br.speaker: SITREP
root_roofline_contact_recover_partial_br.line_1: Brooks gets the drive bagged and logged, but the date range on the label is partially overwritten—someone tried to scratch it out, recently, with something sharp.
root_roofline_contact_recover_partial_br.line_2: The drive is usable, but its custody is already compromised. Brooks can support the naming, provided the report admits the scratch-out instead of smoothing it away.

lines: Brooks gets the drive bagged and logged, but the date range on the label is partially overwritten—someone tried to scratch it out, recently, with something sharp. He can read enough. 'Credentials are Vess's. Date range is approximate but consistent,' he says. 'It'll hold.' He glances at the monitors once, by accident. His reflection is there for a moment, then gone. He doesn't mention it on the radio.
The drive is usable, but its custody is already compromised. Brooks can support the naming, provided the report admits the scratch-out instead of smoothing it away.

root_roofline_contact_recover_failure_br.speaker: SITREP
root_roofline_contact_recover_failure_br.line_1: Brooks is halfway through the in-situ photography when the monitors shift.
root_roofline_contact_recover_failure_br.line_2: The evidence capture fails under movement. The team still has the live log, but the debrief cannot pretend the drive came out clean.

lines: Brooks is halfway through the in-situ photography when the monitors shift. All nine screens show the same image: Brooks, standing at the rack, from directly above—an angle that requires a camera in the ceiling, and there is no camera in the ceiling. He freezes. The drive is in his hand but the chain-of-custody log is open on the desk and he has not written anything in it yet. 'I need a minute,' he says on the radio. Torah can hear that he means more than a minute.
The evidence capture fails under movement. The team still has the live log, but the debrief cannot pretend the drive came out clean.

root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_owen_.speaker: SITREP
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_owen_.line_1: Owen gets Reiss on the line and halfway up before the gantry drops another section.
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_owen_.line_2: Reiss is alive and moving, but the shaft still has him. The closeout now has to account for a live extraction instead of a completed rescue.

lines: Owen gets Reiss on the line and halfway up before the gantry drops another section. The brake jams. Brooks hauls by hand for the last four meters, and Reiss comes over the edge hard, one shoulder taking the concrete lip. Saye's voice on comms: 'He's breathing. Get him flat.' Owen pulls herself up last, moving carefully, and does not look at her hands.
Reiss is alive and moving, but the shaft still has him. The closeout now has to account for a live extraction instead of a completed rescue.

root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_owen_.speaker: SITREP
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_owen_.line_1: The pulse hits when Owen is eight meters down.
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_owen_.line_2: Owen is in the shaft when the pulse lands. The signatory question remains, but first the team has to get its own rescuer back above the gantry.

lines: The pulse hits when Owen is eight meters down. The secondary anchor holds but the primary frame shears and the rope line whips. Owen arrests the fall but the shock load tears the brake assembly. Brooks grabs the line bare-handed and holds it. Reiss is still below. Owen is hanging in the dark, and the shaft is very quiet now, which is worse than the noise.
Owen is in the shaft when the pulse lands. The signatory question remains, but first the team has to get its own rescuer back above the gantry.

root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_brook.speaker: SITREP
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_brook.line_1: Brooks reaches the gate as the contractor vehicle is already rolling.
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_brook.line_2: The vehicle is slowed, not stopped, and the false report is still at risk of leaving site. The signatory record must proceed with custody already under strain.

lines: Brooks reaches the gate as the contractor vehicle is already rolling. He steps into the lane. The driver stops. Two of the three colleagues are already in the cab. The third is still in the break room. Brooks has two of them. He radios Owen: 'I need someone on the brake. Now.' There is a pause on the line before she answers.
The vehicle is slowed, not stopped, and the false report is still at risk of leaving site. The signatory record must proceed with custody already under strain.

root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_brook.speaker: SITREP
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_brook.line_1: Brooks is twenty meters from the gate when the contractor vehicle pulls out.
root_shaft_seven_contact_intercept_brook.line_2: The vehicle gets outside the perimeter with the false report still in motion. Closing the shaft now means working from a damaged evidence chain.

lines: Brooks is twenty meters from the gate when the contractor vehicle pulls out. He radios the plate. The colleagues are gone. He stands at the empty gate for a moment, then turns back toward the shaft. The false report is still in his hand. He looks at it like it has changed shape.
The vehicle gets outside the perimeter with the false report still in motion. Closing the shaft now means working from a damaged evidence chain.

root_forty_day_window_intercept_ross_bou.speaker: SITREP
root_forty_day_window_intercept_ross_bou.line_1: Ross makes the bound but the echo reorients faster than Owen can track.
root_forty_day_window_intercept_ross_bou.line_2: The survivor is reachable, but the echo has adapted. The archive file can still close the grievance, provided the team treats the aisle as unstable.

lines: Ross makes the bound but the echo reorients faster than Owen can track. It catches him in the open for one step. He takes a glancing contact on his left shoulder, a shallow laceration that burns immediately, and he keeps moving. He gets the worker out. He does not mention the shoulder until Saye asks why his breathing changed. Owen, from the aisle: 'I had it. I had it for three seconds.' She does not say anything else.
The survivor is reachable, but the echo has adapted. The archive file can still close the grievance, provided the team treats the aisle as unstable.

root_forty_day_window_intercept_ross_bou.speaker: SITREP
root_forty_day_window_intercept_ross_bou.line_1: Owen's suppression holds for two seconds and then the echo splits its path.
root_forty_day_window_intercept_ross_bou.line_2: The echo has the route, and Ross cannot make the rescue cleanly. The file remains the only stable point in the room.

lines: Owen's suppression holds for two seconds and then the echo splits its path. Ross is halfway to press unit four when it finds him. The laceration opens across his right forearm, deep enough to matter. He goes down to one knee. The worker is still behind the press unit. Torah calls Owen back and Ross pulls himself to cover. Saye, over comms, very quiet: 'Ross. Tell me your depth.' Ross tells her. There is a pause. 'You need to close that before you do anything else.' The survivor is still in the kill zone.
The echo has the route, and Ross cannot make the rescue cleanly. The file remains the only stable point in the room.

root_forty_day_window_intercept_owen_hol.speaker: SITREP
root_forty_day_window_intercept_owen_hol.line_1: Owen holds for one full pass and most of a second.
root_forty_day_window_intercept_owen_hol.line_2: Owen buys a short window, not control. Any closure has to happen while the echo is still testing the aisle.

lines: Owen holds for one full pass and most of a second. On the second pass the echo finds a gap in her coverage and she takes contact on her left side, a shallow cut below the ribs that she does not report immediately. She holds the position anyway. When Ross calls clear she steps back and her voice on comms is level but slower than usual. Torah notices. Owen does not acknowledge it.
Owen buys a short window, not control. Any closure has to happen while the echo is still testing the aisle.

root_forty_day_window_intercept_owen_hol.speaker: SITREP
root_forty_day_window_intercept_owen_hol.line_1: The echo does not behave the way Owen expects.
root_forty_day_window_intercept_owen_hol.line_2: The suppression plan fails before the survivor is safe. The archive decision now starts under injury pressure and a narrowing interval.

lines: The echo does not behave the way Owen expects. It does not follow a straight path. It angles around her second position and she has to move, and when she moves she loses the fix. The sound is suddenly behind her. She turns and it has already passed. Ross is not yet at the press unit. Torah calls the bound off. Owen, back at the stairwell door, is looking at the center aisle with an expression that is not fear exactly. It is the look of someone recalculating a problem they thought they understood.
The suppression plan fails before the survivor is safe. The archive decision now starts under injury pressure and a narrowing interval.

root_enough_until_morning_intercept_part.speaker: SITREP
root_enough_until_morning_intercept_part.line_1: The door goes, but the angle is wrong.
root_enough_until_morning_intercept_part.line_2: The cold-store opens badly and the canteen stays active. The redistribution decision has to happen while the team is still managing the breach.

lines: The door goes, but the angle is wrong. A portion of the discharge hits the canteen wall and runs down it in slow sheets. Ross gets the forklift across the bay entrance before more follows. Three of the seated patients turn their heads toward the wall. Iyad moves between them and it immediately. 'Do not let them touch that,' she says, and her voice is completely flat. The clock is shorter now.
The cold-store opens badly and the canteen stays active. The redistribution decision has to happen while the team is still managing the breach.

root_enough_until_morning_intercept_fail.speaker: SITREP
root_enough_until_morning_intercept_fail.line_1: The door fails before Ross reaches the forklift.
root_enough_until_morning_intercept_fail.line_2: The failed entry leaves the surplus in place and Ross outside the useful angle. The next move is no longer recovery; it is triage under a moving door.

lines: The door fails before Ross reaches the forklift. The discharge enters the canteen at pressure. Ross is hit across the left arm and shoulder. He does not sit down—he backs against the wall and keeps his arm away from his face—but Iyad is already moving toward him, and the seated group has grown by two. Ross says, through his teeth, 'I'm not sitting. I'm not sitting.' He means it as information.
The failed entry leaves the surplus in place and Ross outside the useful angle. The next move is no longer recovery; it is triage under a moving door.

root_enough_until_morning_quarantine_par.speaker: SITREP
root_enough_until_morning_quarantine_par.line_1: The tables are cleared but one volunteer freezes at the far end of the canteen and will not move.
root_enough_until_morning_quarantine_par.line_2: Most of the canteen is clear, but one person remains inside the behavior. The queue and the surplus cannot be reconciled until that hold is broken or documented.

lines: The tables are cleared but one volunteer freezes at the far end of the canteen and will not move. The patient nearest him reaches for the volunteer's jacket pocket—there is a wrapped granola bar in it. Owen gets there before contact, takes the bar, and walks it out the loading bay door. The volunteer sits down in an empty chair. He does not pick up any food. Owen watches him for ten seconds, decides he is not joining the group, and goes for the office. The shredder is already running when she gets there. She gets most of it.
Most of the canteen is clear, but one person remains inside the behavior. The queue and the surplus cannot be reconciled until that hold is broken or documented.

root_enough_until_morning_quarantine_fai.speaker: SITREP
root_enough_until_morning_quarantine_fai.line_1: Voss is not in his office.
root_enough_until_morning_quarantine_fai.line_2: Voss is missing from the office, and the chain of responsibility is no longer in hand. The surplus can still be answered, but not as a clean internal correction.

lines: Voss is not in his office. He has the master inventory binder with him and he is in the loading bay, moving toward his car. Brooks sees him from the roof and calls it down. Owen runs. She gets the binder but Voss has already torn out the summary pages—the ones with the cumulative totals. What remains is enough to show the pattern but not enough to prove the number. Owen stands in the parking lot holding a binder with ragged edges and does not say anything for a moment. Then: 'Brooks, I need you to tell me what you saw from up there. All of it. Start from when you arrived.'
Voss is missing from the office, and the chain of responsibility is no longer in hand. The surplus can still be answered, but not as a clean internal correction.

root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_hold_th.speaker: SITREP
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_hold_th.line_1: Brooks gets through, but the blast door keeps cycling behind him.
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_hold_th.line_2: Brooks makes it through, but the route is not stable. The file can be read only while the door is still negotiating the space.

lines: Brooks gets through. The door advances one increment while he is inside—the gap narrows to the width of a turned shoulder. Ross passes the report through first. Brooks comes through sideways, scraping the frame, and Owen catches his arm. He is breathing hard. 'I'm fine,' he says, which means he is not. The report is intact. Vasek is still inside.
Brooks makes it through, but the route is not stable. The file can be read only while the door is still negotiating the space.

root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_hold_th.speaker: SITREP
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_hold_th.line_2: The door takes the timing away. Ross and Vasek remain inside with the falsified logs, and the next report has to start from a failed extraction.

lines: The door advances mid-bound. Brooks stops at the gap, reads the clearance, and pulls back. 'I can't make that.' Owen's jaw tightens. She does not argue with him. Inside, Ross's relay goes to static. The report is still in there. Owen turns to Park. 'Options.'
The door takes the timing away. Ross and Vasek remain inside with the falsified logs, and the next report has to start from a failed extraction.

root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_suppres.speaker: SITREP
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_suppres.line_1: Park arrests the interference on three channels but the node reroutes through a fourth.
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_suppres.line_2: Park slows the interference without ending it. Saye can keep the form open, but every readback now competes with a rerouted signal.

lines: Park arrests the interference on three channels but the node reroutes through a fourth. The filing link holds—barely. Ross can transmit, but the connection is unstable. Every page takes twice as long. The door moves again while they wait. Park does not look up from the terminal. 'It's going to be close,' she says, which is the most she will say about it.
Park slows the interference without ending it. Saye can keep the form open, but every readback now competes with a rerouted signal.

root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_suppres.speaker: SITREP
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_suppres.line_1: The interference node absorbs the suppression spike and accelerates.
root_kaph_door_cycling_intercept_suppres.line_2: The suppression spike feeds the node. The incident form is still open, but the system has more control than it did before.

lines: The interference node absorbs the suppression spike and accelerates. Every channel goes to ant-pattern simultaneously. Park's terminal link severs. The screen shows a single line of output—the same sensor entry, repeating, from week three of Holt's buried log—and then goes dark. Saye's relay drops. Park sits back. 'I cut us off,' she says. She says it once, flat, and does not say it again.
The suppression spike feeds the node. The incident form is still open, but the system has more control than it did before.

resolution_borrowed_separation_full_disc.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_full_disc.line_1: Saye takes custody of the evidence.
resolution_borrowed_separation_full_disc.line_2: Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

lines: Saye takes custody of the evidence. The disclosure to Reiss is partial—she tells him enough to explain the physical symptoms, not enough to explain the full anomaly mechanism. She is not lying; she is managing what he can absorb right now. The manufacturer's identity goes into the Institute file. The department head's denial of the wig remains request is noted but not escalated. The second heat signature is in Saye's personal notes, not the official report. She will decide later whether to include it. That decision will follow her.
Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

resolution_borrowed_separation_full_disc.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_full_disc.line_1: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them.
resolution_borrowed_separation_full_disc.line_2: The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

lines: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them. The wig remains are in the evidence container but the chain of custody has a gap. Saye files what she has. The manufacturer's identity is in the report but the physical evidence is incomplete. Reiss is in Institute medical and has not been told anything. Saye sits in the vehicle for a long time after filing. Ross finds her there an hour later. He does not ask what she is thinking. He already knows.
The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

resolution_borrowed_separation_torah_spe.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_torah_spe.line_2: The release weakens the hold but does not settle the container. Park can move it only if the record names the residual risk.

lines: Torah speaks the release. The anomaly responds—the warmth in the container drops, the column fracture stops—but the close is incomplete. A section of the wig's base is still adhered to Reiss's scalp, in Institute medical custody now, and the obligation has not fully discharged. Reiss's tremor continues at a lower level. The instrument readings drop but do not reach baseline. The anomaly is suppressed. It is not closed. Saye flags the residual in her report. The evidence container goes into restricted storage. Torah knows, without being told, that this will come back.
The release weakens the hold but does not settle the container. Park can move it only if the record names the residual risk.

resolution_borrowed_separation_torah_spe.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_torah_spe.line_1: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot.
resolution_borrowed_separation_torah_spe.line_2: The release becomes another escalation. The site moves to evacuation posture before any after-action language can call the container safe.

lines: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot. Not warm—hot, the kind of heat that makes Park step back and Owen call everyone to the breach point. The discharge pulse moves through the floor. The temporary column props hold but one of them buckles. The building alarm triggers. Everyone exits. The container is left inside—too hot to carry. Ross seals the east entrance. The instrument readings spike and then drop to a level that Saye, on comms, describes as 'not baseline.' She does not elaborate. The building is condemned. The container is inside it. Torah stands at the cordon line and does not speak for the rest of the operation. Owen files the after-action report. She leaves Torah's role in the resolution section blank and sends it to Saye to complete.
The release becomes another escalation. The site moves to evacuation posture before any after-action language can call the container safe.

babel_resolution_scatter_authority_torah.speaker: SITREP
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_torah.line_1: Torah distributes the cards but one civilian group leader—a transit worker who has been listening to the terminal loop for two hours—repeats a fragment of the approved phrase back to Torah when confirming his instructions.
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_torah.line_2: The paper authority works, but the phrase has leaked into civilian mouths. The cooldown has to track who heard the syntax and who repeated it.

lines: Torah distributes the cards but one civilian group leader—a transit worker who has been listening to the terminal loop for two hours—repeats a fragment of the approved phrase back to Torah when confirming his instructions. It is not a full transmission. It is not routed through the hub. But a thin line of mortar appears at the base of the lobby door, and Saye says, carefully: 'It's not gone. It's quiet. Those are different things.' The building is passable. The residue is not resolved. Torah pockets his card and says nothing.
The paper authority works, but the phrase has leaked into civilian mouths. The cooldown has to track who heard the syntax and who repeated it.

babel_resolution_scatter_authority_torah.speaker: SITREP
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_torah.line_1: Torah is halfway through the second card when a first responder outside, unaware of the protocol, transmits a full approved-syntax check-in on the Institute frequency.
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_torah.line_2: The responder transmission turns the approved wording into a fresh hazard. The paper-card plan cannot be treated as clean command.

lines: Torah is halfway through the second card when a first responder outside, unaware of the protocol, transmits a full approved-syntax check-in on the Institute frequency. The lobby wall rises in four seconds—floor to ceiling, solid brick, cutting Torah off from Owen and the stairwell. Torah is on the platform side with one civilian group. Owen and Ross are in the lobby. Saye is saying something on the hardline but the signal is breaking up. Torah looks at the wall. He looks at the card in his hand. He writes on the back of it: find another way out. He slides it under the wall.
The responder transmission turns the approved wording into a fresh hazard. The paper-card plan cannot be treated as clean command.

babel_resolution_scatter_authority_full_.speaker: SITREP
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_full_.line_1: Saye fragments the signal but the channel noise is uneven—she got three of the four dispatch frequencies and missed the emergency override band.
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_full_.line_2: Saye breaks most of the channel, but the override band remains live. The debrief has to keep the missed frequency in the causal chain.

lines: Saye fragments the signal but the channel noise is uneven—she got three of the four dispatch frequencies and missed the emergency override band. The building is mostly inert. Mostly. Owen finds a hairline crack in the lobby wall that was not there before she finished, and points at it without speaking. The override band is still live. Any full approved-syntax transmission on that frequency will restart the residue. Saye says, from the hardline: 'I need someone to physically cut the override antenna on the roof before you leave. It's a gray box. You'll know it.' She pauses. 'I'm sorry. I should have caught all four.'
Saye breaks most of the channel, but the override band remains live. The debrief has to keep the missed frequency in the causal chain.

babel_resolution_scatter_authority_full_.speaker: SITREP
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_full_.line_1: Saye's fragmentation attempt triggers a feedback loop in the hub's dispatch system.
babel_resolution_scatter_authority_full_.line_2: The dispatch hub turns the fragmentation attempt back on the floor. Any paper authority now exists inside a recorded feedback event.

lines: Saye's fragmentation attempt triggers a feedback loop in the hub's dispatch system. The terminal housing is dark but the frequency is now broadcasting the approved syntax on all four channels simultaneously, louder than before. The walls that had stopped rising begin again. Owen is at the door. Ross is on his feet. Torah is looking at the ceiling. Saye's voice on the hardline is steady but Torah can hear her typing fast: 'I made it worse. Get out. Get everyone out now. I will find another way to close this but not from here and not tonight.' Torah does not argue. He signals Owen: evacuate.
The dispatch hub turns the fragmentation attempt back on the floor. Any paper authority now exists inside a recorded feedback event.

resolution_aldermoor_naming_torah_speaks.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_naming_torah_speaks.line_1: Torah gets through most of it but the specific act—what Voss did before he filed the report—is not in Iyad's notes, only implied.
resolution_aldermoor_naming_torah_speaks.line_2: The naming has weight, but the missing act leaves the floor room to argue. The debrief has to carry the gap instead of closing around it.

lines: Torah gets through most of it but the specific act—what Voss did before he filed the report—is not in Iyad's notes, only implied. The floor slows but does not stop. The outline holds but the seepage continues at the edges, thin and patient. Iyad's voice comes over comms: 'You need the action, not just the time. What he did, not when.' Torah has one more attempt before the window closes.
The naming has weight, but the missing act leaves the floor room to argue. The debrief has to carry the gap instead of closing around it.

resolution_aldermoor_naming_torah_speaks.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_naming_torah_speaks.line_1: Torah speaks but the statement is built on Iyad's preliminary read, and one timestamp is wrong—the gap is forty-one minutes, not thirty-eight, and the floor knows the difference.
resolution_aldermoor_naming_torah_speaks.line_2: The wrong timestamp gives the room a foothold. The debrief cannot treat the naming as reliable until the record is corrected.

lines: Torah speaks but the statement is built on Iyad's preliminary read, and one timestamp is wrong—the gap is forty-one minutes, not thirty-eight, and the floor knows the difference. The seepage surges outward, fast, and the threshold slams shut. Brooks is inside. Ross is outside. Torah is in the corridor with the door between them and the outline spreading toward the walls. Iyad is already pulling the original nurse call log. 'Get Brooks out,' she says. 'I need four minutes.'
The wrong timestamp gives the room a foothold. The debrief cannot treat the naming as reliable until the record is corrected.

resolution_aldermoor_naming_intercept_pa.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_naming_intercept_pa.line_1: Ross gets Voss to the corridor but Voss pulls back at the threshold and the door slams between them—the anomaly reads the struggle as another act at the door and the frame shudders.
resolution_aldermoor_naming_intercept_pa.line_2: Ross moves Voss, but the threshold reads the struggle as part of the offense. The debrief has to mark containment as incomplete.

lines: Ross gets Voss to the corridor but Voss pulls back at the threshold and the door slams between them—the anomaly reads the struggle as another act at the door and the frame shudders. Ross holds Voss against the opposite wall, away from the door, and calls to Torah: 'Do it now. He's here but I can't hold the threshold.' Torah speaks with the door between them and Voss. The floor slows. The outline holds. It is not a clean close—the seepage at the corridor edge does not fully stop—but the room releases Brooks.
Ross moves Voss, but the threshold reads the struggle as part of the offense. The debrief has to mark containment as incomplete.

resolution_aldermoor_naming_intercept_fa.speaker: SITREP
resolution_aldermoor_naming_intercept_fa.line_1: Voss fights Ross at the threshold and the door hits them both.
resolution_aldermoor_naming_intercept_fa.line_2: The door turns the extraction into another injury. The record can still name Voss, but not as a clean removal from Room 14.

lines: Voss fights Ross at the threshold and the door hits them both. Ross takes it across the back and goes down. Voss runs. The floor inside Room 14 surges—the outline expands to the walls and the fluid reaches the corridor in a wide dark line. Brooks is inside, standing in it, and the drag sensation hits him hard enough that he grabs the windowsill. Torah calls the evacuation. Ross gets up slowly, one hand on the wall, and does not say anything for a long time.
The door turns the extraction into another injury. The record can still name Voss, but not as a clean removal from Room 14.

resolution_devoted_property_recover_part.speaker: SITREP
resolution_devoted_property_recover_part.line_1: Owen gets the form signed and the first seven items logged before the pursuit-front releases in a single pressure spike that knocks her sideways into the desk.
resolution_devoted_property_recover_part.line_2: Owen saves part of the chain before the pressure hits her. The cooldown must track which items are signed, which are not, and why.

lines: Owen gets the form signed and the first seven items logged before the pursuit-front releases in a single pressure spike that knocks her sideways into the desk. She catches herself, does not drop the item in her hand, and finishes the entry standing at an angle. The groaning fades unevenly—lower, then lower, then a residual hum that does not fully stop. 'Close enough for tonight,' she says, but she does not sound certain. Iyad, on comms, notes the residual for the record.
Owen saves part of the chain before the pressure hits her. The cooldown must track which items are signed, which are not, and why.

resolution_devoted_property_recover_fail.speaker: SITREP
resolution_devoted_property_recover_fail.line_1: The pursuit-front releases before Owen reaches the desk.
resolution_devoted_property_recover_fail.line_2: The release reaches the desk before custody can form. The cache remains a contested object in the after-action record.

lines: The pursuit-front releases before Owen reaches the desk. The pressure wave moves through the ground floor and the cache scatters—items across the floor, one evidence bag split open, the corrected form blown against the wall. Owen is down, not unconscious but not moving fast. Brooks gets to her. The items are still here, but the transfer is not witnessed, the form is not signed, and Iyad's voice on the earpiece is very careful: 'The anomaly is not closed. It's just quiet.'
The release reaches the desk before custody can form. The cache remains a contested object in the after-action record.

resolution_devoted_property_torah_speaks.speaker: SITREP
resolution_devoted_property_torah_speaks.line_1: Torah gets through eight items before the pursuit-front makes its last move—a directed pressure that hits Torah in the chest and stops there, held, like something testing whether it can pass.
resolution_devoted_property_torah_speaks.line_2: Torah gets enough of the inventory spoken to slow the front, but the pressure still tests her. The cooldown has to preserve the unfinished items.

lines: Torah gets through eight items before the pursuit-front makes its last move—a directed pressure that hits Torah in the chest and stops there, held, like something testing whether it can pass. It cannot. Torah finishes the entry. The form is signed. The transfer is witnessed. But when Torah sets down the last item, there is a faint warmth in the hands that was not there before, and Iyad notes it on the record without being asked.
Torah gets enough of the inventory spoken to slow the front, but the pressure still tests her. The cooldown has to preserve the unfinished items.

resolution_devoted_property_torah_speaks.speaker: SITREP
resolution_devoted_property_torah_speaks.line_1: Torah reaches for the first item and the pursuit-front spikes.
resolution_devoted_property_torah_speaks.line_2: The first item triggers the front before the formal naming can begin. The squad leaves with the cache legally and physically unsettled.

lines: Torah reaches for the first item and the pursuit-front spikes. The cache is physically repelled—items slide off the desk, the form tears, and the pressure in the room becomes directional, pointing at Torah. Brooks pulls Torah back from the desk. The items are on the floor. Ross has not finished his confession. The anomaly is not satisfied, and now it knows who is in charge.
The first item triggers the front before the formal naming can begin. The squad leaves with the cache legally and physically unsettled.

resolution_roofline_naming_full_disclosu.speaker: SITREP
resolution_roofline_naming_full_disclosu.line_1: Torah gets two of the three fields into the live log.
resolution_roofline_naming_full_disclosu.line_2: Two fields will support a report, but the missing field leaves the mechanism breathing. The debrief has to show what Torah could not name.

lines: Torah gets two of the three fields. The victim's name is in Saye's follow-up message but Torah hesitates on it—putting a name on a permanent Institute record without the person's knowledge is not a clean act, and Torah knows it. The cameras on floors one through three release. The fourth floor holds. The monitors show 11:43 but do not advance further. Owen radios: 'The corridor cameras are still. The one above 4F is not.' Saye says, quietly: 'You need the third field.' Torah has to decide whether to finish it.
Two fields will support a report, but the missing field leaves the mechanism breathing. The debrief has to show what Torah could not name.

resolution_roofline_naming_full_disclosu.speaker: SITREP
resolution_roofline_naming_full_disclosu.line_1: Torah opens the log and stops.
resolution_roofline_naming_full_disclosu.line_2: The log stops Torah before the naming can land. The footage cannot be closed as proof until the missing relation is handled.

lines: Torah opens the log and stops. The three fields are there. The drive is bagged on the desk. Saye is on comms, waiting. But the act of putting a victim's name into an Institute record—a record she doesn't know exists, in a system she has no access to—feels like another version of the thing Vess did. Torah closes the log without entering anything. The monitors stay at 11:42. Brooks looks at Torah and does not ask. Owen's radio check comes in four minutes late.
The log stops Torah before the naming can land. The footage cannot be closed as proof until the missing relation is handled.

resolution_roofline_naming_destroy_parti.speaker: SITREP
resolution_roofline_naming_destroy_parti.line_1: Owen gets the fourth-floor mounts down, but the rooftop unit remains live.
resolution_roofline_naming_destroy_parti.line_2: Owen breaks enough hardware to reduce the loop, not enough to end it. The debrief has to distinguish disabled mounts from dead cameras.

lines: Owen gets the fourth-floor mounts. The rooftop unit is harder—it's on a separate power circuit and the coupling is behind a locked panel. Her specialist gets it open but the mount swings during the attempt and catches them across the hand. Not serious. The unit goes dark. The security office monitors go dark. Then Brooks's phone lights up with a video notification from a number he doesn't recognize. He shows it to Torah without playing it. The thumbnail is a window. 11:42 PM. 'It moved,' Saye says on comms. 'Torah, the log is still open.'
Owen breaks enough hardware to reduce the loop, not enough to end it. The debrief has to distinguish disabled mounts from dead cameras.

resolution_roofline_naming_destroy_failu.speaker: SITREP
resolution_roofline_naming_destroy_failu.line_1: The rooftop unit does not go down.
resolution_roofline_naming_destroy_failu.line_2: The rooftop unit stays alive and keeps the floor under observation. The team leaves with the mechanism still able to watch.

lines: The rooftop unit does not go down. Owen's team gets to the panel and finds the power coupling has been physically modified—the wiring runs somewhere it shouldn't, into the building's main trunk, and cutting it would take the whole grid down. Owen calls it: 'I can't do this without taking the building dark, and I'm not doing that with Dolan and Reyes still on the fourth floor.' She pulls her team back. The cameras are still tracking. The monitors are still at 11:42. Brooks looks at Torah. 'The log,' he says. Just that.
The rooftop unit stays alive and keeps the floor under observation. The team leaves with the mechanism still able to watch.

resolution_shaft_seven_close_full_disclo.speaker: SITREP
resolution_shaft_seven_close_full_disclo.line_1: The signatory admits to the timestamp but not to intent.
resolution_shaft_seven_close_full_disclo.line_2: The timestamp is admitted, but motive remains denied. The shaft can quiet only if the record separates confession from accountability.

lines: The signatory admits to the timestamp but not to intent. Brooks accepts it. Saye logs it. The shaft pressure drops but does not stop entirely. One pulse, low, every few minutes, like a reminder. The replicating document slows but does not clear from all terminals. Brooks files what he has. It is enough to close the site. It is not enough to close the case.
The timestamp is admitted, but motive remains denied. The shaft can quiet only if the record separates confession from accountability.

resolution_shaft_seven_close_full_disclo.speaker: SITREP
resolution_shaft_seven_close_full_disclo.line_1: The signatory says the timestamp was a clerical error.
resolution_shaft_seven_close_full_disclo.line_2: The clerical-error story keeps the false report alive. The debrief has to treat the signatory as unresolved.

lines: The signatory says the timestamp was a clerical error. Brooks does not argue. He logs the denial, packs the file, and walks out. Behind him, the shaft emits one long, low pulse that cracks the break room window from the inside. Saye says, quietly on comms: 'The document is still propagating.' Brooks does not respond. He already knows.
The clerical-error story keeps the false report alive. The debrief has to treat the signatory as unresolved.

resolution_shaft_seven_close_torah_speak.speaker: SITREP
resolution_shaft_seven_close_torah_speak.line_1: Torah speaks and the shaft responds, but not cleanly.
resolution_shaft_seven_close_torah_speak.line_2: The shaft reacts to Torah's words but does not release cleanly. The cooldown has to mark a response, not a closure.

lines: Torah speaks and the shaft responds, but not cleanly. The pressure drops by half. The gantry stabilizes. Reiss can be extracted now, or holds his position more easily. But the false report is still on every terminal in the building, and the signatory in the break room has not moved. Torah has bought time. The shaft is not closed. It is waiting.
The shaft reacts to Torah's words but does not release cleanly. The cooldown has to mark a response, not a closure.

resolution_shaft_seven_close_torah_speak.speaker: SITREP
resolution_shaft_seven_close_torah_speak.line_1: Torah speaks and the shaft answers.
resolution_shaft_seven_close_torah_speak.line_2: The answer from the shaft is active enough to change the site. The team cannot write this as a successful release.

lines: Torah speaks and the shaft answers. The pressure pulse that comes back is not random. It is directed. It hits the gantry frame directly above Torah's position and the vibration travels through the concrete into his feet and up. Owen grabs his arm. 'Step back.' Torah steps back. The shaft is still open. Below, Reiss's voice rises again, steady and patient, and the concrete cracks another centimeter outward.
The answer from the shaft is active enough to change the site. The team cannot write this as a successful release.

resolution_forty_day_window_torah_speaks.speaker: SITREP
resolution_forty_day_window_torah_speaks.line_1: Torah reads the file and names the original wrong clearly.
resolution_forty_day_window_torah_speaks.line_2: The original wrong is named, but the retaliatory scope is still spreading. The cooldown has to preserve the difference between cause and closure.

lines: Torah reads the file and names the original wrong clearly. Then Torah reaches the retaliatory scope and the words slow. The excess is there in the document but saying it feels like indicting someone who had a reason. Torah says it, but the phrasing hedges: understandable given the circumstances. The echo does not stop. It makes one more pass, fast, and Owen takes contact on her arm before Torah says it again, without the hedge. The sound stops. Owen is pressing her sleeve against the cut. She does not look at Torah. The file is still open. The countersignature is visible at the bottom.
The original wrong is named, but the retaliatory scope is still spreading. The cooldown has to preserve the difference between cause and closure.

resolution_forty_day_window_torah_speaks.speaker: SITREP
resolution_forty_day_window_torah_speaks.line_1: Torah reads the grievance and stops at the retaliatory scope.
resolution_forty_day_window_torah_speaks.line_2: Stopping at the retaliation leaves the grievance half spoken. The echo holds that omission open.

lines: Torah reads the grievance and stops at the retaliatory scope. The words are there. Torah does not say them. The echo completes a full pass through the squad's position. Ross takes contact. Owen takes contact. Torah closes the terminal. The sound does not stop. Saye, over comms, her voice stripped of everything except information: 'It's not closing. Whatever you need to say, you need to say it now.' Torah looks at the screen. The terminal is closed. The file is gone from view. The echo is still moving.
Stopping at the retaliation leaves the grievance half spoken. The echo holds that omission open.

resolution_forty_day_window_destroy_part.speaker: SITREP
resolution_forty_day_window_destroy_part.line_1: Ross gets the cabinet open but the tools are heavier than expected and the destruction takes longer.
resolution_forty_day_window_destroy_part.line_2: The tools are damaged, but the delay gives the echo more passes. The cooldown has to count the time bought and the time lost.

lines: Ross gets the cabinet open but the tools are heavier than expected and the destruction takes longer. He gets two of the three broken before the echo reorients and finds him at the cabinet. The contact is brief, a cut across his knuckles, and he finishes the third tool with his hand bleeding. The echo contracts but not as far as it should. One tool is still intact inside the cabinet, wedged in the back. Ross cannot reach it without his hand inside the echo's contracted range. He steps back. 'Torah. It's suppressed but not contained. The file is your window.'
The tools are damaged, but the delay gives the echo more passes. The cooldown has to count the time bought and the time lost.

resolution_forty_day_window_destroy_fail.speaker: SITREP
resolution_forty_day_window_destroy_fail.line_1: Ross forces the cabinet and the tools are there but the echo does not respond to the destruction the way the pattern suggested it would.
resolution_forty_day_window_destroy_fail.line_2: The cabinet breaks, yet the pattern does not. The debrief has to admit the tool theory failed.

lines: Ross forces the cabinet and the tools are there but the echo does not respond to the destruction the way the pattern suggested it would. It does not contract. It intensifies. The sound gets louder and the interval drops to three seconds. Ross steps back from the cabinet. The tools are broken on the floor and it does not matter. Saye, over comms: 'Ross, the physical anchor isn't the primary anchor. It's the file. The tools were secondary. Get Torah to the terminal.' Ross relays this without commentary. His voice is flat.
The cabinet breaks, yet the pattern does not. The debrief has to admit the tool theory failed.

resolution_enough_until_morning_full_dis.speaker: SITREP
resolution_enough_until_morning_full_dis.line_1: The redistribution runs until the queue thins, but not everyone on the sign-in sheet is still outside—maybe forty names go unclaimed.
resolution_enough_until_morning_full_dis.line_2: Most of the queue is answered, but the missing names remain real. The cooldown has to attach the unclaimed sheet instead of rounding it away.

lines: The redistribution runs until the queue thins, but not everyone on the sign-in sheet is still outside—maybe forty names go unclaimed. Owen logs them as undelivered and notes the time. The seated group reduces from fourteen to nine. The remaining nine are still chewing when the bay is empty. Iyad says, 'It is not enough. The log still has a gap.' Owen looks at the unclaimed names. 'We can deliver. If we know where they are.' Torah says, 'Find out.' It is not a full close. It is a direction.
Most of the queue is answered, but the missing names remain real. The cooldown has to attach the unclaimed sheet instead of rounding it away.

resolution_enough_until_morning_full_dis.speaker: SITREP
resolution_enough_until_morning_full_dis.line_1: The queue has dispersed—Brooks did not hold them, and without information they left.
resolution_enough_until_morning_full_dis.line_2: The queue is gone because no one told them enough. The debrief has to record abandonment, not redistribution.

lines: The queue has dispersed—Brooks did not hold them, and without information they left. The bay is full of surplus with nowhere to go. Torah stands in the loading bay with the corrected log and no recipients. Owen says, 'We can call the secondary distribution sites. It will take hours.' The seated group inside is now sixteen. Iyad's voice on the radio is careful and even: 'I need a decision. The longer this sits, the more people sit with it.'
The queue is gone because no one told them enough. The debrief has to record abandonment, not redistribution.

resolution_enough_until_morning_destroy_.speaker: SITREP
resolution_enough_until_morning_destroy_.line_1: The destruction is witnessed but Torah is called away before the final pages are signed—Brooks needs a command decision on the perimeter.
resolution_enough_until_morning_destroy_.line_2: The surplus is witnessed, but the paperwork closes with a gap. The cooldown has to carry the unsigned pages.

lines: The destruction is witnessed but Torah is called away before the final pages are signed—Brooks needs a command decision on the perimeter. Owen signs alone. The seated group recovers to eleven. Three remain at the tables, hands in laps, not eating, not responding. Iyad sits with them. She does not try to move them. She says, 'I think they need the delivery to happen. The record is not enough for them.' Owen looks at the three remaining patients and then at the unsigned pages. 'I'll find Torah.'
The surplus is witnessed, but the paperwork closes with a gap. The cooldown has to carry the unsigned pages.

resolution_enough_until_morning_destroy_.speaker: SITREP
resolution_enough_until_morning_destroy_.line_1: The destruction log is incomplete—Voss took the master inventory and the remaining pages do not account for the full surplus.
resolution_enough_until_morning_destroy_.line_2: The master inventory leaves with Voss, and the destruction record cannot prove the surplus. The after-action file remains open.

lines: The destruction log is incomplete—Voss took the master inventory and the remaining pages do not account for the full surplus. Owen cannot certify what was destroyed because she cannot certify what was there. She submits the partial record to Institute command with a cover note. The seated group recovers to eight. Six remain. Iyad does not leave the canteen. When Torah checks on her an hour later, she is sitting at one of the tables—not eating, not chewing, just sitting—and she looks up and says, 'I am not one of them. I just did not want them to be alone.' Torah believes her. Mostly.
The master inventory leaves with Voss, and the destruction record cannot prove the surplus. The after-action file remains open.

resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_full_d.speaker: SITREP
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_full_d.line_1: Ross reads what he can before the relay starts dropping words.
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_full_d.line_2: Ross gets part of the account into the room before the relay fails. The debrief has to mark exactly where the reading stops.

lines: Ross reads what he can. Three entries are illegible—water damage, Holt's handwriting bleeding into the margin. Saye logs what she receives. The door slows but does not stop. It advances one final increment and holds there—gap still passable, barely. Vasek squeezes through. Ross follows. The door does not seal. It does not open further. Park looks at the gap. 'It's waiting,' she says. 'For the rest of the record.' The rest of the record does not exist.
Ross gets part of the account into the room before the relay fails. The debrief has to mark exactly where the reading stops.

resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_full_d.speaker: SITREP
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_full_d.line_1: The relay drops before Ross finishes the third entry.
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_full_d.line_2: The third entry dies in the relay, leaving the report half rendered. The door has not heard enough to release cleanly.

lines: The relay drops before Ross finishes the third entry. Saye's voice cuts to silence. Ross keeps reading into dead air—he reads the whole report to no one, standing at Holt's duty desk, because there is nothing else to do. The door advances to increment five. The gap closes. The grinding stops. Owen is outside. She puts her hand flat against the sealed door and holds it there for a moment. Then she steps back. 'Mark it,' she says. 'Sealed. Personnel inside. Unresolved.'
The third entry dies in the relay, leaving the report half rendered. The door has not heard enough to release cleanly.

resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_seal_p.speaker: SITREP
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_seal_p.line_1: The breaching tool catches the track and holds for six minutes before the door mechanism finds a way around it—not mechanical, it simply resumes from a different point in the cycle.
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_seal_p.line_2: The tool buys minutes, not control. The file can be evacuated, but the door's alternate cycle belongs in the report.

lines: The breaching tool catches the track and holds for six minutes before the door mechanism finds a way around it—not mechanical, it simply resumes from a different point in the cycle. Brooks pulls the tool before it is destroyed. The gap is narrower now. Ross gets through. Vasek gets through. Brooks gets through last, and the door clips his shoulder on the way—not a crush, a graze, but he feels it. He does not mention it until the debrief. The report is out. The door is sealed. The station is closed but not permanently. 'We have the report,' Owen says. 'That's what we came for.'
The tool buys minutes, not control. The file can be evacuated, but the door's alternate cycle belongs in the report.

resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_seal_f.speaker: SITREP
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_seal_f.line_1: The breaching tool goes into the track and the door takes it.
resolution_kaph_file_and_evacuate_seal_f.line_2: The door takes the tool and keeps cycling. The debrief starts with a failed seal and people still inside the mechanism.

lines: The breaching tool goes into the track and the door takes it. The mechanism does not stop—it incorporates the tool, grinds through it, and advances. Brooks watches the tool disappear into the door frame. The gap closes to thirty centimeters. 'Pull back,' Owen says. Brooks does not move immediately. 'Brooks.' He pulls back. The door seals. The grinding stops. The ant-pattern interference on Park's terminal resolves into a single repeating output: the same sensor entry from week three, the one Holt buried first. Park screenshots it. It is the only piece of the record they have.
The door takes the tool and keeps cycling. The debrief starts with a failed seal and people still inside the mechanism.

resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_1: Saye takes custody of the evidence.
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_2: Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

lines: Saye takes custody of the evidence. The disclosure to Reiss is partial—she tells him enough to explain the physical symptoms, not enough to explain the full anomaly mechanism. She is not lying; she is managing what he can absorb right now. The manufacturer's identity goes into the Institute file. The department head's denial of the wig remains request is noted but not escalated. The second heat signature is in Saye's personal notes, not the official report. She will decide later whether to include it. That decision will follow her.
Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_1: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them.
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_2: The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

lines: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them. The wig remains are in the evidence container but the chain of custody has a gap. Saye files what she has. The manufacturer's identity is in the report but the physical evidence is incomplete. Reiss is in Institute medical and has not been told anything. Saye sits in the vehicle for a long time after filing. Ross finds her there an hour later. He does not ask what she is thinking. He already knows.
The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_2: The release weakens the hold but does not settle the container. Park can move it only if the record names the residual risk.

lines: Torah speaks the release. The anomaly responds—the warmth in the container drops, the column fracture stops—but the close is incomplete. A section of the wig's base is still adhered to Reiss's scalp, in Institute medical custody now, and the obligation has not fully discharged. Reiss's tremor continues at a lower level. The instrument readings drop but do not reach baseline. The anomaly is suppressed. It is not closed. Saye flags the residual in her report. The evidence container goes into restricted storage. Torah knows, without being told, that this will come back.
The release weakens the hold but does not settle the container. Park can move it only if the record names the residual risk.

resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_1: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot.
resolution_borrowed_separation_contact_c.line_2: The release becomes another escalation. The site moves to evacuation posture before any after-action language can call the container safe.

lines: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot. Not warm—hot, the kind of heat that makes Park step back and Owen call everyone to the breach point. The discharge pulse moves through the floor. The temporary column props hold but one of them buckles. The building alarm triggers. Everyone exits. The container is left inside—too hot to carry. Ross seals the east entrance. The instrument readings spike and then drop to a level that Saye, on comms, describes as 'not baseline.' She does not elaborate. The building is condemned. The container is inside it. Torah stands at the cordon line and does not speak for the rest of the operation. Owen files the after-action report. She leaves Torah's role in the resolution section blank and sends it to Saye to complete.
The release becomes another escalation. The site moves to evacuation posture before any after-action language can call the container safe.

resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_1: Saye takes custody of the evidence.
resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_2: Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

lines: Saye takes custody of the evidence. The disclosure to Reiss is partial—she tells him enough to explain the physical symptoms, not enough to explain the full anomaly mechanism. She is not lying; she is managing what he can absorb right now. The manufacturer's identity goes into the Institute file. The department head's denial of the wig remains request is noted but not escalated. The second heat signature is in Saye's personal notes, not the official report. She will decide later whether to include it. That decision will follow her.
Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_1: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them.
resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_2: The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

lines: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them. The wig remains are in the evidence container but the chain of custody has a gap. Saye files what she has. The manufacturer's identity is in the report but the physical evidence is incomplete. Reiss is in Institute medical and has not been told anything. Saye sits in the vehicle for a long time after filing. Ross finds her there an hour later. He does not ask what she is thinking. He already knows.
The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_2: The release weakens the hold but does not settle the container. Park can move it only if the record names the residual risk.

lines: Torah speaks the release. The anomaly responds—the warmth in the container drops, the column fracture stops—but the close is incomplete. A section of the wig's base is still adhered to Reiss's scalp, in Institute medical custody now, and the obligation has not fully discharged. Reiss's tremor continues at a lower level. The instrument readings drop but do not reach baseline. The anomaly is suppressed. It is not closed. Saye flags the residual in her report. The evidence container goes into restricted storage. Torah knows, without being told, that this will come back.
The release weakens the hold but does not settle the container. Park can move it only if the record names the residual risk.

resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_1: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot.
resolution_borrowed_separation_burn_clea.line_2: The release becomes another escalation. The site moves to evacuation posture before any after-action language can call the container safe.

lines: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot. Not warm—hot, the kind of heat that makes Park step back and Owen call everyone to the breach point. The discharge pulse moves through the floor. The temporary column props hold but one of them buckles. The building alarm triggers. Everyone exits. The container is left inside—too hot to carry. Ross seals the east entrance. The instrument readings spike and then drop to a level that Saye, on comms, describes as 'not baseline.' She does not elaborate. The building is condemned. The container is inside it. Torah stands at the cordon line and does not speak for the rest of the operation. Owen files the after-action report. She leaves Torah's role in the resolution section blank and sends it to Saye to complete.
The release becomes another escalation. The site moves to evacuation posture before any after-action language can call the container safe.

resolution_borrowed_separation_residual_.line_2: Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

lines: Saye takes custody of the evidence. The disclosure to Reiss is partial—she tells him enough to explain the physical symptoms, not enough to explain the full anomaly mechanism. She is not lying; she is managing what he can absorb right now. The manufacturer's identity goes into the Institute file. The department head's denial of the wig remains request is noted but not escalated. The second heat signature is in Saye's personal notes, not the official report. She will decide later whether to include it. That decision will follow her.
Saye has custody, but the chain is already carrying heat from the breach. The cooldown can proceed only as a guarded evidence transfer.

resolution_borrowed_separation_residual_.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_residual_.line_1: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them.
resolution_borrowed_separation_residual_.line_2: The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

lines: Saye arrives on site and the evidence is already compromised—the protocol guide is missing from the bench, removed by a civilian first responder who thought it was personal property before Ross could stop them. The wig remains are in the evidence container but the chain of custody has a gap. Saye files what she has. The manufacturer's identity is in the report but the physical evidence is incomplete. Reiss is in Institute medical and has not been told anything. Saye sits in the vehicle for a long time after filing. Ross finds her there an hour later. He does not ask what she is thinking. He already knows.
The evidence story is contaminated before Saye can secure it. The cooldown has to record a compromised bench, not a clean handoff.

resolution_borrowed_separation_active_fu.line_2: Saye holds the profile long enough to buy egress, but the bench is gone as a controlled scene. The active cooldown has to start with loss, not closure.

lines: Saye holds the profile long enough to get the squad out, but not long enough to save the evidence bench. The protocol guide is still inside. The wig remains are still on Reiss. The outer cordon is clean and nobody else is hurt. That is what Saye can give them. She files the site as active containment, not resolution. The second-entry plan starts before the dust has settled.
Saye holds the profile long enough to buy egress, but the bench is gone as a controlled scene. The active cooldown has to start with loss, not closure.

resolution_borrowed_separation_active_in.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_active_in.line_1: Owen gets everyone out, but the cordon is ugly.
resolution_borrowed_separation_active_in.line_2: Owen gets bodies out, but the cordon is ragged and Bay 3 is still live. The next report has to preserve that instability.

lines: Owen gets everyone out, but the cordon is ugly. Park comes through last and hard enough to tear her glove. Brooks is on one knee at the breach point before Ross gets an arm under her. The evidence bench is lost inside the dust. The building holds. The case does not close. Owen does not call it luck. She calls it survival and keeps moving.
Owen gets bodies out, but the cordon is ragged and Bay 3 is still live. The next report has to preserve that instability.

resolution_borrowed_separation_active_in.speaker: SITREP
resolution_borrowed_separation_active_in.line_1: Torah speaks the release and the container gets hot.
resolution_borrowed_separation_active_in.line_2: The evacuation attempt fails into heat and alarm. The active cooldown begins with the team outside and the container still inside the danger.
